sections.title: MCP Tools: Browser, Computer, App, UI
MCP Tools: Terminals, Projects, Tasks, Files, Integrations
Preferences: General, Appearance, Tray, Language, Profile
Preferences: API Keys, Custom CLIs, Plugins, Experimental

items.title: Right-click .btn-claude-terminal opens 'Select AI' menu (Claude Code / Codex / Antigravity + custom CLIs); selection launches that tool and persists
preconditions: At least the claude CLI installed; custom CLIs optional
steps: Right-click .btn-claude-terminal
Verify the menu header 'Select AI' and items Claude Code, Codex, Antigravity (with provider icons), plus a divider + any user-defined custom CLIs
Click a provider
Verify a new terminal launches that provider's CLI, the button appearance updates, and a later plain click launches the same provider
items.expected: Menu renders all providers with ✓ on the last-selected one; selecting stores localStorage 'patapim-last-ai', creates an AI terminal for that provider, and the AI button click defaults to it

automationHints.notes: Codex/Antigravity launch will error if the CLI isn't installed — verifying the spawned command line in the terminal is sufficient. Reset patapim-last-ai to 'claude' afterwards

items.title: AI copy-offer markers surface the floating Copy button
steps: Produce output wrapped in the markers, e.g. run: echo '<<<PATAPIM_COPY>>>'; echo 'offer-text-123'; echo '<<<PATAPIM_COPY_END>>>'
Verify the floating copy button (.btn-copy-offer-float) appears with tooltip 'Copy'
Click it; verify 'offer-text-123' lands on the clipboard and the button shows the 'copied' state
Switch tabs and back; verify the offer is per-terminal
items.expected: The marker scan extracts the wrapped text as the terminal's copy offer; button copies it, flashes .copied, and the offer tracks the active terminal (patapim:copy-offer-changed event)

automationHints.notes: Markers must each sit on their own line (COPY_OFFER_RE requires line boundaries); echo each marker separately

items.title: Close idle tab via .tab-close X without confirm
preconditions: ≥2 terminal tabs open; target tab idle (no AI processing/awaiting)
steps: Click the idle shell tab's .tab-close X
items.expected: Tab closes immediately with NO confirm dialog (guard only fires for isProcessing/awaitingResponse); tab count decreases by 1

items.title: Create Claude Code terminal via .btn-claude-terminal, then close it through the waiting-confirm dialog
steps: Note tab count, click the AI terminal button (.btn-claude-terminal)
Wait for the new 'claude' tab to launch a Claude Code session (trust prompt is fine); do not answer it
Click .terminal-tab.active .tab-close
In the confirm dialog 'This terminal is waiting for your answer. Close it anyway?' click the red Close button
items.expected: Tab count +1 with a Claude session starting; on close, the awaiting-response guard shows the closeWaitingConfirm dialog; confirming removes the tab (count back to original)

automationHints.notes: Confirm dialog comes from addCustomModelModal.confirm with okText 'Close' / red; dialog button may need computer_* if not selector-addressable

items.title: Create new shell terminal via .btn-new-terminal and run echo
steps: Click the new-terminal toolbar button (.btn-new-terminal)
Click into the terminal area (.terminal-content .xterm-helper-textarea)
Type 'echo hello_patapim_test' and press Enter
items.expected: A new tab appears (default shell, fallback name 'Terminal N') and becomes active showing a shell prompt; the command echoes and prints 'hello_patapim_test'; no error overlay

automationHints.notes: Tab count before/after via ui_state on .terminal-tab; default shell comes from localStorage 'patapim-default-shell' or system default

items.title: Closing a terminal that is mid-work shows the 'still working' confirm
steps: Send Claude a prompt that takes ≥20s (e.g. 'count to 50 slowly')
While the tab is red/processing, click its .tab-close X
Verify the dialog text, click Cancel first, then repeat and confirm Close
items.expected: Dialog 'This terminal is still working. Close it anyway?' (closeWorkingConfirm — distinct from the waiting variant) with red Close button; Cancel keeps the terminal running; Close removes it

automationHints.notes: isProcessing → closeWorkingConfirm; awaitingResponse → closeWaitingConfirm (covered by terminals-002)

items.title: Shell process exit auto-closes the tab without confirm
preconditions: ≥2 terminal tabs open, a shell tab active
steps: Type 'exit' in the shell terminal and press Enter
items.expected: The pty exits, main sends TERMINAL_DESTROYED, and the renderer closes the tab with skipConfirm (no 'still working?' dialog for a dead process); remaining tab becomes active

items.title: Drag .tab-resize-handle to resize a tab, then 'Reset tab width' from the context menu
preconditions: ≥1 terminal tab open, tab bar visible
steps: Mouse-down on the tab's .tab-resize-handle and drag horizontally ~60px
Verify the tab width changes and persists (localStorage) across a layout change
Right-click the tab and click 'Reset tab width'
items.expected: Drag resizes the individual tab; the stored width survives layout changes; 'Reset tab width' (terminal.context.resetTabWidth) restores the default width

items.title: Drag-and-drop a file onto the terminal pastes its path
preconditions: Shell terminal open; a test file visible in Explorer or the app's file tree
steps: Drag a file from Windows Explorer and drop it onto the terminal content area
Verify the file's full path is pasted at the prompt (quoted if it contains spaces)
items.expected: Drop handler pastes the dropped file's path into the terminal input without executing it

automationHints.notes: OS-level drag from Explorer is flaky for automation; a human pass is more reliable

items.title: Empty states: no project selected vs project with no terminals
steps: Deselect/close all projects (or fresh profile); verify the no-project empty state: 'Select or create a project to get started' with 'New Project' and 'Open Folder' actions, an 'or' divider, and 'Start a Quick Task'
Select a project and close all but one terminal, then close the last one via its context menu Close (if allowed); or open a project that has no terminals
Verify the 'No terminals for this project' empty state
items.expected: Both empty states render with the exact locale strings and working action buttons (New Project / Open Folder / Start a Quick Task each trigger their flows)

automationHints.notes: body gets .no-project-empty-state class in the no-project case

items.title: Find in terminal: Ctrl+F panel, live search, Enter/Shift+Enter navigation, Escape, history
preconditions: Shell terminal with output containing a repeated word (run 'echo findme; echo findme')
steps: Press Ctrl+F; verify #find-panel opens with #find-input focused
Type 'findme'; verify a match highlights after the 150ms debounce
Press Enter (next match) and Shift+Enter (previous match)
Press Escape; verify the panel hides and highlights clear
Re-open Ctrl+F and check #find-history-dropdown offers 'findme'
items.expected: Panel toggles via Ctrl+F; typing searches case-insensitively via SearchAddon.findNext; Enter/Shift+Enter cycle matches; Escape hides; search term saved to history (localStorage patapim-search-history, max 20)

items.title: Ctrl+Shift+W toggles the WhatsApp panel and does NOT close the terminal
preconditions: ≥2 terminal tabs open, a shell tab active and focused
steps: Open >=2 terminals; note the active one
Press Ctrl+Shift+W
Press Ctrl+Shift+W again
items.expected: WhatsApp panel toggles open then closed; the active terminal stays open (the old close-terminal binding was removed — closing is Ctrl+W / Ctrl+F4). Fixed 2026-06-12.

automationHints.notes: Double-binding: src/renderer/index.js ~line 2076 (whatsappPanel.toggle) vs multiTerminalUI.js ~line 2209 (closeTerminal). Same keystroke, two listeners

items.title: Close active terminal with Ctrl+W / Ctrl+F4, and verify the last remaining terminal cannot be closed via shortcut
steps: Press Ctrl+W (or Ctrl+F4) with a shell tab active; verify it closes
With only 1 terminal left, press Ctrl+W again
items.expected: First press closes the active tab (count 2→1). Second press does nothing — both close shortcuts are guarded by terminals.size > 1

automationHints.notes: Ctrl+W must not reach the shell either (in bash Ctrl+W deletes a word — acceptable only if the tab-close handler consumed it). On macOS the combo is Cmd+W

items.title: Create a new terminal for the current project with Ctrl+Shift+T
steps: Press Ctrl+Shift+T
items.expected: A new terminal tab is created under the current project (createTerminalForCurrentProject) and becomes active

automationHints.notes: Same caveat as Ctrl+Tab: keystroke may be swallowed when xterm has focus — test with and without terminal focus

items.title: Switch terminals by number with Ctrl+1 / Ctrl+2
steps: Press Ctrl+1; verify first terminal active
Press Ctrl+2; verify second terminal active
items.expected: Ctrl+N activates the Nth terminal (1-9, order from getTerminalStates); verified via ui_state

items.title: Toggle grid view with Ctrl+Shift+G
preconditions: ≥2 terminal tabs open, tab view active
steps: Press Ctrl+Shift+G
Press Ctrl+Shift+G again
items.expected: View flips tabs→grid then grid→tabs, matching the .btn-view-toggle behavior (terminals-012)

items.title: Telegram-route footer toggle prefixes typed messages with [tg]
preconditions: Active Claude terminal (footer toggles live on multiTerminalUI)
steps: Locate the Telegram route toggle next to the plan-mode footer (.tg-route-toggle, tooltip 'Reply via Telegram — every typed message gets [tg] prefix…')
Toggle it ON; verify the tab shows the Telegram icon (.tab-tg-icon)
Type a short message into the Claude terminal and verify it is sent with a '[tg] ' prefix
Toggle OFF and verify the prefix and tab icon are gone
items.expected: Toggle ON marks the terminal telegramRouted (tab icon appears) and prefixes typed input with [tg]; OFF restores normal input. Actual Telegram delivery is out of scope here

automationHints.notes: Footer can be hidden via its right-click context menu like the plan-mode footer; auto-flips ON when an inbound Telegram message lands in the terminal (needs telegram to test that path)

items.title: WhatsApp bot terminal: 'Export chat history' dialog strings and cancel
preconditions: WhatsApp integration connected; the WhatsApp Bot terminal/panel available
steps: Open the WhatsApp Bot context (tab labeled per terminal.whatsapp.bot = 'WhatsApp Bot')
Click 'Export chat history'
Verify the dialog: title 'Export WhatsApp Chat', prompt 'Enter the contact or group name to export:', placeholder 'e.g. EGG: PRODUCCION', 'Export' button
Cancel/close the dialog without exporting
items.expected: Dialog renders all four locale strings; cancelling leaves no export running and no file written

automationHints.notes: Only the dialog mechanics here; actual export belongs to the integrations section

items.title: Multi-line paste into an AI terminal stays one message (bracketed-paste safety net)
preconditions: Claude terminal at its input prompt; clipboard contains 3 lines of text
steps: Copy a 3-line text block to the clipboard
Press Ctrl+V in the Claude terminal's input
Verify all 3 lines land in the input box as one multi-line message (not 3 submitted messages)
items.expected: For terminals with state.aiTool set, pasteIntoTerminal force-wraps multi-line text in bracketed-paste sequences even if DECSET 2004 isn't reported, so newlines don't auto-submit; plain shells fall back to xterm.paste()

automationHints.notes: Regression guard for the classic 'paste ran my prompt 3 times' bug; also try while a trust-folder dialog is up (bracketed mode off)

items.title: Terminals and Claude sessions restore across app restart
preconditions: Dev instance with 1 Claude terminal (with an active session) + 1 shell terminal
steps: Note terminal names/tabs, then restart the dev instance (./rebuild-dev-safe.bat from Claude Code; never rebuild-dev.bat)
Wait for app ready (app_wait_ready)
Verify the 'Restoring previous session' overlay appears during restore, then the same tabs return
Verify the Claude tab re-runs 'claude --resume <sessionId>' (staggered by the AIResumeQueue, not all at once) and the transcript replays
items.expected: Saved sessions are restored on boot (_restoreSessions); restored tabs show the gold 'restored' name color; AI tabs resume their session via claude --resume with queue staggering; shell tabs respawn at their cwd

automationHints.notes: terminal.restoring locale = 'Restoring previous session'. Antigravity has no --resume flag (in-TUI resume); codex/claude differ — see buildResumeCommand

items.title: Orphan AI processes from a force-killed session are killed on next launch (kill-ai-processes)
preconditions: At least one Claude terminal running
Willingness to force-kill PATAPIM (stable instance not needed for other work)
steps: Open a Claude Code terminal and let the AI process spawn (note the node/claude PIDs via Task Manager or tasklist)
Force-kill the PATAPIM process tree WITHOUT a clean quit (taskkill /f on the electron PID) so the AI child processes are orphaned
Relaunch PATAPIM and let session restore run (pending AI resume tabs appear amber)
Verify the orphaned claude/node processes from the previous session are no longer running
Check the renderer console for the '[TerminalManager] Killed N orphan AI processes' log line
items.expected: On restore with pending AI resumes, KILL_AI_PROCESSES sweeps orphan AI processes from the prior session so resumed tabs don't double-spawn or fight over session files

items.title: Plan-mode footer toggle and Ctrl+Shift+P shortcut on a Claude terminal
preconditions: Active Claude Code terminal past its trust prompt (footer only shows when activeTerminalSupportsPlanMode)
steps: Verify #plan-mode-footer is visible with the 'Plan' label and tooltip 'Toggle Plan Mode for the current AI terminal'
Click the #plan-mode-checkbox toggle; verify the footer gains the active style and Claude switches to plan mode (cyan tab name)
Press Ctrl+Shift+P; verify the checkbox flips back off
Switch to a plain shell tab; verify the footer hides
items.expected: Checkbox toggles plan mode for the active terminal (toggleActivePlanMode), footer reflects state, Ctrl+Shift+P is equivalent, and the footer is hidden for terminals whose aiTool doesn't support plan mode

automationHints.notes: Footer can also be hidden via its own right-click context menu (_showFooterContextMenu) — don't confuse hidden-by-user with unsupported

items.title: Open Sessions panel (button + Ctrl+Shift+S), verify grouping, filter, and empty-filter message
steps: Click .btn-sessions-toggle in the toolbar; verify #sessions-panel opens titled 'Sessions'
Verify sessions grouped under Today / Yesterday / This Week / Older with relative times (e.g. '5m ago') and provider labels (Claude/Codex/Antigravity)
Verify the 'Continue Last {provider} Session' quick-resume button at the top
Type gibberish in the 'Filter sessions...' input; verify 'No sessions match your filter'
Clear filter; press Ctrl+Shift+S to close the panel
items.expected: Panel toggles via button and shortcut; cards grouped by date with time-ago strings; filter narrows live; noMatch empty state shows; a project with no sessions shows 'No sessions found for this project'

automationHints.notes: Loading state shows 'Loading sessions...' (.cs-loading); failedLoad is hard to trigger without breaking the IPC

items.title: Resume a session from a card; busy active terminal spawns a new tab; copy file path button
preconditions: Sessions panel open with ≥1 resumable Claude session card
Active terminal already has an AI tool running
steps: Click a .cs-copy-path button on a card; verify it flips to ✓ and the JSONL path lands on the clipboard
Click the session card itself
Verify that because the active terminal already has aiTool set, a NEW terminal is created and activated, and 'claude --resume <id>' is sent there
Check expired cards show the 'Session expired' badge and tooltip
items.expected: Card click resumes into the active terminal only when it is AI-free; otherwise a fresh terminal is created (no 'aiAlreadyActive' toast in current code); copy-path gives visual ✓ feedback for 1.5s; panel hides after resume

automationHints.notes: Locale keys sessions.noActiveTerminal / sessions.aiAlreadyActive appear unreferenced in the current resume flow (it auto-creates a terminal instead) — flag as dead strings if confirmed

items.title: Right-click .btn-new-terminal opens 'Select Shell' menu listing detected shells; choosing one creates a terminal and persists the default
preconditions: ≥1 shell detected (PowerShell always; optionally PowerShell Core, cmd, Git Bash, WSL)
steps: Right-click .btn-new-terminal
Verify the .shell-menu header 'Select Shell' and one .terminal-context-menu-item per detected shell, with ✓ on the current default
Click a non-default shell (e.g. Git Bash)
Verify a new terminal opens running that shell, and that a subsequent plain click on .btn-new-terminal now uses it
items.expected: Menu lists all detected shells; selection creates the terminal, sets localStorage 'patapim-default-shell', updates the button tooltip, and the ✓ moves on next open

automationHints.notes: Cross-check the menu against MCP shell_list. Menu shows 'Loading...' (common.loading) if shells not yet loaded, then re-renders. Restore the original default shell afterwards

items.title: SSH project terminal spawns 'ssh -t' to the remote host instead of a local shell
preconditions: An ssh:// project configured with a reachable host and working key/agent auth (~/.ssh/config)
steps: Select the SSH project and create a new terminal
Verify the terminal connects over SSH (remote prompt, remote hostname) rather than opening a local shell
Run 'hostname' to confirm it executes remotely
Kill the network or exit; verify the tab handles the pty exit (tab closes)
items.expected: ptyManager detects the ssh:// projectPath/cwd (isSshUrl/parseSshUrl) and spawns the bundled Windows OpenSSH (C:\Windows\System32\OpenSSH\ssh.exe, falling back to PATH) with -t; auth delegated to system ssh config/agent

items.title: Tab name state colors: red while processing, green when finished/needs attention, white idle
steps: Send Claude a prompt taking ~20s
While working, screenshot the tab — name should be red (processing)
Switch to another tab and wait for Claude to finish — the background tab's name should turn green (needs attention)
Focus the Claude tab and type a key — name returns to white (idle)
items.expected: Color precedence per getTabNameColor: restored gold > summary purple > finished/needs-attention green > plan cyan > processing red > idle white; processing red appears during work, green on finish for background tabs, and clears to white on user focus/typing

automationHints.notes: Plan-mode cyan covered in terminals-032; summary purple needs the progress-summary feature (out of scope here)

items.title: Restored gold indicator clears when the user focuses or types in the tab
preconditions: Freshly restarted app with a restored terminal showing the gold tab-name color
steps: Screenshot the restored tab (gold name, #f0b429)
Click the tab to focus it
Verify the gold color clears (wasRestored=false) and no stray needs-attention notification fires from the resume transcript replay
items.expected: state.wasRestored clears on focus (cosmetic); the internal _wasRestored guard keeps the resume replay's finish markers from flashing green/attention

items.title: Exactly one .terminal-tab carries the active class at all times
steps: Read active flags for all tabs via ui_state
Switch active via ui_click('.terminal-tab[data-terminal-id="<other>"]')
Re-read flags
items.expected: Before and after the switch exactly one tab has active:true; the clicked tab gains it and the previous loses it

automationHints.notes: :first-child/:nth-child do NOT reliably match tabs (non-tab sibling precedes; DOM order differs in horizontal mode)

items.title: Rename tab via double-click on tab name
steps: Double-click the active tab's name
In the .tab-rename-input press Ctrl+A, type 'e2e-renamed-tab', press Enter
items.expected: A .tab-rename-input opens with the current name selected; after Enter the tab label reads 'e2e-renamed-tab' and the input is removed

automationHints.notes: Double-click needs computer_* (space:image coords from a FRESH full computer_screenshot, not app_screenshot)

items.title: Switch tabs by click and by Ctrl+Tab / Ctrl+Shift+Tab
steps: Click the inactive tab; verify it becomes active
Focus the terminal, press Ctrl+Tab; check active tab
Press Ctrl+Shift+Tab; check active tab
items.expected: Click switches active reliably. Ctrl+Tab cycles to the next terminal and Ctrl+Shift+Tab to the previous (bound in multiTerminalUI keydown handler). KNOWN FAIL 2026-06-08: Ctrl+Tab/Ctrl+Shift+Tab did nothing — likely consumed by focused xterm before reaching the document handler

automationHints.notes: Verify via ui_state active flag; use [data-terminal-id] selectors, NOT :nth-child (non-tab sibling precedes tabs). Ctrl+Shift+Tab also collides with nothing else; contrast with working Ctrl+1/2 (terminals-016)

items.title: Open Loop a Command overlay and dismiss with Escape
steps: Right-click a shell tab → click 'Loop a Command'
Verify overlay contents, then press Escape without saving
items.expected: Overlay shows 'Command to repeat...' textarea, REPEAT (times) + EVERY (interval) fields, interval presets, 'Enter to start · Escape to cancel'; Escape dismisses cleanly

automationHints.notes: Label from locale: tabbar.loopCommand = 'Loop a Command'

items.title: Open Schedule a Command overlay and dismiss with Escape
steps: Right-click a shell tab → click 'Schedule a Command'
Verify overlay contents, then press Escape without saving
items.expected: Overlay shows 'Command to run...' textarea, date/time field, presets (In 10 min / 1 hour / 4 hours / 8 hours / 12 hours), 'Enter to schedule' hint; Escape dismisses cleanly back to the terminal

automationHints.notes: Label from locale: tabbar.scheduleCommand = 'Schedule a Command'

items.title: Pin and unpin a terminal tab
steps: Right-click a tab → click 'Pin terminal' (top context-menu item)
Verify the tab reorders to the top of the list and shows a pin indicator
Unpin via ui_click on '.terminal-tab .tab-pin.is-pinned' (or the active tab's .tab-pin)
items.expected: Pinned tab moves to the top and gains .tab-pin.is-pinned; unpinning removes the indicator and returns it to the unpinned group. Pinned terminals are re-resumed on restart

items.title: Rename tab via right-click → Rename
steps: Right-click the tab (coords from a fresh full computer_screenshot)
Click 'Rename' in the context menu (Pin terminal / CUSTOM ICONS / Rename / Schedule / Loop / Close)
Ctrl+A, type 'e2e-ctx-renamed', Enter
items.expected: Context menu opens; Rename opens .tab-rename-input; tab label becomes 'e2e-ctx-renamed'

automationHints.notes: Use region-scoped computer_click_text for menu items — pixel-clicking small items mis-fires; beware the ~5px .icon-swatch row directly above Rename (accidental icon application)

items.title: Custom icon swatch row, apply an icon, and expand 'More icons' picker
steps: Right-click a tab; verify the 'CUSTOM ICONS' label and a row of .icon-swatch buttons (red square, green play, pause, warning, clock) ending with a '…' More-icons button and an X
Click one swatch; verify the icon appears on the tab
Re-open the menu and click the '…' button; verify an expanded icon picker opens
Clear the icon via the X swatch to restore the tab
items.expected: Swatch row + label + '…' button present; clicking a swatch applies the icon to the tab; '…' opens the expanded picker (NOT positively confirmed on 2026-06-08 — swatches are ~5px targets); X clears the custom icon

automationHints.notes: Tiny targets; take a fresh full computer_screenshot before each click. Seed marked the expanded-picker step unconfirmed

items.title: Dismiss tab context menu with Escape
steps: Right-click the active tab to open the context menu
Press Escape
items.expected: Menu fully disappears; terminal content restored; no side effects (no rename input, no icon applied)

items.title: Scheduled/loop command indicators on the tab show detail popovers
steps: Right-click the tab → 'Schedule a Command'; enter 'echo scheduled_ok', pick the 'In 10 min' preset, press Enter to schedule
Verify the tab gains a pending-schedule indicator; hover it and verify the popover shows '{date} at {time}' (terminal.schedule.dateAt)
Cancel/remove the scheduled command via the popover or by re-opening the schedule UI
Repeat with 'Loop a Command' (echo loop_ok, 3 times every 60s); verify the loop indicator popover shows 'x/y iterations · every interval', then stop the loop
Cleanup: ensure no scheduled or looping command remains before leaving the test
items.expected: Both indicators render on the tab while pending/active; popovers show the schedule time and loop progress strings; cancelling clears the indicators and nothing fires afterwards

automationHints.notes: Marked destructive because a forgotten schedule/loop executes commands later; the commands are harmless echoes but MUST be cancelled. Tooltips: tabbar.schedulePending 'Scheduled command pending', tabbar.loopProgress 'Loop: x/y'

items.title: Change grid arrangement via .grid-layout-select (1x2 vs 2x2)
steps: Set .grid-layout-select to '1x2'; observe relayout
Set it to '2x2'; observe relayout
items.expected: 1x2 renders full-height columns; 2x2 renders quadrants; the select value tracks each selection and each change produces a visible relayout

items.title: Tab-bar layout context menu: Vertical / Horizontal / Disabled round-trip
steps: Right-click empty tab-bar space just below the last tab (vertical mode) → 'TERMINAL LAYOUT' menu with Vertical (✓) / Horizontal / Disabled
Click 'Horizontal'; verify tabs move to a top row and the terminal fills full width
Re-open the layout menu from the empty strip right of the tabs (tiny target) and click 'Disabled'; verify the tab bar hides
Restore 'Vertical' via the same menu
items.expected: Each option applies immediately; ✓ marks the current mode; round-trip back to Vertical works. 2026-06-08 run verified Vertical→Horizontal only; Disabled and the round-trip were not exercised (empty-strip target too small in horizontal mode)

automationHints.notes: In horizontal mode right-clicks easily land on the wide active tab (tab menu) or in the terminal (PowerShell right-click paste — clear with Ctrl+C). With layout Disabled, plan how to re-open the menu before disabling

items.title: Toggle tab view ↔ grid view via .btn-view-toggle
steps: Click .btn-view-toggle
Verify grid layout (panes side-by-side) and that .grid-layout-select becomes visible
Click .btn-view-toggle again
items.expected: Grid view shows all terminals as panes and reveals the layout dropdown; second click returns to single-pane tab view and hides .grid-layout-select

items.title: Paste via Ctrl+V, right-click, and middle-click
preconditions: Shell terminal open; clipboard contains 'echo paste_ok'
steps: Press Ctrl+V in the focused terminal; verify the text appears at the prompt
Clear the line (Ctrl+C), right-click inside the terminal area; verify paste
Clear, middle-click inside the terminal; verify paste
items.expected: All three paths paste the clipboard text into the prompt (pasteClipboardInto); Ctrl+Shift+V also works

automationHints.notes: On PowerShell the right-click paste is also a shell feature — the app handler fires regardless; just verify the text landed once, not twice

items.title: Run 'dir' in a shell terminal and verify directory listing
preconditions: Shell terminal open in a project with files
steps: Focus the terminal textarea, type 'dir', press Enter
items.expected: Output shows the project directory listing (Mode/LastWriteTime/Length/Name columns on PowerShell) with the project's files; no error overlay; tab stays active

items.title: Smart Ctrl+C: copies when text is selected, sends SIGINT when not
steps: Select a word of terminal output (Ctrl+Shift+A or mouse drag)
Press Ctrl+C; check the clipboard and that the selection cleared
Run 'ping -t 127.0.0.1' (or 'sleep 30'), press Ctrl+C with no selection
items.expected: With selection: text lands on the clipboard, selection clears, no SIGINT. Without selection: Ctrl+C passes through as SIGINT and interrupts the running command

automationHints.notes: Ctrl+Shift+C is the explicit copy combo; verify clipboard via a paste back or PowerShell Get-Clipboard in another terminal

items.title: Ctrl+Shift+C copies the terminal selection to the clipboard (and is inert without a selection)
preconditions: ≥1 shell terminal open with some output visible
steps: Run 'echo copytest123' in the terminal
Select the word 'copytest123' in the output with the mouse
Press Ctrl+Shift+C
Verify the system clipboard now contains 'copytest123' and the selection was cleared
With no selection, press Ctrl+Shift+C again and verify nothing is sent to the shell and the clipboard is unchanged
items.expected: With a selection: clipboard.writeText(selection) runs and the selection clears (terminalManager.js handler for ctrl+shift+'c'). Without a selection: the keypress is swallowed (returns false) — no SIGINT, no characters appear at the prompt, clipboard unchanged.

automationHints.notes: Complements terminals-024 (smart Ctrl+C). Selecting text needs a real mouse drag (computer_drag) over the rendered output. Read the clipboard back by pasting into a scratch input or via a shell command.

items.title: Scrollback navigation with Shift+PageUp / Shift+PageDown
preconditions: Shell terminal with >2 screens of output (e.g. run 'dir -Recurse C:\Windows\System32 | Select -First 200')
steps: Press Shift+PageUp twice; verify the viewport scrolled up into history
Press Shift+PageDown until back at the prompt
items.expected: Shift+PageUp/PageDown scroll one page per press through the 10000-line scrollback; typing any key snaps back to the bottom

items.title: Zoom terminal font with Ctrl+= / Ctrl+- / Ctrl+0 across all terminals
steps: Focus a terminal, press Ctrl+= twice (zoom in)
Switch to the other terminal; verify its font also grew
Press Ctrl+- once, then Ctrl+0
items.expected: Font size steps by 2 per press from default 14, clamped to min/max, applied to ALL terminal instances (setFontSize iterates every instance); Ctrl+0 resets to 14

automationHints.notes: Verify visually via screenshot diff or by reading xterm cell metrics; zoom triggers a refit (multiTerminalUI zoom listener)

items.title: Clear buffer with Ctrl+Shift+K and select-all with Ctrl+Shift+A
steps: Press Ctrl+Shift+A; verify the whole buffer is selected (highlight)
Press Ctrl+Shift+K; verify the scrollback is cleared leaving only the current line
items.expected: Ctrl+Shift+A selects all buffer content; Ctrl+Shift+K clears the xterm buffer (terminal.clear())

items.title: Ctrl+← / Ctrl+→ word navigation moves the cursor by words at the shell prompt
preconditions: ≥1 shell terminal open with a readline-capable shell
steps: Focus a shell terminal
Type 'echo one two three' without pressing Enter
Press Ctrl+← three times
Type 'X' and observe where it is inserted
Press Ctrl+→ and verify the cursor jumps forward a word
items.expected: Ctrl+←/→ are intentionally passed through to the terminal (terminalManager.js: 'Ctrl+Left/Right for word navigation → let terminal handle', returns true) and move the cursor word-by-word; the typed 'X' lands at a word boundary, not at line end. No app-level shortcut hijacks these keys.

automationHints.notes: Listed in the Keyboard Shortcuts help window ('Word Navigation Left/Right'). Pure passthrough — the assertion is that PATAPIM does NOT intercept them.

items.title: Ctrl+Delete deletes the word after the cursor (sends ESC d to the shell)
preconditions: ≥1 shell terminal open with a readline-capable shell (bash/Git Bash)
steps: Focus a shell terminal
Type 'echo alpha beta' without pressing Enter
Press Home (cursor moves to line start)
Press Ctrl+→ once to place the cursor after 'echo '
Press Ctrl+Delete
Press Enter and read the output
items.expected: Ctrl+Delete removes the word after the cursor ('alpha'); the executed command is 'echo beta' and prints 'beta'. Handler at terminalManager.js attachCustomKeyEventHandler sends '\x1bd' (ESC d) for Ctrl+Delete.

automationHints.notes: Key events must originate from real keyboard input (computer_key) so xterm's attachCustomKeyEventHandler sees them — terminal_write bypasses the handler. Behavior depends on shell readline; PowerShell's PSReadLine maps ESC d differently, prefer bash.

items.title: Ctrl+V with an image on the clipboard pastes a quoted temp-file path
preconditions: Clipboard contains an image (no text), e.g. via a screenshot to clipboard
steps: Copy an image to the clipboard (Win+Shift+S region capture or PowerShell Set-Clipboard -Path on an image won't do — must be image data)
Press Ctrl+V in a shell terminal
items.expected: The image is saved to a temp file (CLIPBOARD_SAVE_IMAGE IPC) and the quoted file path is pasted into the prompt instead of nothing

automationHints.notes: Image-fallback only triggers on the Ctrl+V path (allowImageFallback=true), not right/middle-click. Getting raw image data on the clipboard from automation is the hard part — may need a human

items.title: Ctrl+Y yanks (re-inserts) text previously killed with Ctrl+Backspace
preconditions: ≥1 shell terminal open with bash (readline kill-ring)
steps: Focus a bash terminal
Type 'echo yankme' without pressing Enter
Press Ctrl+Backspace (kills the word 'yankme' via Ctrl+W mapping)
Press Ctrl+Y
Press Enter and read the output
items.expected: Ctrl+Y re-inserts the killed word from readline's kill-ring; the command runs as 'echo yankme' and prints 'yankme'. Ctrl+Y is a shell passthrough (no PATAPIM handler) but is documented in the Keyboard Shortcuts window as 'Yank (Paste killed text)', so it must reach the pty un-intercepted.

automationHints.notes: Works only with shells exposing a kill-ring (bash/zsh). PowerShell PSReadLine maps Ctrl+Y to Redo — run in Git Bash.

items.title: Line-editing shortcuts: Ctrl+Backspace, Home/End, Shift+Enter, Ctrl+Z
steps: Type 'echo one two', press Ctrl+Backspace; verify 'two' deleted
Press Home then End; verify cursor moves to line start/end
In a Claude terminal type a message and press Shift+Enter; verify a newline is inserted without submitting
Press Ctrl+Z after deleting; verify readline undo (sends Ctrl+_)
items.expected: Ctrl+Backspace deletes the previous word (sends 0x17); Home/End send Ctrl+A/Ctrl+E; Shift+Enter sends \n (multi-line input, no submit); Ctrl+Z sends undo instead of SIGTSTP

automationHints.notes: Behavior depends on the shell honoring readline codes; PowerShell PSReadLine handles Ctrl+A/E differently — Git Bash is the cleanest target

items.title: Drag-reorder toolbar buttons with ghost + drop indicator; order persists
steps: Press and drag a reorderable toolbar button (e.g. the Tasks button) ≥5px horizontally onto another button's far side; release
Verify the new order in the DOM
Restart the dev instance and re-check the order
items.expected: After the 5px threshold a .toolbar-reorder-ghost clone follows the cursor, the hovered target shows .drop-before/.drop-after, body gets .toolbar-reordering; on drop the button is re-inserted at the indicator and the order persists across restart; a plain click (<5px movement) still activates the button instead of dragging

automationHints.notes: Note: terminal TABS themselves have no drag-reorder — only pinning reorders tabs; this covers the toolbar buttons, the only drag-reorder in the tab bar. Update button + hamburger stay anchored at the end.

items.title: Tab-bar height resize handle: drag to add rows, double-click to reset, persists
preconditions: Horizontal tab layout; enough tabs that multi-row is meaningful (≥4 tabs or wide tabs)
steps: Drag the .tab-bar-resize-handle (bottom edge of the tab bar) downward ~40px; release
Verify the bar height snapped to a whole number of rows and localStorage 'patapim-tabbar-max-rows' was written
Restart the dev instance and verify the row preference is re-applied
Double-click the handle
items.expected: Drag sets _userMaxRows (1..10) as a minimum preferred height (content can still force more rows); value persists across restart; double-click clears the preference (localStorage key removed) and the bar returns to auto height

automationHints.notes: Handle is a thin strip — get coords from a full computer_screenshot. body gets .tabbar-resizing during drag.

items.title: Toolbar width grip compresses the actions container so buttons wrap; double-click resets
preconditions: Horizontal tab layout with at least one visible tab (drag is ignored with no tabs)
steps: Drag the .toolbar-width-handle (left edge of the toolbar buttons) to the RIGHT ~80px; release
Verify the buttons wrapped into extra rows and 'patapim-toolbar-max-width' was saved
Drag fully LEFT past the natural width; release — constraint should clear
Set a constraint again, then double-click the handle
items.expected: Drag-right shrinks --toolbar-max-width (never below ~half the natural width, so no 1-button strip) and the tab bar grows to keep all buttons visible; drag-left to/past natural width clears the saved constraint; double-click resets to auto and re-runs auto-fit

automationHints.notes: body gets .toolbar-resizing during drag. Inverted axis: drag RIGHT = shrink.

items.title: Add a custom 'Run in terminal' tool button via the panel form and verify it persists to .patapim/toolbar.json
preconditions: Custom Toolbar panel open on a local project
steps: Click the Add button ('#custom-toolbar-add-btn') — the inline '.ct-form' appears
Type 'e2e-tool' in '.ct-form-label-input'
Open the icon picker via '.ct-icon-trigger' and select an icon cell ('.ct-icon-cell')
Leave action type as 'Run in terminal' ('.ct-form-type-trigger') and type 'echo gap-ipc-002' in '.ct-form-value-input'
Click '.ct-btn-save'
Verify a new '.ct-card' labeled 'e2e-tool' renders in the panel
Read <project>/.patapim/toolbar.json from disk and verify the button entry (label, command) was saved
Click the card and verify 'echo gap-ipc-002' is executed in the active terminal
Cleanup: delete the tool via its card menu ('.ct-card-menu' → Delete)
items.expected: Tool card appears immediately, toolbar:save-config writes the entry to .patapim/toolbar.json, and clicking the card runs the command in the active terminal via run-command

automationHints.notes: SAVE_TOOLBAR_CONFIG handler in main/patapimProject.js; global (all-projects) buttons go to ~/.patapim/toolbar-global.json instead — keep '.ct-check-all-projects' unchecked

items.title: Run a tool with 'Ask for params' + 'Ask for files': params overlay, native file picker, live command preview, Ctrl+Enter run
preconditions: A custom command tool saved with '.ct-check-ask-params' and '.ct-check-ask-files' checked
steps: Click the tool card — a params overlay opens instead of running immediately
Type 'extra-arg' in the params input and verify the command preview appends it live
Click the Attach files button — a native OS file picker opens (pick-files IPC); select one file
Verify the picked file appears as a removable chip and is appended (quoted if it contains spaces) to the preview
Remove the file via its '.ct-params-file-rm' button and re-add it
Press Ctrl+Enter (or click '.ct-params-run') and verify the composed command is sent to the terminal
Re-open the overlay and verify Escape / '.ct-params-cancel' / overlay-backdrop click all dismiss without running
items.expected: Overlay previews the exact composed command; pick-files returns absolute paths from the native dialog; run sends the full command (base + params + quoted files) to the active terminal

automationHints.notes: Native file dialog needs computer_* tools to drive; PICK_FILES handled in main with { multi: true, title: 'Attach files' }

items.title: Toggle the Custom Toolbar (Tools) panel via the tab-bar tools button and verify it loads the project's tool config
steps: Click the '.btn-custom-toolbar-toggle' button in the terminal tab bar toolbar
Verify the '#custom-toolbar-panel' element gains the 'visible' class and shows a header with collapse and close buttons plus an Add button ('#custom-toolbar-add-btn')
Click '#custom-toolbar-close' (or the collapse button '#custom-toolbar-collapse-btn') to dismiss
Click the toggle again and confirm the panel re-opens with the same content (config re-requested via toolbar:get-config)
items.expected: Panel opens listing the project's custom tool cards (or an empty state), driven by the toolbar:get-config → toolbar:config-data IPC round trip; closing and reopening is idempotent

automationHints.notes: Panel element is created lazily on init; config handlers live in main/patapimProject.js (GET_TOOLBAR_CONFIG / TOOLBAR_CONFIG_DATA)

items.title: Tool card context menu: Edit (with label rename moving the tool folder), Pin to tab bar, Open tool folder, Delete
preconditions: Custom Toolbar panel open with at least one saved custom tool that has a tool folder
steps: Click the card's '⋯' menu button ('.ct-card-menu') — a '.ct-context-menu' appears with Edit / Pin to tab bar / Open tool folder / Delete
Click 'Open tool folder' — the OS file explorer opens the tool's folder under the project's .patapim directory (toolbar:open-tool-folder)
Re-open the menu, click 'Pin to tab bar' and verify the tool button appears in the terminal tab bar; unpin afterwards
Re-open the menu, click Edit, change the label, Save — verify the tool folder on disk is renamed to match (toolbar:rename-tool-folder)
Re-open the menu and click Delete — the card disappears and the entry is removed from .patapim/toolbar.json
Verify the context menu also dismisses on Escape and outside click
items.expected: All four actions work: folder opens in Explorer, pin mirrors the tool into the tab bar, rename moves the on-disk tool folder, delete removes card + config entry

automationHints.notes: 'Open tool folder' opens a real OS window — verify via file_tree that the folder exists rather than screenshotting Explorer; rename handled by RENAME_TOOL_FOLDER in main/patapimProject.js

items.title: Clicking a grid cell makes its terminal active (single .grid-cell.active)
steps: Click inside the content of the non-active cell (not the header buttons)
Inspect cells
items.expected: Clicked cell gains .active, the previous one loses it (exactly one active); manager.activeTerminalId switches; grid view stays (no mode change)

items.title: Grid cell Close button (×) closes that terminal and the grid re-renders
preconditions: ≥2 terminals open (one disposable bash); grid view active
steps: Click the × in the disposable terminal's .grid-cell-actions
items.expected: closeTerminal runs (confirm dialog only if AI awaiting input); the cell disappears and remaining terminals re-fill the grid

items.title: Grid cell Focus button (◎) returns to tab view with that terminal active
steps: In grid view click the ◎ button in the SECOND cell's header
Verify view mode and active terminal
items.expected: View switches to tabs (setViewMode('tabs')), the clicked terminal is the active tab, .grid-layout-select hides

items.title: Grid shows at most rows×cols terminals; extras are simply not rendered
preconditions: 5 terminals open; grid view with layout 2x2
steps: Set .grid-layout-select to '2x2' with 5 terminals open
Count .grid-cell elements
Switch to '3x2' and re-count
items.expected: 2x2 renders exactly 4 cells (terminals.slice(0, maxCells) — 5th terminal hidden but still alive in tab view); 3x2 renders all 5; no error and no crash for the overflow terminal

automationHints.notes: Cleanup: close the extra terminals and return to tab view.

items.title: Drag a grid cell's right resizer to resize adjacent cells
steps: Drag the left cell's .grid-resizer-right ~100px to the right; release
items.expected: Cursor becomes col-resize, the cell grows while the sibling shrinks (both respect 150px minimum), terminals re-fit (manager.fitAll) without xterm rendering artifacts; same behavior for .grid-resizer-bottom in a 2-row layout

automationHints.notes: Cell sizes are stored only in an in-memory Map (cellSizes) — note: a re-render (layout change) resets them; visual smoothness is the human part.

items.title: Grid layout select offers all 8 presets and is hidden in tab view
steps: In tab view verify .grid-layout-select is display:none
Toggle to grid view and enumerate the select's options
items.expected: Options are exactly 1x2, 1x3, 1x4, 2x1, 2x2, 3x1, 3x2, 3x3 (GRID_LAYOUTS keys); select is visible only while viewMode === 'grid' and its value tracks the current layout

automationHints.notes: TRM-12/13 already verified toggle + 1x2/2x2 relayout; this checks the full option set and visibility gating.

items.title: Layout round-trip Disabled → Vertical → Horizontal with ✓ on current mode; mirrors removed outside vertical
steps: Open the layout menu (right-click empty tab-bar area; in disabled mode use the bar strip) and verify '✓' sits on 'Disabled'
Click 'Vertical' — verify tabs return as a left column and .vtpn-mirror buttons exist in the chip
Reopen the menu, verify ✓ on Vertical, click 'Horizontal'
Verify tabs move to a top row and the .vtpn-mirror clones are removed from the DOM
items.expected: Each switch toggles body classes correctly (terminals-vertical / terminals-list-disabled / neither), the ✓ badge tracks the current mode, and leaving vertical removes the mirrored chip buttons

automationHints.notes: Restore the layout the run started with at the end (TRM-14 left dev in Horizontal).

items.title: Switch terminal layout to Disabled — tab list hidden, chip becomes the title
preconditions: ≥2 terminals open; current layout Vertical or Horizontal
steps: Right-click an EMPTY part of the tab bar (below/beside the tabs, not on a tab or button)
In the 'TERMINAL LAYOUT' menu click 'Disabled'
Inspect body classes and the toolbar chip
items.expected: body gains .terminals-list-disabled (and loses .terminals-vertical); the tab list is hidden; the project chip (.vertical-toolbar-project-name) doubles as the bar title showing project name plus the ACTIVE terminal's name (.vtpn-text); active terminal still fills the content area

automationHints.notes: This is the path TRM-14 could not exercise. In vertical mode right-click just below the last tab; in horizontal the empty strip right of the tabs is tiny — switch to vertical first if needed.

items.title: Middle-click the title chip in list-disabled mode closes the active terminal
preconditions: Layout = Disabled; ≥2 terminals open (one disposable bash, active)
steps: Middle-click the project/terminal title chip text (NOT the buttons in .vtpn-actions)
Observe
items.expected: Active terminal closes via the normal closeTerminal confirm flow (dialog only if AI is awaiting input); another terminal becomes active and the chip text updates; middle-clicking the mirrored buttons area (.vtpn-actions) must NOT close anything

automationHints.notes: Handler guards: button===1, list-disabled class, target not in .vtpn-actions.

items.title: Middle-click on the chip is inert in vertical mode
steps: Middle-click the project chip above the terminal list
items.expected: Nothing closes — the chip middle-click handler returns early unless body has .terminals-list-disabled; terminal count unchanged

items.title: Custom icon and custom name survive app restart
preconditions: Dev instance; a tab with custom icon + custom rename applied
steps: Assign an icon and rename a tab to 'persist-check'
Restart the dev instance (rebuild-dev-safe.bat) and wait for ready
Inspect the corresponding tab
items.expected: After restart the tab still shows the custom name 'persist-check' and the same .tab-custom-icon

automationHints.notes: Restart kills/restarts only the dev instance; never run rebuild-dev.bat from the Bash tool.

items.title: Pinned terminal stays pinned, first in the list, and is re-resumed after restart
preconditions: Dev instance; ≥2 terminals, the second one pinned via .tab-pin
steps: Pin the second terminal (click its .tab-pin)
Restart the dev instance and wait for ready
Inspect tab order and pin state
items.expected: After restart the pinned terminal renders FIRST (getTerminalStates returns pinned at top), carries .pinned class and .tab-pin.is-pinned with 'Unpin' title, and its session is auto-resumed

automationHints.notes: Basic pin/unpin toggle already PASSed as TRM-08 — this item is the restart persistence only.

items.title: Terminal layout orientation persists across restart
steps: Set layout to Vertical via the layout context menu
Restart the dev instance and wait for ready
Check body classes
items.expected: After restart body has .terminals-vertical (panelManager snapshot saved via LAYOUT_SAVE_CURRENT and re-applied by bootstrap → applyLayout); tab list renders as the left column

automationHints.notes: Snapshot save is debounced 250ms — wait ~1s before restarting.

items.title: 'More icons' (…) opens the expanded icon picker with categories and search
steps: Right-click the tab
Click the '…' swatch (.icon-swatch.more) at the end of the CUSTOM ICONS row
Verify the expanded picker modal; pick any icon or close it
items.expected: Context menu hides and openIconPicker shows the full picker (categories + search + favorites, SVG presets and emoji); picking an icon applies it to the tab; this was NOT positively confirmed in the 2026-06-08 run (TRM-11 partial) — explicit retest

automationHints.notes: Use ui_click on .icon-swatch.more instead of pixel clicks — the prior run likely hit a neighboring swatch.

items.title: 'Reset Tab Width' appears only after a custom resize and clears it
preconditions: A tab with a custom width set (run item 018 first)
steps: Right-click the resized tab — confirm a 'Reset Tab Width' item is present
Click it
Right-click the same tab again
items.expected: First menu shows the reset item; clicking removes the entry from 'tab-widths' and clears inline width styles (tab returns to default width); second menu no longer shows the item

automationHints.notes: Item is conditional on tabWidths[termId] being set.

items.title: Assign a favorite icon swatch from the context menu's CUSTOM ICONS row
preconditions: ≥1 terminal tab open, no custom icon set
steps: Right-click the tab
In the CUSTOM ICONS row click the first .icon-swatch
Inspect the tab
items.expected: Menu closes; tab gains a .tab-custom-icon element before .tab-name matching the chosen icon; reopening the menu shows that swatch with the .selected class

automationHints.notes: Swatches are ~5px targets per the 2026-06-08 run — prefer ui_click on .icon-swatch once the menu is open.

items.title: Clear swatch (✕) removes the custom icon
preconditions: A tab with a custom icon assigned (run item 021 first)
steps: Right-click the tab
Click the .icon-swatch.clear (X) at the end of the icon row
items.expected: setTerminalIcon(id, null) runs: .tab-custom-icon is removed from the tab; reopening the menu shows no .selected swatch

items.title: Context menu composition and order match spec
preconditions: ≥2 terminal tabs open (so Close is meaningful)
steps: Right-click a terminal tab
Screenshot and enumerate the menu items top to bottom
items.expected: Order: Pin/Unpin (first), divider, 'CUSTOM ICONS' section label + swatch row (+ … + ✕), divider, Rename, [Reset Tab Width only if custom width], divider, Schedule a Command, Loop a Command, divider, Close — and the menu clamps inside the viewport when opened near an edge

automationHints.notes: Dismiss with Escape (TRM-18 verified Escape works) to avoid side effects.

items.title: Context menu repositions when opened near the right/bottom viewport edge
preconditions: A terminal tab close to the window's right edge (resize a tab wide or use horizontal layout)
steps: Right-click a tab as far right as possible
Measure the menu's bounding rect
items.expected: Menu's right edge stays ≥5px inside window.innerWidth and bottom inside innerHeight (clamp logic after .visible is added); no clipped items

items.title: Drag tab resize handle to set per-tab width (clamped 60–600px, persisted)
preconditions: ≥2 terminal tabs open; auto-resize-tabs preference OFF
steps: Locate the .tab-resize-handle on a tab's right edge
Drag it 80px to the right; release
Drag it far left (>600px) and release
items.expected: During drag the tab's width/maxWidth follow the cursor, cursor becomes col-resize; width is clamped to [60, 600]; on mouseup the width is saved to localStorage 'tab-widths' keyed by terminal id and survives a re-render (tab switch)

automationHints.notes: Handle is a thin strip on the tab's right edge — read coordinates from a fresh full computer_screenshot.

items.title: Middle-click a terminal tab closes it
preconditions: ≥2 terminal tabs open; one is a disposable bash terminal
steps: Middle-click (mouse button 2 / wheel click) the disposable bash tab
items.expected: Tab closes through manager.closeTerminal (same confirm flow as ×: a confirm dialog appears only if the terminal is awaiting AI input); tab count drops by 1

automationHints.notes: mousedown e.button === 1 handler; needs a real middle click via computer_mouse_click with middle button.

items.title: Rename input: empty value reverts; clicking elsewhere (blur) commits
steps: Open rename via double-click, select-all and Delete so the input is empty, press Enter — verify original name kept
Open rename again, type 'blur-commit', click into the terminal area (blur without Enter)
items.expected: Empty input falls back to the previous name (trim || currentName); blur commits 'blur-commit' without needing Enter; no stuck input element remains in the DOM in either case

items.title: Rename input: Escape cancels and restores the original name
steps: Double-click the tab name to open .tab-rename-input
Type 'should-not-stick'
Press Escape
items.expected: Input is replaced by the original span with the ORIGINAL name; no rename is sent (input.value reset to currentName before blur)

items.title: Auto-resize tabs mode hides resize handles and sizes tabs to content
preconditions: Auto-resize tabs preference available in Preferences (patapim-auto-resize-tabs)
steps: Enable the auto-resize tabs preference
Inspect the tab bar: handles, tab widths, a tab with a long name
Try to drag a tab's right edge
Disable the preference (cleanup)
items.expected: Tab bar root gets .auto-resize-tabs; .tab-resize-handle is hidden/inert (mousedown handler returns early); stored widths are cleared from non-restoring tabs so tabs grow to fit their names; restoring tabs are excluded

automationHints.notes: Preference path in Preferences UI to be located during execution; setAutoResize is called from multiTerminalUI on the stored flag.

items.title: Middle-click an editor tab closes the editor
preconditions: An editor tab open in the tab bar (open a file via the in-app editor)
steps: Middle-click the .editor-tab
items.expected: Editor tab closes via onEditorTabClose; terminal tabs unaffected

items.title: Claude waiting for input shows green name + amber bell (needs-attention)
steps: Send a short prompt and wait for Claude to finish answering
Switch to a different tab so the finished tab is inactive
Inspect the finished tab
items.expected: Tab gains .needs-attention: static green .tab-name (no pulse) with 20px left padding and an amber bell ::before; when that tab is active it also gets the rgba(63,185,80,0.12) background tint

automationHints.notes: Bell is a CSS ::before — verify visually via screenshot, class via ui_state.

items.title: Working Claude terminal shows red pulsing 'processing' tab state
preconditions: 1 Claude Code terminal open and trusted
steps: Send a prompt to the Claude terminal that takes ≥10s (e.g. 'count to 30 slowly')
While it works, check the tab's classList
items.expected: Tab gains .processing; .tab-name is red (#f85149) with the tab-processing-pulse animation; class clears when Claude stops

automationHints.notes: Use terminal_write to drive the prompt; poll ui_state for the class.

items.title: Active loop adds loop icon with progress tooltip; popover cancels the loop
steps: Right-click the bash tab → 'Loop a Command', enter 'echo loop-test', set REPEAT 3 / EVERY 60 seconds, press Enter
Verify the tab shows the loop arrows icon and its title contains iteration progress (tabbar.loopProgress)
Click the loop icon and press the popover's Cancel button
items.expected: Tab gains .tab-loop-icon with a 'loop x/y' progress title; clicking opens a .schedule-popover (loop variant) with command, iteration/interval details and Cancel; Cancel stops the loop and the icon is removed

automationHints.notes: 60s interval means at most one echo fires before cancel — harmless.

items.title: Close × is omitted when only one terminal tab remains
preconditions: Exactly 1 terminal open, no editor tabs
steps: Inspect the single .terminal-tab for a .tab-close button
Create a second terminal via .btn-new-terminal and re-inspect both tabs
items.expected: With 1 terminal the tab has NO .tab-close button (render guard: terminals.length > 1 || editors.length > 0); after creating a second terminal both tabs show ×

automationHints.notes: Guard lives in the full-render template (line ~2144).

items.title: Duplicate terminal names get visual ' (2)' suffix
steps: Double-click the first tab's name, type 'dup-test', press Enter
Double-click the second tab's name, type 'dup-test', press Enter
Read both .tab-name texts
items.expected: Tabs render as 'dup-test' and 'dup-test (2)' (computeUniqueTerminalLabels suffix is visual only; renaming one tab back removes the suffix from the other on next update)

automationHints.notes: Double-click needs computer_* (no ui dblclick). Use [data-terminal-id] selectors, not :nth-child (non-tab siblings precede tabs).

items.title: Hover tooltip shows 'Terminal N' title plus live recap
preconditions: ≥1 terminal tab open; a Claude terminal that has produced output gives the richer recap case
steps: Move the real mouse cursor over a terminal tab and hold for ~1s
Check document.body for .tab-instant-tooltip
Move the cursor off the tab bar
items.expected: A .tab-instant-tooltip is appended to body below the tab with .tooltip-title = terminal-id text (tabbar.terminalId) and, for terminals with recent AI output, a .tooltip-recap clamped to ~3 lines; tooltip hides on mouseleave and never lingers after the tab is removed

automationHints.notes: Tooltip is mouseover-driven; ui_click won't trigger it. Read coordinates from a fresh full computer_screenshot.

items.title: Pending AI-session restore shows amber pulsing tab with scrolling marquee name
preconditions: A Claude terminal with an active session existed before app restart; restart the app so the session is pending restore
steps: Restart the dev instance with a resumable Claude session
Before clicking the restoring tab, inspect it
Activate the terminal and let the restore complete
items.expected: Tab has .restoring: amber pulsing background and a .marquee-inner span with duplicated scrolling text inside .tab-name; tab keeps default width even in auto-resize mode; after restore completes the class flips to .was-restored

items.title: Per-terminal browser shows 'Browser N' label on the tab
steps: Click the browser toggle in the toolbar (.btn-browser-toggle) to open the per-terminal browser
Inspect the active tab
Toggle the browser off (cleanup)
items.expected: Tab gains a .tab-browser-label span reading the tabbar.browserTab text with the terminal number; label gets .detached when the browser is popped out; label is removed when the browser closes

items.title: Plan mode tints the tab cyan; plan-finished glows green/teal
steps: In the Claude terminal press Shift+Tab until plan mode is active, send a planning prompt
Verify the tab while planning
Approve/exit the plan and verify again
items.expected: While planning: .plan-mode class, cyan name (#11a8cd) and cyan background tint. After the plan is ready: .plan-finished with the green/teal breathing glow animation

items.title: Scheduled command adds clock icon; clicking it opens popover with Run now / Edit / Cancel
steps: Right-click a tab → 'Schedule a Command', enter 'echo scheduled-test', pick the 'In 1 hour' preset, press Enter
Verify the tab now shows a clock icon
Click the clock icon
In the popover click the Cancel button (cleanup)
items.expected: Tab gains .tab-schedule-icon titled with tabbar.schedulePending; clicking it opens a .schedule-popover showing the Scheduled header, the command text, the date/time, and Run now / Edit / Cancel buttons; Cancel removes the schedule and the clock icon disappears on next render

automationHints.notes: Native-feel context menu needs computer_*; popover buttons are DOM, ui_click works. Do NOT click Run now.

items.title: At max terminals the new-terminal buttons' tooltips switch to the limit message
preconditions: Project open; know manager.maxTerminals (create terminals until creation stops)
steps: Create terminals via .btn-new-terminal until the count stops growing
Read the title attributes of .btn-new-terminal and .btn-claude-terminal
Close the extra terminals (cleanup)
items.expected: Both buttons' title becomes the tabbar.terminalLimit message including the max count; after closing one terminal the titles revert to 'New terminal' / AI label

automationHints.notes: Cleanup: middle-click or × each extra terminal.

items.title: Plain shell output marks tab 'recently-active' (lighter gray name)
preconditions: 2 terminals: one bash that just ran a command, one idle ≥31 min (or freshly created)
steps: Run 'echo hi' in the bash terminal
Switch to the other tab
Inspect the bash tab's classes

items.title: Telegram-routed terminal shows airplane icon next to the name

Inspect the tab

items.title: Drag the vertical list resize handle — width clamps 150–500px and persists

Drag it far left (past the minimum); release

items.title: Mirrored AI/new-terminal buttons in the vertical chip create terminals
steps: Locate the two .vtpn-mirror buttons inside the project chip's .vtpn-actions
Click the mirrored new-terminal button
Click the mirrored AI (Claude) button

items.title: Right-click a mirrored chip button opens the shell/AI picker anchored at the clone
steps: Right-click the mirrored new-terminal button in the chip

Escape; right-click the mirrored AI button and verify the AI picker (Claude Code / Codex / Antigravity…) opens below it

items.title: Add Existing Folder (📁) and New Project (+) header buttons open their flows
steps: Click #btn-add-project (folder icon in PROJECTS header) — a NATIVE folder picker opens

Press Escape to dismiss it

items.title: Adding a project beyond the plan limit shows the upgrade modal

items.title: Archive a project with the hover ↓ button and move it back

steps: Hover the test project's row — the floating action pill appears (magnifier, +, ↓)
Click the ↓ button (title 'Move to Later')

CLEANUP: expand Later, hover the test project row, click the ↑ button (title 'Move to Now') to unarchive
Verify it returns to the Now list

items.title: Auto git pull status icon lifecycle when opening a project
preconditions: Test project is a git repo with a reachable remote and autoGitSync ON (item 017)
steps: Select a different project, then select the test project (triggers WORKSPACE_PROJECT_OPENED → background git pull --ff-only)
Watch the .project-git-pull-status icon: it should switch to a spinner (git-pulling), then to a ✓ (git-ok) on success, then revert to the cloud-down icon after ~3s
Make the working tree dirty (touch a file), reopen the project, and verify the icon stays as the cloud with a 'skipped: dirty tree' tooltip (git-skipped)
CLEANUP: restore the tree and toggle autoGitSync off if it wasn't on before
items.expected: Status transitions pulling → ok → resting (3s revert), skip reasons surface in the tooltip (autoPullSkipDirty / NoRemote / NoGit / Disabled / Remote / Missing), errors tint the icon via .git-error; pull is best-effort and never blocks the UI.

automationHints.notes: Transitions are fast — take rapid screenshots or read classes via ui_state-equivalent; tooltip text lives in dataset.tipDesc.

items.title: Hover the cloud / auto-pull / syncthing status icon to see its tooltip
preconditions: PATAPIM window frontmost, maximized and unoccluded
≥1 project with cloudSync, autoGitSync, or syncthingSync enabled
Move the real mouse precisely onto the 12px icon (use element bounding box center, not OCR)

Move the real mouse to the icon's center and hold

Move away and verify the tip hides

automationHints.notes: Seed BLOCKED: icon hit-target only a few px at 3x downscale. Compute exact screen coords from getBoundingClientRect via a UI-state route if available, or zoom the app.

items.title: Drag a project row to reorder the Now list

items.title: Expand All / Collapse All header button toggles every project with terminals

items.title: Initialize a non-PATAPIM folder via the 'Initialize as PATAPIM' button: confirm dialog, existing-files warning, cancel and accept paths

Because CLAUDE.md already exists, verify the warning block (dialog.initProject.existingWarning) lists 'CLAUDE.md' and the dialog type is 'warning'

Click #btn-initialize-patapim again and this time click 'Initialize' (dialog.initProject.initialize)
Verify .patapim/, STRUCTURE.json, PROJECT_NOTES.md, tasks.json and QUICKSTART.md are created on disk and the pre-existing CLAUDE.md is NOT overwritten

Verify #btn-initialize-patapim is now hidden for this project

items.title: Arrow keys + Enter navigate and select projects in the list

items.title: Quick-create a local project end-to-end
