Verify a .quick-create-popover opens with 'Local' (✓ checked) and 'SSH' items
Click 'SSH'
Verify the chip label becomes 'SSH', the input placeholder changes to the user@host:/path form, the input gets class ssh-mode, and the 🔖 saved-connections button appears
Switch back to Local — placeholder reverts and 🔖 hides
items.expected: Popover toggles mode; placeholder, chip tooltip (toggleSSHTooltip/toggleLocalTooltip), ssh-mode class and saved-button visibility all track the mode; input keeps focus throughout.

automationHints.notes: Clicking the chip while the popover is open closes it (toggle).

items.title: Saved SSH connections popover: empty state, save, fill, delete
preconditions: No saved connections (or note the existing ones)
steps: Click the 🔖 button — popover opens with header 'Saved connections' and (if none) the empty-state text
Type a valid connection string in the input (e.g. 'user@example.com:/home/user/proj'), reopen the popover, click the '+ Save current input' footer
An inline label prompt appears prefilled with the host — press Enter to save
Verify the connection now lists with label + user@host detail
Click the saved row — verify it fills the input with the raw connection string
CLEANUP: reopen the popover, click the row's × delete button, accept the confirm, verify the list returns to empty
items.expected: Footer is disabled (is-disabled class) while the input isn't a parseable SSH string; save → SSH_CONNECTIONS_ADD persists; row click fills input for editing; delete asks for confirm (deleteConnectionConfirm) then removes.

automationHints.notes: Delete uses window.confirm — drive with computer_key. Saving doesn't connect anywhere; safe with a fake host.

items.title: SSH quick-create with an unreachable/invalid connection string shows an error and allows retry
steps: Type an invalid string (e.g. 'nonsense-no-at-sign') and press Enter
Verify .quick-create-status shows an error (class 'error') and the input re-enables, keeping focus
Type a syntactically valid but unreachable string (e.g. 'user@192.0.2.1:/tmp') and press Enter
Verify 'Connecting…' status (projects.sshConnecting) shows, then an error after the connection fails, with the input re-enabled
items.expected: Failure path never dismisses the input: status shows the error text (or projects.quickCreateError fallback), controls re-enable, retry possible. No project is added.

automationHints.notes: Unreachable-host attempt may take several seconds to time out.

items.title: Quick Tasks section renders at the top and collapses/expands
steps: Verify a .projects-section-quick-tasks section renders ABOVE all project rows with a lightning icon, 'Quick Tasks' title and state badges
Click the section header — terminal sub-items collapse (toggle glyph ▸)
Click again — they re-expand (▾)
Click a quick-task sub-item and verify it activates that terminal
items.expected: Section only exists while quick-task terminals exist; header toggles collapse; badges mirror terminal states like project rows; section highlights as active when a quick task terminal is active.

automationHints.notes: Creating a quick task terminal is covered in the terminals section; here only the sidebar rendering is in scope.

items.title: Remove a project from the list via context menu (with confirm)
preconditions: Use the dedicated test project only
Note its exact path for re-adding
steps: Right-click the test project row, click 'Remove this project from the list (your files won't be deleted)'
A window.confirm dialog appears with the project name — accept it
Verify the row disappears and the folder still exists on disk
If it was the active project, verify another project auto-selects
CLEANUP: re-add the folder via the Add Existing Folder flow or project_add MCP tool
items.expected: Confirm dialog uses projects.removeConfirm text; on accept the project is removed from workspaces.json but files remain; canceling leaves it untouched; active project falls back to another project.

automationHints.notes: window.confirm is a blocking native dialog — drive it with computer_key (Enter/Esc), not ui_click.

items.title: Project row clock icon shows scheduled tasks and opens the schedule popover
preconditions: ≥1 pending scheduled command on a project (create one disposable schedule first)
steps: Verify the project row shows the .project-schedule-icon clock (class has-schedule on the row)
Click the clock — a popover lists the scheduled items (header from projects.scheduledHeader) with their time/cron description and a 'Run now' action
Dismiss the popover
CLEANUP: delete the disposable scheduled command
items.expected: Clock renders only while pending schedules exist (re-rendered on schedule changes); popover shows each task with cron/date formatting (projects.cronLabel / dateAtTime) and Run now (projects.runNow).

automationHints.notes: Scheduling mechanics belong to the scheduler section; here only the sidebar affordance is verified. Do NOT click 'Run now' on a real task.

items.title: Toggle the sidebar with Ctrl+B and the toggle button
steps: Press Ctrl+B — sidebar (#sidebar) hides (display:none); the #sidebar-toggle arrow flips to ▶ and gains class sidebar-is-hidden
Verify the terminal area re-fits to the freed width
Press Ctrl+B again — sidebar returns at its previous width, arrow flips back to ◀
Repeat using the #sidebar-toggle button clicks instead of the shortcut
items.expected: Both paths call sidebarResize.toggle(); hidden state saved to localStorage 'sidebar-hidden'; width before hide is restored on show; terminal refits each time.

automationHints.notes: Ctrl+B handled in index.js global keydown (modKey && !shift && 'b').

items.title: Drag the sidebar resize handle — width clamps to 240–500px and persists
steps: computer_drag the #sidebar-resize-handle 150px to the right — sidebar widens live
Drag far right (e.g. +600px) — width stops at 500px (MAX)
Drag far left — width stops at 240px (MIN)
Release and verify localStorage 'sidebar-width' holds the final width
Restart and verify the width is restored (and clamped if an old out-of-range value was stored)
items.expected: Live resize between MIN_WIDTH 240 and MAX_WIDTH 500; width persisted on mouseup; resize callback refits the terminal; saved value clamped on next init.

automationHints.notes: body gets class 'sidebar-resizing' during drag.

items.title: Double-click the sidebar resize handle resets width to default (400px)
steps: Double-click #sidebar-resize-handle
Verify the sidebar snaps to 400px and localStorage 'sidebar-width' = '400'
items.expected: resetWidth() applies DEFAULT_WIDTH 400 and persists it; terminal refits.

automationHints.notes: Send two rapid clicks (dblclick); right-side panels (Tasks etc.) have the same dblclick-reset behavior via sidePanelResize.js if time permits.

items.title: Remote (SSH) project row shows globe icon and host label
preconditions: ≥1 remote SSH project registered in the workspace
steps: Locate the remote project's row in the sidebar
Verify its icon is a globe (🌐, class project-icon-remote) instead of 📁
Verify a dim .project-remote-host label shows the host, with the full user@host:port as its tooltip
Right-click it and verify the context menu has NO 'Sync ▸' entry
items.expected: Remote projects are visually distinct (globe + host label) and excluded from sync options (proj.remote === true guard).

automationHints.notes: If no SSH project exists, register one via quick-create SSH against a reachable host first (or mark blocked).

items.title: Add a subfolder via context menu, including the own-git mode picker
preconditions: Test project with a real subdirectory on disk; one variant subdirectory containing its own .git
steps: Right-click the test project → 'Add Subfolder' — a NATIVE folder picker opens
Select a plain (non-git) subdirectory
Verify the subfolder entry appears indented under the project (📁 icon, name, no badge) with gitMode 'parent' by default
Repeat with a subdirectory that has its own .git — verify the centered git-mode picker appears ('has own git' prompt with 'Use parent repo' / 'Use own repo') and choose 'Use own repo'
Verify that subfolder shows the 'own' git badge
CLEANUP: remove both subfolders via their context menu
items.expected: SELECT_SUBFOLDER_FOLDER → native picker → ADD_SUBFOLDER; subfolders persist in workspaces.json and render indented between the project row and its terminals; own-git folders get the modal picker (Escape/backdrop cancels).

automationHints.notes: Native picker needs computer_* or human driving.

items.title: Right-click a subfolder shows the subfolder-specific context menu
preconditions: A project with ≥1 subfolder, project expanded
steps: Right-click a .subfolder-item row
Read the menu items
Press Escape
items.expected: Menu contains exactly: Open folder, Copy path, separator, git-mode toggle ('Keep own repo' or 'Use parent repo' with current mode annotation like '(parent repo)'), separator, 'Remove Subfolder'. Does NOT contain Set Color / Add Subfolder / Sync / Create Summary. Escape dismisses.

automationHints.notes: Seed used the fifa/minigames subfolder.

items.title: Subfolder + button creates a terminal whose CWD is the subfolder
steps: Hover the subfolder row, click its + button (project-llm-btn in the subfolder's hover pill)
Verify the subfolder auto-expands and a new terminal sub-item appears under it (indented, .subfolder-tabs-list)
Run `pwd` (or check terminal_info cwd) — it must equal the subfolder path
Verify the subfolder row shows a terminal-count badge and a chevron
CLEANUP: close the created terminal
items.expected: Terminal is created with cwd + subfolderPath of the subfolder, renders under the subfolder (excluded from the project-level tabs list), and selecting it sets the subfolder active.

automationHints.notes: The + uses mousedown, not click (drag suppression).

items.title: Toggle a subfolder's git mode and remove the subfolder
preconditions: Test project with a subfolder in parent mode
steps: Right-click the subfolder → click the git-mode toggle (label 'Use own repo' with '(parent repo)' annotation)
Verify the subfolder re-renders with the 'own' badge and the menu annotation flips to '(own repo)'
Toggle back to parent
Hover the subfolder row and click its × (title 'Remove Subfolder') — verify the entry disappears (files untouched)
items.expected: UPDATE_SUBFOLDER_GIT_MODE persists the mode; ownBadge/ownBadgeTitle strings render on the badge; REMOVE_SUBFOLDER removes only the workspace entry, not the directory.

automationHints.notes: No confirm dialog on Remove Subfolder — it removes immediately.

items.title: Sync flyout adapts to git vs non-git folders
preconditions: One local project that IS a git repo and one that is NOT
steps: Right-click the git-repo project → 'Sync ▸'
Verify the header shows the repo status (📦 'This folder is a git repository' variant) and the toggles offered are 'Auto git pull on open' + 'Sync to cloud' (no 'Sync folder files')
Dismiss; right-click the non-git project → 'Sync ▸'
Verify the header shows the non-repo status (📁) and the toggles are 'Sync folder files' (teal ⟳) + 'Sync to cloud' (no auto git pull)
items.expected: WORKSPACE_FOLDER_HAS_GIT drives the flyout: git auto-pull and Syncthing file-sync are mutually exclusive and never shown together; cloud metadata sync always offered.

automationHints.notes: Read-only — open and dismiss, do not click the toggles (that's item 017/043).

items.title: Toggle 'Sync to cloud' and 'Auto git pull on open' from the Sync flyout and revert
preconditions: Use the dedicated test project (a git repo for the auto-pull toggle)
Signed in for cloud sync to actually do anything
steps: Right-click the test project, click 'Sync ▸' — the .project-sync-menu flyout opens
Click the '☁ Sync to cloud' toggle — menu closes; re-open and verify a ✓ check appears next to it and a cloud icon appears beside the project name
Click '⤓ Auto git pull on open' — re-open and verify its ✓ and the cloud-with-down-arrow icon replaces the plain cloud
CLEANUP: toggle both OFF again and verify checks and icons disappear
items.expected: Each toggle invokes WORKSPACE_UPDATE_CLOUD_SYNC / WORKSPACE_UPDATE_AUTO_GIT_SYNC and persists in workspaces.json; row icons update on re-render (autoGitSync shows CLOUD_DOWN, cloudSync-only shows plain CLOUD).

automationHints.notes: Seed SKIPPED as external/destructive. Flyout opens at the original right-click coordinates. Remote (SSH) projects have no Sync entry.

items.title: Sync help (?) opens the explainer popup and closes via ×, backdrop and Escape
preconditions: Sync flyout open on any local project
steps: Click the '?' button in the flyout header
Verify a modal .sh-overlay/.sh-card appears with the two-box diagram (git repo vs plain folder), a cloud note and a mutual-exclusivity warning note
Close it with the × button; reopen; close by clicking the dark backdrop; reopen; close with Escape
items.expected: All three close paths work; opening the help closes the sync flyout first; no console errors.

items.title: Toggle 'Sync folder files' on a non-git folder and verify the teal icon + error paths
preconditions: A dedicated NON-git test folder added as a project
Account/file-sync feature available (else expect the disabled toast)
steps: Right-click the non-git project → Sync ▸ → click '⟳ Sync folder files'
If file sync is unavailable, verify a toast with projects.syncFilesDisabled text and no state change
If available, re-open the flyout and verify the ✓; verify the teal .project-syncthing-icon appears next to the project name
Hover the icon — tooltip shows syncFilesDesc (or syncing/conflict state text while active)
Run `git init` inside the folder, trigger a refresh — verify the git-transition path removes the syncthing icon (handed back to git)
CLEANUP: toggle file sync off, delete the .git folder
items.expected: Toggle persists via WORKSPACE_UPDATE_SYNCTHING_SYNC; res.ok===false surfaces reason toasts (sync-disabled / has-git); SYNCTHING_STATUS_EVENT drives syncing/error/conflict classes; folders that gain a .git auto-transition out of file sync.

automationHints.notes: Extra context-menu item 'Sync folder files' was first observed in the seed run as undocumented — now part of the Sync flyout.

items.title: Project row terminal count badges match terminal states
steps: Call terminal_list and count terminals per state for the project (processing / awaiting / attention / idle)
Screenshot the project row and read the .project-terminal-badges
Start a long-running command in one terminal and verify the busy (red) badge appears with the right count
items.expected: Badges are mutually exclusive counts: pulsing '?' for awaiting, green for finished/attention, red for busy, gray for idle; numbers match terminal_list reality; badge titles use projects.terminalAwaiting/Finished/Processing/Idle strings.

automationHints.notes: States: processing > awaiting > needsAttention > idle (no double counting).

items.title: Middle-click and × close a terminal sub-item; hover shows the recap tooltip
preconditions: Project expanded with ≥2 terminals (use disposable terminals)
steps: Hover a terminal sub-item — verify the .tab-instant-tooltip recap appears to the RIGHT of the sidebar with 'Terminal N' title and the last meaningful output paragraph
Click the sub-item's × (.tab-subitem-close) — terminal closes and the row disappears
Middle-click another sub-item — it closes too
Verify badges and counts update, including for ARCHIVED (Later) projects' expanded sub-items (regression: stale sub-items used to swallow close clicks)
items.expected: Both close paths call terminalManager.closeTerminal; sidebar updates immediately via refreshBadges, including #later-list entries; recap tooltip hides when the anchor leaves the DOM.

automationHints.notes: Create throwaway terminals first via terminal_create so nothing real is closed.

items.title: Right-click a terminal sub-item opens the tab bar's context menu
preconditions: PATAPIM window frontmost, maximized and unoccluded
Target the sub-item via its data-terminal-id bounding box, not OCR (rows are ~5px in downscaled view)
steps: Right-click the .project-tab-subitem row of a known terminal
Verify the SAME context menu as right-clicking its top tab appears (Rename, Pin/Unpin, icon picker, Close, etc.)
Press Escape to dismiss
items.expected: Sub-item routes to tabBar.showTerminalContextMenu — identical menu to the top tab bar; no errors when dismissed.

automationHints.notes: Seed BLOCKED: two right-click attempts missed the tiny target. Compute exact coords from the DOM element rect first.

items.title: Refresh projects button pulls cloud workspace and re-renders
steps: Click #btn-refresh-projects in the PROJECTS header
Verify the project list re-renders without errors (app_logs clean)
Optionally catch the transient 'refreshing' spinner class (~450ms minimum)
items.expected: WORKSPACES_SYNC_PULL_NOW invoked (no-op when sync is off), list re-rendered from latest local state, no console errors; spinner class held ≥450ms for responsiveness.

automationHints.notes: Works signed-out too (pull is a no-op) — account only needed to verify an actual cloud pull.

items.title: Cloud project with no local folder shows Clone/Link card
preconditions: Signed in with workspace sync; a synced project that has no local folder on this machine (missingPath card visible)
steps: Locate the project rendered with class project-missing (dimmed, 'unlinked' style name)
Verify it shows a 'Link' button (and 'Clone' too when the cloud record has a gitRemote)
Click 'Link' — a native folder picker opens; Escape to cancel and verify the card resets without error
OPTIONAL full path: pick the correct local folder and verify the card re-renders as a normal project row
items.expected: Path-less cloud cards never crash the render (null-path guard); Clone shows 'Cloning…' busy state and falls back to 'try Link' errors; Link via WORKSPACES_SYNC_RELINK_PROJECT; success re-renders via WORKSPACE_UPDATED.

automationHints.notes: Requires a second machine (or a manufactured cloud record) to produce the missing-path state.

items.title: Drag a file from the file tree into a terminal types its absolute path (quoted if it contains spaces)
preconditions: Project selected with visible files; a terminal open at a shell prompt
steps: With a real mouse drag (computer_drag), drag a file row from #file-tree onto the terminal area
Read the terminal input line
Repeat with a file whose absolute path contains a space (create one first if needed; cleanup after)
items.expected: The file's absolute path is typed into the dropped-on terminal; paths containing spaces arrive wrapped in double quotes (dragstart sets text/plain quoted + application/x-patapim-filepath)

automationHints.notes: HTML5 drag requires a real OS-level mouse drag; coordinates must come from a fresh full computer_screenshot. Drop resolves the target CELL, not just the active terminal — in grid mode verify it lands in the dropped-on pane

items.title: Drag an external file from OS Explorer onto a terminal delivers its path
preconditions: A terminal open at a shell prompt; a Windows Explorer window with a known file visible next to the PATAPIM window
steps: Drag a file from Windows Explorer and drop it onto the terminal area
Read the terminal input line
items.expected: The dropped file's absolute path is typed into the terminal (preload getDropPaths → deliverDropPaths); multi-file drops deliver all paths

items.title: Drop an external file onto a tree folder copies it into that folder with drag-over highlight
preconditions: Throwaway project with a visible folder in the tree; an external file ready to drag from OS Explorer
steps: Drag an external file over the folder row — observe the highlight
Drop it on the folder
Verify the tree and the folder contents on disk
Cleanup: delete the copied file
items.expected: While hovering, the folder row gets .drag-over highlight (removed on leave/drop); on drop the file is copied into the folder (COPY_FILES IPC) and the tree refreshes to show it

items.title: Editing content flips status to Modified and shows unsaved dot on the tab
preconditions: A file open in an editor tab with status 'Ready'
Throwaway test project (content will be modified)
steps: Click into .editor-textarea
Type a marker string (e.g. ' TEST_EDIT_MARKER')
items.expected: Status bar text changes to 'Modified' (amber, .editor-status.modified); the editor tab shows the unsaved dot indicator

automationHints.notes: computer_type requires real OS focus on the textarea (click with real mouse first)

items.title: Save with Ctrl+S persists to disk and resets status to Ready
preconditions: Editor tab open with unsaved changes (status 'Modified')
Editor textarea has true OS focus
steps: Press Ctrl+S
Verify status bar and tab dot
Read the file from disk and compare content
items.expected: Unsaved dot disappears, status returns to 'Ready' (after transient 'Saving...'/'Saved'); file content on disk includes the typed edit

automationHints.notes: Ctrl+S only saves when the textarea has true OS focus — if the find panel is focused it silently does nothing; the .btn-editor-save button is the reliable fallback

items.title: Save status transitions: Saving → Saved → Ready after ~2s
preconditions: Editor tab open with unsaved changes
steps: Click .btn-editor-save
Screenshot immediately (catch 'Saving...'/'Saved')
Wait ~2.5s and screenshot again
items.expected: Status shows 'Saving...' (.editor-status.saving) then 'Saved' (.editor-status.saved) on FILE_SAVED success; after ~2s it reverts to 'Ready' with the plain class; tab dot cleared; file tree refresh triggered (editor.js FILE_SAVED listener)

automationHints.notes: The Saving state is brief on local disk — the Saved state (2s) is the reliable observable

items.title: Find count shows 'N found' for matches and orange 'No results' for none
preconditions: Editor tab open with known content; find panel open
steps: Type a term with known matches (e.g. 'e') into .find-input
Verify .find-count
Type a guaranteed non-matching term (e.g. 'zzzQQQnomatch999')
items.expected: Matching term shows '<N> found' with no .no-results class; non-matching term shows 'No results' with .no-results styling (orange)

items.title: Navigate find matches via Next/Previous buttons and F3/Shift+F3
preconditions: Editor find panel open with a multi-match search term entered
steps: Click .btn-find-next twice
Click .btn-find-prev
Real-mouse-focus the find input, press F3 then Shift+F3
items.expected: Each action moves the textarea selection to the next/previous match (with wrap-around at ends); no console errors

automationHints.notes: Selection visible as a highlight box in screenshots; F3 handled in textarea keydown, Enter/Shift+Enter handled in find-input keydown

items.title: Open editor find panel via Ctrl+F and close via Escape / close button
preconditions: Editor tab open and textarea has true OS focus
steps: Press Ctrl+F
Verify find panel appears
Real-mouse-click into the find input, press Escape
Re-open and click .btn-find-close
items.expected: Ctrl+F shows the find panel (Find... input, prev/next chevrons) docked under the editor toolbar; Escape (with find input focused) and the close button both hide it and return focus to the textarea

automationHints.notes: Escape only closes when the find input has true OS focus; after a plain ui_click it may not

items.title: Toolbar find button toggles the find panel open/closed
steps: Click .btn-editor-find in the editor toolbar
Click it again
items.expected: First click removes .hidden from .editor-find-panel and focuses the find input; second click hides the panel again

items.title: Opening find with text selected prefills the search input
preconditions: Editor open; a single-line word selected in the textarea (e.g. double-click a word); textarea has OS focus
steps: Select a word in the textarea (double-click)
Press Ctrl+F
Read .find-input value and .find-count
items.expected: Find input is prefilled with the selected text (only when the selection has no newline), the input is focused/selected, and the match count is computed immediately

automationHints.notes: Double-click to select via computer_mouse_click with clickCount/double; multi-line selections must NOT prefill

items.title: Ctrl+H opens replace row; Replace All replaces every occurrence
preconditions: Editor tab open with a unique known marker string in content; textarea has OS focus
steps: Press Ctrl+H
Type the marker in .find-input (verify '1 found')
Type a replacement in .replace-input
Click the 'All' button (.btn-replace-all)
Save via the .btn-editor-save button
Read the file from disk
items.expected: Ctrl+H shows the find panel WITH the replace row; Replace All swaps all occurrences in the textarea, count becomes 'No results', status 'Modified'; after Save the replacement is on disk

automationHints.notes: Ctrl+S does NOT save while the find panel has focus — use the Save button

items.title: Single 'Replace' button replaces the current match and advances to the next
preconditions: Editor open with ≥2 occurrences of a known term; replace row open (Ctrl+H) with find and replace terms filled
steps: Click .btn-find-next to select the first match
Click the 'Replace' button (.btn-replace)
Inspect the content and the new selection
Press Enter inside .replace-input to replace the next one
Cleanup: restore content and save
items.expected: Only the selected occurrence is replaced (selection must match the search term), count decrements, status 'Modified', and the next match is auto-selected; Enter in the replace input performs the same single replace

items.title: Cursor position indicator shows line and column and updates on movement
preconditions: Editor tab open with multi-line content; textarea has OS focus
steps: Click at the start of the file
Read .editor-cursor-pos in the status bar
Press ArrowDown and ArrowRight a few times
Read it again
items.expected: Status bar right side shows the localized 'Ln <line>, Col <col>' (editor.cursorPosition) and updates on click/keyup/focus to match the caret position

items.title: Syntax highlighting renders per extension and falls back to plain text for unknown types
preconditions: Throwaway project with a .js, a .json/.md, and a file with an unmapped extension (e.g. .xyz)
steps: Open the .js file and screenshot the editor (colored tokens expected)
Open the .json or .md file and screenshot
Open the .xyz file and screenshot
Type in the .js editor and verify the highlight overlay updates and scroll stays in sync
items.expected: Known extensions render Prism-colored tokens in .editor-highlight (mapped via _getLanguage); the unknown extension renders un-colored plain text without errors; on input the overlay re-highlights and scrolling the textarea scrolls the overlay in lockstep (no visual offset)

automationHints.notes: Overlay/textarea misalignment is a visual judgment — human confirms. Files >25000 lines intentionally skip highlighting (perf)

items.title: Tab key inserts two spaces instead of moving focus
preconditions: Editor tab open; textarea has OS focus with caret at a known position
steps: Press Tab
Inspect the content at the caret
Verify the editor still has focus and the status flips to 'Modified'
Cleanup: undo/re-save original content
items.expected: Two spaces are inserted at the caret (selection replaced), caret advances by 2, focus stays in the textarea (no focus jump), status becomes 'Modified'

items.title: Copy All button copies content and shows 'Copied!' feedback that reverts
steps: Click .btn-editor-copy-all
Observe button label immediately
Wait ~2s and observe again
items.expected: Button label switches to 'Copied!' with .copied class immediately; after ~1.5s it reverts to 'Copy All'; clipboard contains the full file content

automationHints.notes: ui_click's returned .text already shows 'Copied!' (feedback fires synchronously)

items.title: Ctrl+Shift+C copies the whole file (same feedback as Copy All button)
preconditions: Editor tab open; textarea has true OS focus
steps: Press Ctrl+Shift+C
Observe the Copy All button
Paste the clipboard into a terminal to verify
items.expected: Copy All button shows the 'Copied!' feedback; clipboard contains the full editor content (shortcut handled in textarea keydown: mod+shift+'C')

automationHints.notes: Handler matches e.key === 'C' (uppercase, as produced with Shift held)

items.title: Opening a file that was deleted from disk fails gracefully (no tab, no crash)
preconditions: Throwaway project; a file visible in the tree that is then deleted from disk without refreshing the tree (watcher race: delete and click quickly, or pre-hide panel refresh)
steps: Create 'ghost.txt' in the project and wait for it to appear in the tree
Delete it from disk and IMMEDIATELY click its (stale) tree entry before the watcher refresh removes it
Check app logs and the tab bar
items.expected: No editor tab is created; the openFile promise rejects (READ_FILE failure → 'Failed to open file' / editor.failedOpen) and the error is logged via console.error in openFile — app remains responsive, no renderer crash

automationHints.notes: Timing-sensitive: the watcher may remove the entry first; retry a few times. The error surfaces only in the console, not in UI — that's the current designed behavior

items.title: Saving to a locked/read-only file shows 'Save failed' error status
preconditions: Throwaway test project; a text file open in the editor with an unsaved edit
steps: From a shell, set the file read-only: attrib +R <file> (or open an exclusive lock)
Click .btn-editor-save in the editor
Read the status bar
Cleanup: attrib -R <file>, then save again to confirm recovery
items.expected: FILE_SAVED arrives with success:false; status bar shows the localized 'Save failed: <error>' with .editor-status.error class; content stays Modified; after clearing read-only, save succeeds and status recovers to Saved/Ready

automationHints.notes: Marked destructive because it flips file attributes on disk — cleanup step is mandatory. Windows fs.writeFile on +R file fails with EPERM

items.title: Expand and collapse a folder in the file tree via click
preconditions: Project with at least one folder containing a file is selected (e.g. src/sample.js)
Files panel visible and FILES section expanded
steps: Click the folder row (.file-item.folder) for 'src'
Observe arrow and children
Click the same folder row again
items.expected: First click rotates the folder arrow 90° and shows child items (.folder-children display:block); second click rotates the arrow back and hides the children

automationHints.notes: Verify via screenshot; ui_state has no per-selector mode

items.title: Filter auto-expands matching folders and a collapsed FILES section
preconditions: Project with file 'sample.js' inside collapsed folder 'src'
steps: Type 'sample' into #file-tree-filter-input

items.title: Edit → 'Paste Received Message' menu item and its CmdOrCtrl+Shift+Alt+V accelerator trigger the pending-paste flow (check double-binding)

items.title: View → Reload has NO Ctrl+R accelerator: Ctrl+R in a terminal does shell reverse-search, and the menu click reloads the renderer with terminal reattach

Clicking Reload calls webContents.reload(); the app re-initializes and terminals are restored via the persistence path.

automationHints.notes: Run on the DEV instance — the reload drops in-memory renderer state. Use app_wait_ready after clicking Reload. shortcuts-menus-037 covers item presence; this covers the no-accelerator regression and the reload action itself.

automationHints.notes: To force: move %APPDATA%/PATAPIM-dev/models/parakeet-v3 aside on the dev instance, restart, watch. Restore afterwards to avoid a 671MB re-download.

items.title: Hovering the mic button shows the 'Hold Ctrl+Alt to speak' tooltip
preconditions: PATAPIM window frontmost and unoccluded on its own display (real mouse reaches the app)
steps: Move the real mouse over the .btn-dictation-float button and hold it there
Observe the tooltip that appears above the button
Move the mouse away
items.expected: A custom DOM tooltip with text from t('terminal.holdCtrlAlt') (e.g. 'Hold Ctrl+Alt to speak') fades in above the button on mouseenter and is removed on mouseleave

automationHints.notes: Tooltip is a DOM child of the button (not a native title), so it IS visible in the CDP window screenshot once a real mouseenter fires.

items.title: Mic button idle state carries no state classes
steps: Inspect .btn-dictation-float classList while idle
items.expected: Only the base class is present — none of recording/processing/downloading/done/error; button is dimmed with the default border and no red/blue/green coloring

automationHints.notes: _updateDictationButton removes all five state classes before applying the current one.

items.title: Push-to-talk: hold Ctrl+Alt records, release stops and transcribes
steps: Hold Ctrl+Alt (no Shift, no Meta) and keep holding
Observe the mic button turn red (.recording)
Speak a short phrase, release the keys
Watch the button cycle processing → idle and the text land in the active terminal
items.expected: Recording starts on key-down hold (terminal indicator pulses), stops ~300ms after release (tail-end grace), transcribed text is written to the active terminal followed by Enter

items.title: Ctrl+Shift+D toggles persistent dictation mode on the active terminal
preconditions: PATAPIM window focused (keystrokes reach the app)
No other PATAPIM instance owns the shortcut
steps: Press Ctrl+Shift+D
Screenshot: mic button and active terminal indicator
Press Ctrl+Shift+D again to exit
items.expected: First press enters persistent mode: mic button gains .recording class (red), target terminal shows the recording indicator. Second press exits: recording stops, transcription (if any) is sent to the terminal, button returns to idle

automationHints.notes: Uses Cmd on macOS. On a first run this opens the first-run wizard instead of recording.

items.title: Mic probe timeout shows Retry and 'I'll set it up later' actions
preconditions: A condition where getUserMedia never resolves within 8s (e.g. OS permission prompt left unanswered on a fresh install)
steps: Trigger the first-run wizard and leave the OS mic prompt unanswered for >8s
Inspect the error block
Grant permission in the OS, then click 'Retry'
items.expected: After the 8s timeout the panel shows "Couldn't reach your microphone in time." with help text 'You can grant access and retry, or set this up later.', a 'Retry' button (re-runs the probe and renders the device picker on success) and an 'I'll set it up later' button (closes). The UI never freezes while waiting

items.title: Permission-denied state shows actionable error and 'Open System Settings' deep-link
preconditions: Microphone access for PATAPIM denied in OS privacy settings (Windows: Settings → Privacy & security → Microphone)
steps: Right-click the mic button to open the setup panel (or trigger the first-run wizard)
Inspect the error block
Click 'Open System Settings'
Also attempt a push-to-talk recording and note the error toast
items.expected: Error block shows 'Microphone access denied. Please allow microphone access in your system settings.' plus the per-OS help line ('Open Settings → Privacy & security → Microphone and enable PATAPIM.' on Windows); the button opens ms-settings:privacy-microphone (x-apple.systempreferences… on macOS). A recording attempt surfaces 'Microphone access denied. Enable it in your system privacy settings, then try again.'

items.title: Worker-error block offers 'Run diagnostic' (5-step results) and 'Copy diagnostic'
preconditions: A dictation worker error has been surfaced (e.g. kill the patapim-parakeet utility process mid-transcription, or corrupt the model dir on a dev profile)
steps: Right-click the mic button — the setup panel should show the human-readable error (e.g. 'Dictation worker crashed unexpectedly.')
Click 'Run diagnostic' — button shows 'Running…' while disabled
Inspect the inline results list
Click 'Copy diagnostic' and inspect the clipboard
items.expected: Diagnostic renders ✓/✗ lines for steps: sherpa-binaries, utility-process, model, worker-init, transcribe (silent end-to-end). Copy diagnostic puts a JSON payload (time/platform/userAgent/errors) on the clipboard and flashes 'Copied' for 1.5s. Error-code mapping covers SHERPA_BINARY_MISSING / SHERPA_LOAD_FAILED / MODEL_DOWNLOAD_FAILED / WORKER_CRASHED / WORKER_FORK_FAILED / WORKER_PREMATURE_EXIT

automationHints.notes: Inject the failure on the dev instance only. A later healthy recording clears _lastDictationError, so run this check before recovering.

items.title: No-microphone state shows the 'No microphones found' message
preconditions: All audio input devices disabled/unplugged (e.g. disable mics in Device Manager on a test machine/VM)
steps: Right-click the mic button to open the setup panel
Inspect the body
items.expected: Error block reads 'No microphones found. Please connect a microphone.' instead of the device picker; no VU meter or Save button rendered

items.title: 'Mute sound while I'm speaking' defaults on, mutes system audio during recording, and persists when toggled
preconditions: Audio playing from another app (e.g. music)
steps: On a fresh profile open the Dictation menu — verify the mute checkbox is checked by default
Hold Ctrl+Alt: verify system audio mutes while recording
Release: verify audio restores immediately on release (not after transcription finishes)
Uncheck the menu item, record again — audio keeps playing
Restart the app and re-open the menu — verify the unchecked state persisted
items.expected: Default ON (no localStorage value). While enabled, recording start fires MUTE_SYSTEM_AUDIO and stop fires UNMUTE immediately. Toggling writes 'dictation-mute-while-speaking' ('1'/'0') and survives restart. If recording start fails after muting, audio is restored

items.title: Selected microphone persists across app restart and is checkmarked in the Dictation menu
preconditions: A non-default mic selected and saved (see dictation-029)
steps: Restart PATAPIM
Verify localStorage 'dictation-device-id' still holds the device id
Open the titlebar Dictation menu and inspect the Microphone section
items.expected: After restart the saved device is still selected: the Dictation menu shows the checkbox checked on that device (and 'System Default' unchecked); recordings bind to it

automationHints.notes: Native menu requires a real-desktop screenshot.

items.title: End-to-end transcription accuracy: spoken phrase lands in the active terminal and in history
preconditions: Active Claude terminal at an input prompt
steps: Hold Ctrl+Alt, clearly say 'list the files in the current directory', release
Watch the terminal input
Right-click the mic button and check the history panel
items.expected: The recognized text (reasonably accurate, whitespace-normalized) is written to the active terminal followed by Enter (submits). The same text is appended as the newest entry in transcription history

items.title: 5 seconds of silence during recording pops the silence panel with the wrong-mic warning
preconditions: Mic muted at hardware/OS level or covered so RMS stays below threshold
steps: Start persistent dictation
Produce no audio for 5+ seconds
Inspect the floating panel that appears
items.expected: The compact mic-setup panel appears (silence reason) with a yellow warning 'No audio detected. Wrong microphone?', the VU meter, the device selector, and a 'Sounds good!' confirm button

items.title: Failed transcription failsafe: retry once, then save the raw audio to disk
preconditions: Ability to break the worker persistently (e.g. temporarily rename the sherpa native dir AFTER model init so both transcribe attempts fail)
steps: Break the transcription path so both attempts fail
Record a short phrase via push-to-talk and release
Watch the state messages
Check <userData>/failed-recordings/ for a new .webm file
Cleanup: restore the broken component and delete the test .webm
items.expected: First failure shows 'Transcription hiccup — retrying...'; after the second failure the audio is persisted to <userData>/failed-recordings/<timestamp>.webm and the error reads 'Transcription failed (…) — your recording was saved and can be recovered.'

items.title: Persistent mode: clicking another terminal flushes the transcript and switches the recording target
steps: Click the mic button to enter persistent mode targeting terminal A
Speak phrase 1
Click terminal B's tab/pane
Speak phrase 2
Click the mic button to exit persistent mode
items.expected: Switching targets stops the current recording and delivers phrase 1 to terminal A; a new recording starts targeting B (indicator moves); exiting delivers phrase 2 to terminal B. Any other click during persistent mode exits dictation

items.title: Worker crash self-heals: the next dictation respawns the worker and succeeds
preconditions: Model ready and at least one successful dictation done
steps: Kill the 'patapim-parakeet' utility process from Task Manager (or wait for a natural crash)
Immediately dictate a short phrase via push-to-talk
items.expected: The transcription succeeds (possibly with one visible 'Transcription hiccup — retrying...' message): parakeetService._ensureReady respawns and re-inits the worker transparently. No app restart needed; logs show the respawn

items.title: Long dictation (>512 chars) is delivered in paced chunks with a separate final Enter
steps: Dictate continuously for ~60s so the transcript exceeds 512 characters
Release and watch the terminal
items.expected: The full transcript arrives without truncation (sent as paced 512-byte chunks), then Enter is sent as a separate keypress ~200ms later so paste-detection sees a clean submit. No fragment-only delivery

items.title: Model download failure surfaces an error state and recovers when network returns
preconditions: Dev instance with the parakeet model dir removed
steps: Disconnect the network and restart the dev instance
Wait ~3s for the background preload and observe the mic button / messages
Reconnect the network and click the mic button to retry
items.expected: Offline: preload fails with 'Model download failed: …' and the button shows the error state. Online retry: toggle re-initializes ('Loading model...' / download progress) and dictation becomes usable without restart

items.title: Recording with a saved-but-missing mic falls back to the default device
preconditions: A removable mic (e.g. USB) saved as the dictation device, then unplugged
Another working mic present as system default
steps: Unplug the saved mic
Hold Ctrl+Alt and speak, release
Check localStorage 'dictation-device-id'
items.expected: Recording does not fail: the OverconstrainedError triggers a silent fallback to the default mic, the saved device id is cleared from localStorage, and the transcription completes

items.title: Right-click the mic button opens the Mic Setup panel and the Transcription History panel together
preconditions: PATAPIM window frontmost and unoccluded (real right-click reaches the app)
steps: Right-click the .btn-dictation-float button with the real mouse
Wait up to 2s for device enumeration
Screenshot the lower-right of the window
items.expected: Both panels appear stacked: .mic-setup-panel-float (Microphone Setup) anchored bottom-right, with .transcription-history-panel positioned above it

automationHints.notes: ui_click only fires left-click (would toggle dictation); a real contextmenu event via computer_* is required. Toggle behavior: right-clicking again while panels are open closes both.

items.title: 'Clear history' asks for confirmation and only clears on accept
preconditions: Run on dev instance / disposable profile — cleared history is unrecoverable
steps: Click the 'Clear history' button in the panel footer
Verify a confirm dialog appears with text 'Clear all transcription history?'
Click Cancel — verify entries remain
Click 'Clear history' again and accept
Verify the empty-state message appears and the footer hides
Cleanup: none possible (history is gone) — dictate one test phrase to confirm new entries still append
items.expected: Cancel preserves all entries; accept clears the store (TRANSCRIPTION_HISTORY_CLEAR), shows 'No transcriptions yet — start dictating to build history.' and hides the Clear button

automationHints.notes: Uses a native window.confirm — not visible in CDP screenshot; needs computer_screenshot or human eyes for the dialog itself.

items.title: 'click to copy all' copies the whole history and flashes 'Copied!'
steps: Click the 'click to copy all' hint in the history header
Inspect the hint text and the clipboard
items.expected: Clipboard contains all loaded entries joined by blank lines; hint text changes to 'Copied!' in green for ~1.2s then reverts to 'click to copy all'. Enter/Space on the focused hint does the same

automationHints.notes: Overwrites the user clipboard — save/restore clipboard contents around the test.

items.title: Changing the mic dropdown saves the device and restarts the VU preview on it
steps: Select a different device in .mic-setup-select
Check localStorage key 'dictation-device-id'
Speak into the newly selected mic and watch the VU bar
items.expected: Selection is persisted to localStorage immediately on change (no Save needed); the level preview stops and restarts bound to the new deviceId; VU responds to the new mic

automationHints.notes: Selecting the empty 'System Default' option removes the localStorage key. VU response needs a human ear/mic.

items.title: Clicking outside both panels dismisses them
preconditions: Both panels open via right-click (see dictation-003)
steps: Click (mousedown) somewhere in the terminal area away from both panels and away from the mic button
items.expected: Both panels close. Clicks INSIDE either panel, on the mic button, or on a first-run overlay do NOT close them

automationHints.notes: Handler is a capture-phase document mousedown listener; also verify a click inside the panel does not dismiss.

items.title: Escape dismisses both panels
preconditions: Both panels open via right-click (see dictation-003)
steps: Press Escape
items.expected: Both panels close and the document-level keydown/mousedown dismiss handlers are removed (pressing Escape again has no further effect)

automationHints.notes: Escape works even while device enumeration is still in flight (handlers installed before the async show).

items.title: History panel header shows 'Transcription history' title and 'click to copy all' hint
preconditions: Both panels open via right-click (see dictation-003)
steps: Inspect the .transcription-history-panel header
items.expected: Title 'Transcription history' with clipboard icon on the left; 'click to copy all' hint (role=button, tabbable) on the right; × close control at the far right

items.title: History panel X closes both panels
preconditions: Both panels open via right-click (see dictation-003)
steps: Click the × (.transcription-history-close) in the History panel header
items.expected: Both the History panel and the Mic Setup panel close (they open and close as a pair)

automationHints.notes: TranscriptionHistoryPanel.hide() fires onHide which multiTerminalUI wires to _closeMicPanels.

items.title: Mic Setup panel shows title, device dropdown, live VU meter, and Save button
preconditions: Mic Setup panel open via right-click (see dictation-003)
steps: Inspect the panel header and body
Stay silent and watch the VU label
Speak; watch the VU bar and label change
items.expected: Header reads 'Microphone Setup'; body has a select with first option 'System Default' plus enumerated devices; VU track (.mic-setup-vu-track) shows label 'Say something...' then 'No signal' when silent and 'Receiving audio' (green) when audio detected; a 'Save' button (manual mode) is at the bottom

automationHints.notes: Agent can verify structure; only a human can confirm the VU bar actually tracks live audio. Manual reason shows common.save 'Save'; silence reason shows 'Sounds good!'.

items.title: Mic Setup panel X button dismisses both panels
preconditions: Both panels open via right-click (see dictation-003)
steps: Click the × (.mic-setup-close) in the Mic Setup panel header
Screenshot the area
items.expected: Both the Mic Setup panel and the Transcription History panel animate out and are removed from the DOM; level preview (mic stream) is released

automationHints.notes: Panels were opened together by _onDictationConfigure and must close together (onHide wiring).

items.title: Save on the setup panel shows the '{mic} assigned' confirmation and auto-closes
preconditions: Mic Setup panel open in manual mode (right-click)
steps: Pick a device in the dropdown
Click 'Save'
Watch the panel body
items.expected: Body swaps to an animated green check (circle + tick draw-in) with text '<device label> assigned'; after ~1.25s the panel closes. The device id is persisted

automationHints.notes: If 'System Default' is selected the confirmation text uses 'System Default' as the name.

items.title: Clicking a single history entry copies just that entry with a green highlight
steps: Hover an entry — verify the tooltip 'click to copy this transcription'
Click the entry
Inspect the clipboard, the entry styling, and the header hint
items.expected: Clipboard contains only that entry's text; the entry flashes a green .is-copied background for ~0.6s; the header hint flashes 'Copied!'. Enter/Space on a keyboard-focused entry behaves the same

automationHints.notes: Save/restore the user clipboard around the test.

items.title: History pagination: scrolling to the top loads 50 older entries with anchored scroll
steps: Verify the panel opened scrolled to the bottom (newest visible)
Scroll to the very top of the list
Watch the loader and the list
items.expected: 'Loading older…' appears briefly; up to 50 older entries are prepended; the scroll position is anchored so the entry being viewed stays in place; when no more entries exist the loader stops appearing

automationHints.notes: Seed >50 entries by appending to the history store on a dev profile before the test.

items.title: History panel shows the empty-state message when no transcriptions exist
preconditions: Fresh profile or cleared transcription history
steps: Open the panels with an empty history
Inspect the history body and footer
items.expected: Body shows 'No transcriptions yet — start dictating to build history.' in italic; the footer with the 'Clear history' button is hidden (display:none)

automationHints.notes: Use the dev instance with a clean userData to get an empty history without destroying real data.

items.title: WhatsApp voice note to the bot is transcribed by Parakeet and routed to Claude
preconditions: WhatsApp bot connected and linked to a terminal
steps: From the linked WhatsApp chat, send a short voice note (under the max duration)
Watch the linked terminal and the bot's reply
items.expected: The audio is extracted, converted and transcribed via parakeetService; the terminal receives '[Voice message]: <transcribed text>' and Claude responds to it. Empty/failed transcriptions produce a graceful bot error, not a crash

items.title: Drag the split handle to resize terminal/browser panes
preconditions: Browser panel open with a page loaded (split view)
steps: Locate '.browser-split-handle' between terminal and panel
Drag it ~200px left, release
Drag it ~300px right, release
items.expected: Terminal and browser widths follow the drag live; after release the WebContentsView bounds match the new viewport exactly (no overlap onto the terminal, no gap); the split ratio persists across a tab switch within the session.

automationHints.notes: Native view bounds only update via _updateBrowserBounds — verify alignment with computer_screenshot, not app_screenshot.

items.title: Panel repaints correctly after editor-tab round trip (hidden to visible)
preconditions: Browser panel open with a page loaded
An editor tab open in the same project
steps: Switch to the editor tab (all browsers hidden)
Switch back to the terminal tab
Inspect the panel content area
items.expected: The page is fully painted at the correct position/size — not blank, not stale-positioned (show() detects the hidden→visible transition and kicks a repaint: bounds bump + invalidate + 80ms second nudge).

automationHints.notes: This targets the Chromium-on-Windows dropped-first-composite bug; repeat the round trip a few times to catch the intermittent case.

items.title: App zoom (Ctrl+= / Ctrl+- / Ctrl+0) keeps the view aligned with its container
preconditions: Browser panel open with a page loaded
steps: Press Ctrl+= twice (zoom in)
Inspect alignment of the page vs the panel frame
Press Ctrl+0 (reset)
items.expected: After each zoom change the WebContentsView is rescaled to the panel's CSS rect (reapplyBoundsForZoom multiplies cached CSS bounds by zoomFactor) — the page must not drift away from or overlap outside its container.

automationHints.notes: DIP-vs-CSS-pixel mismatch is the bug class here; misalignment is obvious in a desktop screenshot.

items.title: Renderer crash auto-reloads the page instead of going blank
preconditions: Ability to kill the view's renderer process (Task Manager: find the Electron child process for the page, or Process Explorer)
steps: Identify the embedded page's renderer process and kill it
Watch the panel for ~2 seconds
items.expected: Main log shows '[BrowserManager] Renderer crashed for <id>' then 'Auto-reloading ... after crash'; after ~250ms the same URL reloads and content reappears. The panel must NOT silently swap to about:blank on first crash.

items.title: Crash-loop guard falls back to about:blank on rapid repeat crash
preconditions: Same setup as the auto-reload crash test
steps: Kill the embedded renderer process
Within 15 seconds of the auto-reload, kill it again
items.expected: Second crash within the 15s window does NOT trigger another reload: log shows 'Crash loop or no URL for <id> — loading about:blank' and the view loads about:blank, breaking the loop. The app itself stays responsive.

items.title: Closing the floating window auto-reattaches the browser
preconditions: Browser detached in a floating window
steps: Close the floating window with its OS titlebar X
items.expected: The 'closed' handler re-adds the view to the main window and notifies the renderer (detached:false); the panel can be shown again with the page intact; no crash, no zombie WebContentsView.

automationHints.notes: Watch for the z-order shuffle side effect seen in the seed run when the floating window closes.

items.title: Detach browser to a floating window and reattach via panel toggle
preconditions: Browser panel open with https://example.com loaded
steps: Click '.browser-detach-btn'
Verify the floating window on the desktop (computer_screenshot)
Click '.btn-browser-toggle' in the main window (reattaches then closes the panel)
Reopen the panel
items.expected: A separate floating window opens with its own toolbar (back/forward/refresh/URL bar showing https://example.com/) rendering the page; main-window terminal expands. Toggling the panel off reattaches (no orphan floating window). Reopening the panel restores the same browser still on example.com.

automationHints.notes: Seed finding (medium): while detached, the main-window panel DOM (including .browser-detach-btn / .browser-reattach-btn) was removed, so in-main-window reattach selectors were unreachable. Closing the floating window also shuffled background window z-order.

items.title: Detached floating window renders content immediately (no black view)
preconditions: Browser panel with a page loaded
steps: Click '.browser-detach-btn'
Immediately look at the floating window content area
Resize the floating window
items.expected: The page is visible as soon as the window shows (kickRepaint on ready-to-show + 80ms safety nudge) — not a black rectangle. On resize, the view tracks the window (positioned below the 44px toolbar strip) with no gaps or overlap.

items.title: Detached overlay with Reattach button appears in the main-window viewport
preconditions: Browser panel open with a page loaded
Terminal tab stays active after detaching
steps: Click '.browser-detach-btn'
Inspect the main window's browser viewport area
items.expected: Per code, the panel's viewport shows '.browser-detached-overlay' with text 'Browser is detached in a floating window' ('browser.detachedOverlay') and a '.browser-reattach-btn' labeled 'Reattach' ('browser.reattachButton'); the detach button's tooltip flips to 'Reattach browser'. Clicking the overlay's Reattach button reattaches. NOTE: seed run found the overlay/panel REMOVED from the main DOM while detached (medium finding) — verify whether the overlay is actually reachable.

automationHints.notes: If the split view collapses on detach (state.browser.detached → no split rendered), this is the known UX gap from BRP-11; record as FAIL/finding rather than blocked.

items.title: Reattach via the floating window's own Reattach button
preconditions: Browser detached in a floating window
steps: In the floating window's toolbar, click the '#reattach' button (tooltip 'Reattach to main window')
items.expected: Floating window closes; the view is re-added to the main window (hidden) and the renderer is notified (BROWSER_DETACHED detached:false); reopening/showing the panel displays the same page. No orphan window.

automationHints.notes: The floating toolbar is a separate BrowserWindow — ui_click targets the main window only; use real mouse on the floating window.

items.title: Floating toolbar stays in sync: URL bar, loading state, window title
steps: In the floating window's URL bar ('#url'), type 'https://example.org' and press Enter (real keyboard)
Use '#back' / '#forward' / '#refresh' buttons
Observe the window title
items.expected: Navigation works from the floating toolbar; its URL bar updates on every navigation (browser:url-changed forwarded to the floating window); window title follows the page title (page-title-updated → setTitle); loading spinner reflects did-start/stop-loading.

items.title: Device dropdown lists presets and applies iPhone 15 emulation
steps: Click '.browser-device-btn'
Verify dropdown contents
Click the 'iPhone 15' row (NOTE: it is :nth-child(4) — child 1 is Desktop, child 2 is the separator div, child 3 is iPhone SE)
items.expected: Dropdown lists Desktop, then iPhone SE 375x667, iPhone 15 393x852, iPhone 15 Pro Max 430x932, Pixel 8 412x915, Galaxy S24 360x780, iPad Mini 768x1024, iPad Pro 12.9" 1024x1366. Selecting iPhone 15: viewport shows a centered 393x852 portrait frame with the page reflowed mobile-width; '.browser-device-btn' gets 'active' class; '.browser-rotate-btn' appears.

automationHints.notes: Dropdown is visible in app_screenshot because the WebContentsView is hidden while it is open (BROWSER_HIDE). Do not use :nth-child(3) for iPhone 15 — that's iPhone SE.

items.title: Rotate button toggles portrait/landscape
steps: Click '.browser-rotate-btn'
Verify the frame
Click it again to return to portrait
items.expected: Frame rotates to landscape (~852 wide x 393 tall), content reflows wider, device button stays active. Second click returns to portrait. Frame label updates dimensions accordingly.

items.title: Selecting 'Desktop' clears device emulation
steps: Click '.browser-device-btn'
Click the 'Desktop' item ('.browser-device-item:first-child')
items.expected: Device frame removed, page returns to full panel width, '.browser-device-btn' loses 'active' class, '.browser-rotate-btn' hidden, '.device-frame-label' removed; the CDP debugger is detached and the page reloads with the desktop UA.

items.title: Device dropdown dismisses on outside click and restores the view
preconditions: Browser panel with a page loaded
steps: Click '.browser-device-btn' to open the dropdown (this hides the WebContentsView)
Click elsewhere in the app (e.g. the terminal area)
Observe the viewport
items.expected: Dropdown closes on the outside click; the WebContentsView reappears at its correct bounds (onDeviceChange triggers a bounds recalculation) — the page must not stay hidden/blank.

automationHints.notes: Pressing Escape should also dismiss (attachOutsideDismiss). Verify page visible again via computer_screenshot.

items.title: Device emulation overrides the user agent (page reloads with mobile UA)
steps: Navigate to https://httpbin.org/user-agent via the URL bar + Go
Apply iPhone 15 via the device dropdown
Read the page after the automatic reload
items.expected: After applying the device the page reloads automatically and reports an iPhone Safari UA (per the preset's userAgent), not the desktop Chrome UA. Clearing to Desktop reloads back to the desktop UA.

automationHints.notes: setDevice sends Emulation.setUserAgentOverride + setTouchEmulationEnabled then reload().

items.title: Device frame label shows device name and current dimensions
steps: Inspect the area below the device frame in the viewport
items.expected: '.device-frame-label' reads 'iPhone 15 — 393×852' in portrait and 'iPhone 15 — 852×393' after rotating.

automationHints.notes: Label is DOM (visible in app_screenshot).

items.title: File downloads from the embedded browser auto-save to ~/.patapim/downloads
steps: Navigate the panel to a direct file URL (e.g. https://raw.githubusercontent.com/git/git/master/README.md or any small downloadable file that triggers a download)
Check ~/.patapim/downloads on disk
Call the MCP tool browser_downloads
Download the same file again
items.expected: File lands in ~/.patapim/downloads with its original name; browser_downloads lists it with state 'completed', source terminalId, url, byte counts. Re-downloading the same filename saves as 'name (1).ext' (unique-suffix logic). History capped at 50 entries.

automationHints.notes: Per-partition sessions get their own will-download listener; verify the terminal browser's persist:browser-shared partition is attached.

items.title: F12 opens the terminal's current target (dev server or last touched file) in the panel
