steps: Focus the terminal body
Press F12 (no modifiers)
items.expected: The browser panel auto-enables if needed and navigates to the resolved target (dev-server URL preferred, else the last file Claude touched). A toast '.patapim-f12-toast' may appear during resolution. The xterm keyboard handler passes F12 through to the app.

automationHints.notes: RESOLVE_TERMINAL_TARGET in main; openFileInBrowser handles enable+create+navigate. Seed never covered F12.

items.title: F12 with no resolvable target shows an informative toast
preconditions: A fresh terminal where Claude has not edited any file and no dev server is running
steps: Focus the terminal body
Press F12
items.expected: A toast appears: 'No hay archivo siendo editado en esta terminal' (or 'Este archivo no es texto/HTML (.<ext>)' for unsupported extensions). No panel opens, no error. FINDING TO VERIFY: these toast strings are hardcoded in Spanish in index.js, not localized via i18n — flag if the app locale is English.

automationHints.notes: Toast auto-fades; screenshot quickly after the keypress.

items.title: Typing in an embedded page form is not interrupted by terminal focus reclaim
preconditions: Browser panel open on a page with a text input (e.g. a search engine homepage)
steps: Click into the page's text input (real mouse)
Type a sentence at normal speed while the terminal has activity (e.g. a command producing output)
Trigger a navigation in another browser/terminal meanwhile if possible
items.expected: Focus stays in the form field for the entire typing session — no characters lost to the terminal and no mid-word focus yank (form-focus tracking via browserContentPreload blocks the nav focus-reclaim while an editable field is focused).

items.title: Toolbar navigation returns keyboard focus to the terminal (browser pane not active)
preconditions: User last clicked in the terminal (browser pane NOT the active pane)
steps: Click in the terminal to make it the active pane
Use ui_fill + Go to navigate the panel to a new URL
Immediately type a character
items.expected: Within ~30ms of the navigation, OS focus is reclaimed for the main window and the typed character lands in the terminal — Chromium's focus steal on loadURL is countered (withFocusPreservation / _reclaimFocusAfterNav). Conversely, if the user had last clicked INSIDE the browser page, focus must stay with the browser.

items.title: Switch dropdown lists all live browsers including MCP ones
preconditions: Browser panel open in a terminal
At least one MCP named browser created (browser_navigate with browser_id 'qa-test')
steps: Create an MCP browser via the MCP tool browser_navigate (browser_id 'qa-test', url https://example.org)
Click '.browser-switch-btn' in the panel toolbar
items.expected: '.browser-switch-dropdown' lists the terminal's own browser (labeled with terminal name) and an entry 'MCP: qa-test' with its URL/title as detail (truncated at 50 chars); the currently shown browser has the 'active' class.

automationHints.notes: Dropdown is DOM and the view is hidden while open, so app_screenshot captures it.

items.title: Switching to an MCP browser overrides the panel content
preconditions: Switch dropdown open with an MCP browser listed (from previous item)
steps: Click the 'MCP: qa-test' item
Observe URL bar and page
Open the dropdown again and switch back to the terminal's own browser
items.expected: Panel now shows the MCP browser's page and URL (override set; URL/title pushed immediately); '.browser-switch-btn' gains the 'overridden' class. Switching back restores the terminal's own browser state and removes 'overridden'.

items.title: Closing the panel while an MCP override is active preserves the MCP browser
preconditions: Panel showing an MCP browser via switch override
steps: Click '.browser-close-btn'
Via MCP, call browser_list (or browser_info with the browser_id)
items.expected: Panel closes; the MCP browser is NOT destroyed — it is moved offscreen and still appears in browser_list with its URL intact; only the override mapping is cleared.

automationHints.notes: close() short-circuits when browserOverrides has the terminal: clears override, sets MCP view to offscreen bounds.

items.title: Navigate via URL bar + Go button
steps: Fill '.browser-url-input' with 'https://example.com'
Click '.browser-go-btn'
Verify page content with a desktop screenshot
Fill 'https://example.org' and click Go again
items.expected: example.com loads (page content visible, 'Example Domain'), URL bar shows https://example.com/, placeholder hidden, no error overlay. Second navigation loads example.org and URL bar updates.

automationHints.notes: Go button is the reliable automation path; page content only visible via computer_screenshot.

items.title: Back button navigates to previous history entry
preconditions: Browser panel with history: example.com then example.org
steps: Click '[data-action="back"]'
items.expected: URL bar reverts to https://example.com/ and the page renders. With no history (fresh panel), clicking Back is a silent no-op (canGoBack guard) — no error.

automationHints.notes: Also try Back on a freshly opened panel before any navigation to cover the no-history guard.

items.title: Bare hostname is normalized to https://
steps: Fill '.browser-url-input' with 'example.com'
Click '.browser-go-btn'
items.expected: Navigates to https://example.com/ (https prepended for bare hostnames); URL bar shows the normalized URL after load.

items.title: file://, local paths, and restricted protocols are refused
steps: Fill '.browser-url-input' with 'file:///C:/Windows/win.ini' and click Go
Repeat with 'C:\\Windows\\win.ini'
Repeat with 'javascript:alert(1)'
Repeat with 'chrome://settings'
items.expected: None of them load: the view stays on its previous content (or placeholder), no file content is ever rendered. Main-process log shows '[BrowserManager] navigate refused: file:// blocked' / 'local path blocked' / 'restricted protocol blocked'. This is the prompt-injection guard — a load of local file content is a security FAIL.

automationHints.notes: Check app_logs for the 'navigate refused' warnings.

items.title: Forward button re-navigates after going back
preconditions: Browser panel on example.com after going back from example.org
steps: Click '[data-action="forward"]'
Cleanup: click Back to return to example.com
items.expected: URL bar shows https://example.org/ and the page renders. No error overlay.

items.title: Load-error overlay on unreachable host, cleared by next navigation
steps: Fill '.browser-url-input' with 'https://nonexistent-domain-patapim-qa.invalid' and click Go
Observe the viewport
Fill 'https://example.com' and click Go
items.expected: Error overlay '.browser-error' appears with title 'Failed to load page' ('browser.errorTitle'), the failed URL, and the Chromium error description (e.g. ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED). After the second (successful) navigation the error element is removed and the page renders.

automationHints.notes: The error overlay is DOM, so app_screenshot CAN capture it (the failed view paints nothing over it). _navigate() removes .browser-error before sending the new navigation.

items.title: localhost / 127.0.0.1 are normalized to http://
preconditions: Optionally a local server on port 8000 (python -m http.server) for a positive load
steps: Fill '.browser-url-input' with 'localhost:8000'
Click Go
items.expected: Both navigate to http://localhost:8000/ and http://127.0.0.1:8000/ (http prepended, not https). If no server is running, the load-error overlay appears with the normalized http:// URL — that still proves correct normalization.

items.title: Navigate via URL bar + Enter key (real keyboard)
preconditions: A human at the real keyboard (automation keystrokes were swallowed by the native WebContentsView overlay in the seed run)
steps: Click into '.browser-url-input'
Type 'https://example.com'
Press Enter
items.expected: Page loads exactly as with the Go button; the input blurs before navigation (code blurs on Enter to avoid the focus-routing edge case). URL bar shows https://example.com/.

items.title: Refresh button reloads the current page
steps: Click '.browser-refresh-btn'
items.expected: Page reloads; URL bar unchanged (https://example.com/); refresh button gets a transient 'loading' class during the reload and is NOT stuck in loading afterwards; no error overlay.

automationHints.notes: The loading class is too fast to capture on example.com; use a slow page (e.g. https://httpbin.org/delay/3) to assert the transient state.

items.title: target=_blank link navigates in place instead of opening a window
preconditions: Browser panel open with a page containing a target=_blank link (e.g. https://example.com 'Learn more' is same-tab; use a known test page such as https://www.w3.org/ links or a local fixture with <a target="_blank">)
steps: Load a page with a target=_blank link
Click the link inside the embedded page (real mouse click on the view)
items.expected: No new window opens; the embedded view navigates in place to the link URL and the panel URL bar updates.

automationHints.notes: Clicks inside the native view need computer_* (no DOM there). setWindowOpenHandler denies and loadURL's in place.

items.title: window.close() from embedded page must not close the PATAPIM main window
preconditions: An MCP named browser exists (same _blockWindowClose path as terminal browsers)
steps: Create/use an MCP browser: browser_navigate to https://example.com with a browser_id
Run browser_evaluate with 'window.close()'
Check the main app is still alive (app_info)
items.expected: PATAPIM main window stays open; main log may show '[BrowserManager] Blocked webContents.close()'. window.close is overridden to a no-op on did-start-loading/did-frame-navigate/dom-ready, with a close-event preventDefault as last resort.

automationHints.notes: Without the guard, window.close() in a WebContentsView closes the owner BrowserWindow (the whole app).

items.title: window.open() popup (OAuth-style) opens a real 500x600 BrowserWindow
preconditions: Browser panel open on a page that calls window.open with features (e.g. a Google/Firebase sign-in button)
steps: Trigger a window.open popup from the embedded page (e.g. click a 'Sign in with Google' button on a test site)
Observe the desktop
items.expected: A separate small popup window (~500x600, no menu bar) opens and renders the popup content; the main panel stays put. Closing the popup returns to the page without breaking the panel.

items.title: URL input selects all text on focus
preconditions: Browser panel with a URL already in the bar
steps: Click into '.browser-url-input'
Type a new URL without manually clearing
items.expected: Existing text is selected on focus, so typing replaces the whole URL instead of appending.

items.title: Toggle browser panel open and closed via toolbar globe button
preconditions: A project is open with at least one terminal
A terminal tab (not an editor tab) is active
steps: Click '.btn-browser-toggle' (globe icon in the terminal tab bar toolbar)
Observe the split view
Click '.btn-browser-toggle' again
items.expected: First click: split view renders — terminal left, browser panel right with toolbar (back/forward/refresh/device/switch buttons, URL input with placeholder 'Enter URL (e.g., localhost:3000)', go/pin/detach/close) and placeholder 'Enter a URL to preview your website' + hint 'Try localhost:3000 or any URL'. Second click: panel removed, terminal expands to full width.

automationHints.notes: app_screenshot does NOT capture the native WebContentsView overlay — placeholder/toolbar (DOM) are visible in it, loaded page content is not. Use computer_screenshot to see page content.

items.title: Browser panel state is independent per terminal
preconditions: Two terminal tabs in the same project
Browser panel open with a page loaded in terminal A; terminal B has no panel
steps: Switch to terminal B's tab
Observe the content area
Switch back to terminal A's tab
items.expected: Terminal B shows full-width terminal (no browser, no leftover WebContentsView painted over it). Terminal A's split view returns with its page intact and correctly positioned (bounds recomputed after hidden-to-visible).

automationHints.notes: show() applies a repaint kick on hidden→visible transitions — watch for a blank view on return to A.

items.title: Close panel via the X (Close browser) button
preconditions: Browser panel open with a page loaded
steps: Click '.browser-close-btn' (X icon, tooltip 'Close browser')
items.expected: Browser panel removed from DOM, terminal expands to full width, '.btn-browser-toggle' loses its active state, no error overlays, no orphaned WebContentsView visible.

automationHints.notes: Close button calls toggleBrowserPanel() via onClose callback.

items.title: Reopening the panel restores the last loaded URL
preconditions: Browser panel open with https://example.com loaded
steps: Click '.browser-close-btn' to close the panel
Click '.btn-browser-toggle' to reopen
items.expected: Panel reopens showing https://example.com/ in the URL bar with the page still loaded (the WebContentsView instance survives close/reopen within a session); placeholder stays hidden.

automationHints.notes: Seed BRP-11/12 observed restore-after-reopen behavior. Verify URL bar value via app_screenshot (DOM) and page content via computer_screenshot.

items.title: Toggle browser panel via Ctrl+Shift+B keyboard shortcut
preconditions: Keyboard focus is inside the xterm terminal body (click it first) — the shortcut does not fire from the file tree
steps: Click into the terminal body to focus it
Press Ctrl+Shift+B
Observe the panel
Press Ctrl+Shift+B again to close
items.expected: Panel opens (split view + placeholder) and closes via the shortcut. Note: Ctrl+B WITHOUT Shift toggles the sidebar instead — verify it does not open the browser.

automationHints.notes: Seed run confirmed the shortcut is focus-sensitive: it only works when terminal/main content has keyboard focus. Requires Computer Control ON for computer_key.

items.title: Toggle browser while an editor tab is active produces no visible panel
preconditions: A project with one terminal and one open editor tab
The editor tab is the active view
steps: With the editor tab showing, click '.btn-browser-toggle'
Observe the content area
Activate the terminal tab
items.expected: While the editor is active no browser panel renders (editor mode hides all browsers). After switching to the terminal tab, the split view with the browser panel appears (the toggled-on state takes effect). No blank/ghost panel over the editor.

automationHints.notes: Seed env note: panel is per-terminal and only renders inside the terminal split view; _renderTabView calls hideAllBrowsers() in editor mode.

items.title: Login/session state persists across app restart (shared partition)
steps: Navigate to a site that sets persistent state (e.g. log into github.com, or set a long-lived cookie via any site login)
Restart the app
Reopen a browser panel in any terminal and navigate to the same site
items.expected: The session survives the restart: still logged in / cookie present. All terminal panels share one persistent partition ('persist:browser-shared') by design, so the login is visible from ANY terminal's panel, not just the original one.

items.title: Left-click pin toggles the project pin and button state
preconditions: Browser panel open with a URL loaded in a project
steps: Left-click '.browser-pin-btn'
Inspect the button
Left-click it again
items.expected: First click: button gains 'active' class and its tooltip changes to 'Unpin' ('browser.unpin'); pin (terminalId+url) is saved for the current project. Second click: pin removed, tooltip back to 'Pin browser for this project' ('browser.pin'), 'active' removed.

automationHints.notes: Pin send IPC BROWSER_PIN_PROJECT { projectPath, terminalId, url }. Selecting 'Pin on this project only' in the right-click dropdown does the same toggle.

items.title: Pin state persists across app restart
preconditions: A browser pinned for the current project with a URL
steps: Pin a browser with https://example.com loaded
Restart the app (dev instance: ./rebuild-dev-safe.bat, then app_wait_ready)
Reopen the same project and a terminal with a browser panel
items.expected: Pin state is reloaded from disk for the project (BROWSER_GET_PIN_STATE): the pin button shows active on the corresponding terminal's panel and the pinned URL is associated with the project.

automationHints.notes: Note: terminalIds reset per launch ('term-N' counter), so verify how the pin maps to a terminal after restart — possible staleness edge worth a human eye.

items.title: Pinned browser takes precedence in other terminals of the same project
preconditions: Two terminals in the same project
Terminal A's browser pinned (with a page loaded)
Terminal B has no browser of its own
steps: Pin terminal A's browser (left-click pin)
Switch to terminal B's tab
items.expected: Terminal B's view renders as a split with terminal B on the left and terminal A's PINNED browser panel on the right (pinned browser wins over the active terminal's own browser state). Unpinning restores terminal B to full width.

automationHints.notes: _renderTabView: pinned browser overrides displayedBrowserTerminalId unless the pinned browser is detached.

items.title: Right-click pin button opens the pin-scope dropdown
preconditions: PATAPIM window frontmost and unoccluded (real right-click must land on the pin icon)
steps: Right-click '.browser-pin-btn' (real mouse right-click)
Inspect the dropdown
Press Escape
items.expected: '.browser-pin-dropdown' opens with exactly two items: 'Pin on this project only' (enabled) and 'Pin on all projects' (disabled/greyed with tooltip 'Coming soon'). Escape closes it without applying a pin.

automationHints.notes: Right-click requires computer_mouse_click with button:right; seed needed two attempts to land on the small icon.

items.title: MCP browser_screenshot captures embedded page content
preconditions: An MCP named browser with https://example.com loaded (browser_navigate)
steps: Call browser_screenshot for that browser_id
Compare with the page content
items.expected: Screenshot shows the rendered page (Example Domain), not a blank/black image — even though the MCP view is offscreen (capture uses temporary positive-coordinate bounds so Chromium composites it, then restores). Visible terminal-panel browsers must not flicker or move during another browser's capture.

automationHints.notes: Concurrent captures are serialized via _captureLocks; also note app_screenshot can never capture WebContentsView content (known limitation, by design).

items.title: app_info returns ready, version, instance, port and window state
steps: Call app_info with no args
Call app_info with instance:'stable' explicitly
items.expected: Both return {ready:true, version:<semver>, instance:'stable'|'dev', port:<31415|31416>, window:{title,bounds,visible,focused,minimized}}; window is null only if the main window is destroyed.

automationHints.notes: GET /app/info; doubles as health check

items.title: app_screenshot captures the main PATAPIM window as an image block
preconditions: PATAPIM main window exists (visible or not)
steps: Call app_screenshot with no args
Call app_screenshot with format:'png'
items.expected: Image content blocks returned (jpeg then png), non-empty data, downscaled to <=1280px wide; visually shows the PATAPIM UI.

automationHints.notes: POST /app/screenshot via instanceRequest

items.title: app_wait_ready returns ready:true quickly for a running instance
steps: Call app_wait_ready with timeout:10
items.expected: Returns within ~1s with {ready:true, version, instance, port, window...} (spread of /app/info).

automationHints.notes: polls /app/info every 1s until info.ready

items.title: app_evaluate executes (or cleanly rejects) JS in the renderer under the current CSP
steps: Call app_evaluate with script:'document.title'
items.expected: Either returns the renderer document title, OR — given the renderer CSP now blocks unsafe-eval — returns a structured error message (HTTP 500 with error text), never a hang or process crash. Record which behavior occurs.

automationHints.notes: Known: app_evaluate is CSP-blocked since the unsafe-eval removal; ui_click/ui_fill are the supported path. Assert graceful failure.

items.title: app_logs returns captured renderer console logs with level/message/timestamp
steps: Call app_logs once (installs the console capture shim on first call)
Trigger renderer console output (e.g. interact with the UI via ui_click)
Call app_logs again
items.expected: Response is an array/object of entries {level:'log'|'warn'|'error'|'uncaught', message, timestamp}, capped at 100 entries; second call includes newer entries.

automationHints.notes: uses executeJavaScript to install window.__patapimConsoleLogs; may be affected by CSP — verify it still works, this is its own regression risk

items.title: app_screenshot still works while the window is minimized (CDP fallback)
preconditions: PATAPIM main window minimized (minimize it via app_evaluate/computer interaction or manually)
steps: Minimize the PATAPIM window
Call app_screenshot
Restore the window
items.expected: Screenshot still returns a valid image: capturePage failure with 'display surface' triggers the CDP Page.captureScreenshot fallback. No 'image is empty' 500.

automationHints.notes: fallback only triggers on 'display surface' errors; verify via app_info window.minimized:true first

items.title: app_wait_ready on a non-running instance returns ready:false after timeout instead of throwing
steps: Call app_wait_ready with instance:'dev', timeout:5
items.expected: After ~5s returns {ready:false, error:'dev instance did not become ready within 5s'} — a normal result object, not an MCP exception.

automationHints.notes: catch-per-poll loop swallows ECONNREFUSED

items.title: browser_navigate loads a URL and reports success
steps: Call browser_navigate with url:'https://example.com'
Call browser_info
items.expected: navigate returns success JSON; browser_info shows url containing 'example.com' and title 'Example Domain'.

automationHints.notes: POST /browser/navigate {url,timeout,waitUntil}

items.title: browser_screenshot returns a JPEG image block by default and PNG when format:'png'
preconditions: A page loaded in the browser panel
steps: Call browser_screenshot with no args
Call browser_screenshot with format:'png'
items.expected: First call returns an MCP image content block with mimeType image/jpeg; second returns image/png. Non-empty base64 data, width <=1280px. Empty data would surface as 'Browser screenshot returned empty image data' error — must not happen.

automationHints.notes: server maps result.format to mimeType; empty data throws

items.title: browser_status reports the browser panel as available
preconditions: PATAPIM running with remote server on port 31415
MCP terminal spawned by PATAPIM (token + PATAPIM_TERMINAL_ID set)
steps: Call browser_status with no args
items.expected: JSON response (no error); indicates the browser panel/session availability state. No 403/500.

automationHints.notes: GET /browser/status; with browser_id it appends a query param

items.title: browser_back, browser_forward and browser_refresh navigate history correctly
steps: Navigate to https://example.com
Navigate to https://patapim.ai
Call browser_back; verify browser_info shows example.com
Call browser_forward; verify browser_info shows patapim.ai
Call browser_refresh; verify browser_info still shows patapim.ai
items.expected: Each call returns success; URL reported by browser_info matches the expected history position after each step.

automationHints.notes: POST /browser/back, /browser/forward, /browser/refresh

items.title: browser_content returns page text by default and truncated HTML with a note when maxLength is small
steps: Call browser_content with no args (defaults type:'text')
Call browser_content with type:'html', maxLength:500
items.expected: Text call contains 'Example Domain'. HTML call returns truncated:true plus a note string 'Content truncated from N to 500 characters...' and html length <=500.

automationHints.notes: MCP server adds the .note field client-side when result.truncated is set

items.title: browser_info returns current URL and page title
preconditions: A page already loaded in the terminal-bound browser
steps: Call browser_info with no args
items.expected: Response contains the current URL and title fields matching the loaded page.

automationHints.notes: GET /browser/info

items.title: browser_navigate with waitUntil:'networkidle' waits for SPA network quiescence
steps: Call browser_navigate with url:'https://patapim.ai', waitUntil:'networkidle', timeout:30000
Immediately call browser_content type:'text'
items.expected: Navigation resolves only after network idles (>=500ms no pending requests) or timeout; content call returns fully rendered page text, not an empty shell.

automationHints.notes: waitUntil enum: load|networkidle; navigate returns success even if still loading after timeout

items.title: browser_click by CSS selector clicks an element and auto-scrolls it into view
preconditions: Test page loaded: file_write a local HTML with link placed below the fold, browser_navigate to its file:// URL
steps: Call browser_click with selector:'#lnk'
Call browser_info
items.expected: Click succeeds (success JSON, no 'Element not found'); browser_info URL now ends with '#clicked' proving the element was scrolled into view and actually clicked via CDP mouse events.

automationHints.notes: selectors here are defined by the test page itself, not the app

items.title: browser_evaluate auto-returns the last expression and supports explicit return
steps: Call browser_evaluate with script:'document.title'
Call browser_evaluate with script:'const n = 2 + 3; return n * 2'
items.expected: First call returns the page title string; second returns 10. Multi-statement script with explicit return works.

automationHints.notes: auto-falls back to CDP if page CSP blocks eval

items.title: browser_fill sets an input value React-compatibly (input + change events fire)
preconditions: Test page with <input id='t1'> and listeners recording input/change events
steps: Call browser_fill with selector:'#t1', value:'hello world'
browser_evaluate to read the input value and event flags
items.expected: Input value is 'hello world'; both input and change events were observed (native value setter path).

automationHints.notes: test-page selector

items.title: browser_cdp_evaluate executes JS via CDP and reuses const/let across calls
steps: Call browser_cdp_evaluate with expression:'const xx = 41; xx + 1'
Call browser_cdp_evaluate again with expression:'const xx = 1; xx' (same identifier)
items.expected: First returns 42. Second does NOT throw 'Identifier xx has already been declared' (expressions are auto-wrapped in block scope).

automationHints.notes: also test awaitPromise:true with 'Promise.resolve(7)' -> 7

items.title: browser_click by visible text finds and clicks the element, returning totalMatches
preconditions: Test page with two buttons labeled 'Do it' loaded via file:// URL
steps: Call browser_click with text:'Do it'
Inspect the response
items.expected: First matching button is clicked; response includes totalMatches:2 (per tool description). Page-side click handler effect observable via browser_evaluate.

automationHints.notes: text search: interactive elements -> aria-labels -> broad DOM walk-up; exact then partial match

items.title: browser_click with a non-matching selector returns a clear error and logs to browser-mcp-errors.log
steps: Call browser_click with selector:'#definitely-not-here-xyz'
Read ~/.patapim/browser-mcp-errors.log tail
items.expected: Tool returns an MCP error (element not found), not a crash/timeout; a new JSONL entry {ts,tool:'browser_click',args,error} is appended to ~/.patapim/browser-mcp-errors.log.

automationHints.notes: logBrowserError appends to ~/.patapim/browser-mcp-errors.log on every tool failure

items.title: browser_click with index clicks the Nth match
preconditions: Test page with two buttons labeled 'Do it' (each sets a distinct window flag on click)
steps: Call browser_click with text:'Do it', index:1
browser_evaluate to read which flag was set
items.expected: Only the second button's flag is set; the first button was not clicked.

automationHints.notes: index is zero-based, default 0

items.title: browser_press_key sends a key with modifiers (Shift+Enter)
preconditions: Test page with a keydown listener recording key+shiftKey on a focused textarea
steps: Focus the textarea (browser_click)
Call browser_press_key with key:'Enter', modifiers:['shift']
browser_evaluate to read the recorded event
items.expected: Recorded keydown has key='Enter' and shiftKey=true; no form submit/plain Enter behavior.

automationHints.notes: modifiers enum: shift|ctrl|alt|meta

items.title: browser_scroll scrolls by pixels and scrolls a selector into view
preconditions: A long page loaded (e.g. test page with 5000px body and #bottom anchor)
steps: Call browser_scroll with direction:'down', amount:800
Inspect returned scroll position info
Call browser_scroll with selector:'#bottom'
browser_evaluate 'window.scrollY'
items.expected: First call returns scroll position ~800px down. Selector call scrolls #bottom into viewport (scrollY near page bottom).

automationHints.notes: selector overrides direction/amount

items.title: browser_set_device emulates a mobile device and 'desktop' clears emulation
steps: Call browser_set_device with device:'iphone-15'
browser_evaluate 'navigator.userAgent + "|" + window.innerWidth'
Call browser_set_device with device:'desktop'
Re-evaluate
items.expected: After iphone-15: mobile UA (contains 'iPhone') and narrow viewport (~393px). After desktop: original UA and viewport restored.

automationHints.notes: also covers landscape:true variant if time permits

items.title: browser_type types character-by-character into a contenteditable element
steps: Call browser_type with selector:'#ce', text:'typed-via-cdp'
browser_evaluate to read #ce textContent
items.expected: contenteditable contains 'typed-via-cdp'; typed via CDP Input.dispatchKeyEvent (no \n in text so no Enter fired).

automationHints.notes: WARNING from description: \n in text triggers Enter

items.title: browser_upload_file sets files on an input[type=file] without a native dialog
preconditions: A small temp file written to disk
steps: Call browser_upload_file with selector:'#f1', files:['<abs temp file path>']
browser_evaluate to read document.querySelector('#f1').files[0].name
items.expected: files list on the input contains the temp file (correct name); no native file dialog appeared.

automationHints.notes: CDP DOM.setFileInputFiles path

items.title: browser_wait resolves when a delayed element appears
preconditions:
Test page that injects
ready
after 2s via setTimeout
steps: Navigate to the test page
Immediately call browser_wait with selector:'#late', timeout:10000
items.expected: Wait resolves successfully in ~2s (polls every 200ms), well before the 10s timeout.

automationHints.notes: also exercise text: and visible:true variants

items.title: browser_wait timeout returns page title and content preview for debugging
steps: Call browser_wait with selector:'#never-appears', timeout:2000
items.expected: After ~2s the response indicates timeout AND includes the page title and a content preview (per tool description) to help debug the bad selector — not a bare error.

automationHints.notes: key error-path UX promise of the tool

items.title: browser_click with doubleClick:true sends a double click
preconditions: Test page with an element that has a dblclick listener setting window.__dbl=true
steps: Call browser_click with selector for the element and doubleClick:true
browser_evaluate 'window.__dbl'
items.expected: dblclick event fired (window.__dbl === true).

items.title: browser_click with raw x/y coordinates clicks at pixel position
preconditions: Test page that records click coordinates on document (window.__last = {x,y})
steps: Call browser_click with x:100, y:150 (no selector/text)
browser_evaluate 'window.__last'
items.expected: Click lands at approximately (100,150) in page coordinates; recorded values within a few px.

automationHints.notes: pixel path is for canvas/shadow-DOM cases

items.title: Named browser session (browser_id) is created on first use and appears in browser_list
steps: Call browser_navigate with url:'https://example.com', browser_id:'qa-test-1'
Call browser_list

automationHints.notes: GET /browser/list returns count and max:10

items.title: browser_close closes a named session and it disappears from browser_list
steps: Call browser_close with browser_id:'qa-test-1'
Call browser_list
items.expected: Close returns {success:true, browser_id:'qa-test-1', closed:true}; browser_list no longer contains it and count decremented.

items.expected: MCP error with message 'No browser session named "no-such-session-xyz"' (HTTP 404 from /browser/close-session); no crash.

items.title: browser_downloads returns downloadDir and a downloads array
steps: Call browser_downloads with no args

items.expected: Response is {success:true, downloadDir:<path>, downloads:[...]}; after triggering a download the new file appears in the list.

automationHints.notes: GET /browser/downloads; browser_id filters by named session

items.title: Creating an 11th named browser session is rejected (max 10)
preconditions: No named sessions open (close all first via browser_list + browser_close)

Attempt browser_navigate with browser_id:'qa-pool-11'
Cleanup: browser_close all 10 sessions
items.expected: Sessions 1-10 succeed; the 11th returns a clear limit error mentioning the max of 10 named sessions; browser_list shows count:10, max:10.

items.title: computer_click_text finds a visible label via OCR and clicks its center

Verify via app_screenshot/computer_screenshot that the click took effect

items.title: computer_mouse_click left-clicks at image coords and returns the new cursor position

computer_mouse_click at a harmless spot (e.g. PATAPIM window title bar) with space:'image'
Verify via computer_cursor_position and app_info (window focused)
items.expected: {success:true, x, y}; the click lands at the intended point (PATAPIM window gains focus when its title bar is clicked).

items.title: computer_mouse_move with space:'image' moves the real cursor to the on-screen point seen in the screenshot

Call computer_mouse_move with those x,y and space:'image'

items.title: computer_screenshot returns image plus display metadata text block

items.title: computer_batch runs a sequence of actions and returns per-step results

items.expected: {success:false, failedAt:1, results:[{ok:true,type:'wait',...},{ok:false,type:'key',error:'Unknown key: notakey'}]} — step 2 (index 1) failed, step 3 never ran (results length 2).

automationHints.notes: also verify unknown action type errors: {type:'fly'} → 'unknown action type: fly'

items.title: computer_click_text returns TEXT_NOT_FOUND with the list of text it did see

items.title: computer_cursor_position returns {x,y} in logical (DIP) coordinates
steps: Call computer_cursor_position

automationHints.notes: GET /computer/cursor

items.title: computer_drag performs press-move-release between two points
preconditions: A draggable target visible (e.g. a desktop icon area or PATAPIM window title bar to move the window slightly)

computer_drag from the title bar (x1,y1) to a point 50px right (x2,y2), space:'image'

items.title: computer_find_text OCRs the screen: query returns matches, no query dumps all lines
preconditions: PATAPIM window visible with known text (e.g. project name in sidebar)
steps: Call computer_find_text with query:'patapim'
Call computer_find_text with no query
Call computer_find_text with a region to narrow the OCR
items.expected: Query call: {success:true, imageSize:{width,height}, matches:[{text,x,y}...], note about image-pixel coords}. No-query call returns lines:[...] instead of matches. Region call is faster and returns coords within the region image space. First OCR may take longer (worker init, 45s cap).

automationHints.notes: eng+spa Tesseract; regression guard: multiple lines must be returned (the truncate-to-1-line bug was fixed)

items.title: computer_key presses combos (mod+a) and rejects unknown key names
preconditions: A scratch text field with content, focused
steps: computer_key with keys:['mod','a'] (select all) then keys:['delete']
computer_key with keys:['notakey']
items.expected: Combo selects all then deletes (field empties; returns {success:true, pressed:['mod','a']}); modifiers pressed first, released in reverse. 'notakey' returns 500 with 'Unknown key: notakey'. Empty keys array → 400 'keys (non-empty array) is required'.

automationHints.notes: mod = Ctrl on Windows / Cmd on macOS; per-key press/release avoids libnut 'Invalid key flag' bug

items.title: computer_mouse_click with button:'right' opens a native context menu
preconditions: Desktop or an app area with a known right-click menu visible

computer_mouse_click with button:'right' at an empty desktop area (space:'image')
computer_screenshot again
Press Escape (computer_key ['escape']) to dismiss

items.title: computer_screenshot with region zooms into a sub-rectangle and remaps image-space clicks

Click a target visible in the zoomed image via computer_mouse_click space:'image'

items.title: computer_scroll scrolls the wheel down then up over a target point
preconditions: A scrollable window visible (e.g. browser with long page)
steps: computer_screenshot; note the scrollable area's coords

computer_screenshot to verify content moved
computer_scroll dy:-5 to restore
items.expected: {success:true, scrolled:{dx:0,dy:5}}; visible content scrolled down then back up. dy>0 = down, dy<0 = up per convention.

automationHints.notes: pointer is moved to (x,y) first when provided

items.title: computer_type types ASCII key-by-key and non-ASCII via clipboard paste (clipboard restored)
preconditions: A safe focused text field (e.g. open Notepad or focus a scratch terminal)
Put a known string 'CLIP-BEFORE' on the clipboard first

computer_type with text:'plain ascii 123'
computer_type with text:'acentos: ñandú — 🎉'
Verify both strings rendered; after ~1s read the clipboard
items.expected: ASCII path typed key-by-key; Unicode path pasted via clipboard preserving 'ñandú — 🎉' intact (no Mac-Roman mojibake); clipboard restored to 'CLIP-BEFORE' ~200ms after paste. Returns {success:true, typed:true} both times.

items.title: computer_batch rejects empty and oversized action arrays

items.title: computer_mouse_move without numeric x/y returns 400 'x and y (numbers) are required'
steps: Via Bash curl (or malformed tool call): POST /computer/mouse-move with body {"x":"abc"} and valid token
items.expected: HTTP 400 {error:'x and y (numbers) are required'}; validation happens BEFORE the enabled-gate so no mouse movement occurs.

items.title: computer_screenshot maxWidth caps the produced image width

items.title: computer_confirm approve flow: dialog appears and approving returns approved:true
steps: Call computer_confirm with action:'QA approve test', detail:'click Approve in the dialog'
In the PATAPIM window, click the Approve/confirm button in the modal (agent path: locate it via app_screenshot + ui_click; human path: click it)
items.expected: A confirmation modal appears in the renderer showing the action label and detail; after approving, the tool returns {success:true, approved:true}.

automationHints.notes: renderer receives IPC COMPUTER_CONTROL_CONFIRM {id,action,detail}; reply via COMPUTER_CONTROL_CONFIRM_REPLY. The confirm call blocks until reply or 30s timeout — run it from one terminal and click from another agent path.

items.title: computer_confirm reject flow: denying the dialog returns approved:false
steps: Call computer_confirm with action:'QA reject test'
Click the Deny/reject button in the modal
items.expected: Returns {success:true, approved:false}; the calling agent is expected NOT to proceed. No side effects.

automationHints.notes: _resolveConfirm(id, false) path

items.title: computer_confirm times out to approved:false after 30s with no user response
steps: Call computer_confirm with action:'QA timeout test'
Wait without interacting
items.expected: After CONFIRM_TIMEOUT_MS (30s) the call resolves {success:true, approved:false}; the pending entry is removed (a later approve click on the stale dialog does nothing / dialog dismisses).

automationHints.notes: CONFIRM_TIMEOUT_MS=30000; fail-safe deny

items.title: All computer_* actions return 403 FEATURE_DISABLED while the Computer Control toggle is OFF
steps: Call computer_cursor_position
Call computer_mouse_click with x:10,y:10
Call computer_screenshot
Call computer_find_text with query:'x'
items.expected: Every call rejects with 'Computer Control is disabled' (server code FEATURE_DISABLED, HTTP 403). The MCP server does NOT retry the next token candidate for FEATURE_DISABLED (distinct from token mismatch).

automationHints.notes: _ccGate + per-route checks; FEATURE_DISABLED short-circuits token fallback in patapimRequest

items.title: computer_confirm with toggle OFF returns 403 FEATURE_DISABLED (no dialog shown)
steps: Call computer_confirm with action:'QA disabled-path test'
items.expected: Error 'Computer Control is disabled' (FEATURE_DISABLED); no confirmation modal appears in the PATAPIM window.

automationHints.notes: POST /computer/confirm checks settings before requestConfirmation

items.title: instance:'dev' targeting a stopped dev instance returns a clear 'not running' error
steps: Call app_info with instance:'dev'
Call browser_status with instance:'dev'
items.expected: Both reject with 'dev PATAPIM instance is not running (port 31416). Start it first.' (ECONNREFUSED mapped) or 'Cannot read token for dev instance' if mcp-token-dev is absent — either way a descriptive error, never a raw socket error or hang.

automationHints.notes: instanceRequest reads ~/.patapim/mcp-token-dev fresh and maps ECONNREFUSED

items.title: space:'image' action without a prior computer_screenshot returns a guidance error

items.expected: Error: 'No screenshot recorded yet — call computer_screenshot first, then click with space:"image".' Mouse does not move.

items.title: Wrong x-patapim-token yields 'Access denied. Token mismatch' after exhausting candidates
steps: Via Bash: curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:31415/browser/navigate -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'x-patapim-token: bogus-token-123' -d '{"url":"about:blank"}'
Observe the HTTP status and body
items.expected: HTTP 403 with an access-denied body and NO FEATURE_DISABLED code. (Through the MCP server this surfaces as 'Access denied. Token mismatch — PATAPIM may have restarted. Try restarting the terminal.' after both file and env token candidates fail.)

automationHints.notes: raw HTTP via Bash; the MCP retry chain (file token then env token) lives in attemptRequest

items.title: Kill-switch hotkey (Ctrl+Alt+Shift+Escape) disables Computer Control and aborts pending confirms

Check the pending confirm result and the Settings toggle

items.expected: The pending confirm resolves approved:false immediately; the Settings toggle flips OFF (persisted via settingsStore); subsequent computer_* calls return FEATURE_DISABLED; toolbar button turns red/off state.

automationHints.notes: KILL_SWITCH_ACCELERATOR='CommandOrControl+Alt+Shift+Escape'; the hotkey itself must be pressed on the real keyboard (cannot use computer_key after it fires) — human assist or pre-arranged

items.title: Computer Control enabled state persists across an app restart (kill-switch re-registers)
preconditions: Computer Control toggle ON in the dev instance
steps: Verify computer_cursor_position works (toggle ON)
Restart the dev instance (rebuild-dev-safe.bat or equivalent)
app_wait_ready instance:'dev'
Call computer_cursor_position with instance:'dev' again
items.expected: After restart the toggle is still ON (init() re-applies settingsStore state): computer_* calls succeed without re-enabling, and killSwitchOk is true in the renderer state.

automationHints.notes: init() calls onSettingsChange(settingsStore.get('computerControl')) at startup; run on dev instance to avoid killing the working stable instance

items.title: 'Controlling' indicator lights during computer actions and decays ~1.5s after the last one
steps: Run a computer_mouse_move
Within 1s, take app_screenshot — observe the gold toolbar button / top 'controlling' strip
Wait 3s with no actions, take app_screenshot again
items.expected: During/just after the action the controlling indicator (gold button + top strip) is visible; after CONTROL_IDLE_MS (1500ms) of inactivity it turns off. Rapid back-to-back actions keep it lit without flicker.

automationHints.notes: _markControlling + COMPUTER_CONTROL_STATE IPC {enabled, controlling, active, killSwitchOk}

items.title: perf_metrics returns a populated metrics object

items.title: ui_click on a valid selector clicks the element and reports tag/text

items.title: MCP calls survive a target-instance restart via fresh file-token fallback
preconditions: MCP session in a stable-instance terminal

Hide the window (close-to-tray or minimize so isVisible() is false)
Trigger another notification
Observe the OS toast
items.expected: Visible window → no toast; hidden window → native toast titled '<Kind>: <project> · <terminal>' with up-to-240-char body. Multiple rapid notifications coalesce into a single summary toast

items.title: Clicking an OS toast restores the window and focuses the source terminal
preconditions: An OS toast is visible (see notifications-030)
steps: Click the toast
items.expected: Main window restores/shows/focuses (dock revealed on macOS); the renderer receives FOCUS_TERMINAL and lands on the terminal that fired the notification; the toast clears. Toasts are also closed programmatically when the user views/dismisses the card in-app (no lingering Notification Center entries)

items.title: OS toast suppressed when bell toggle OFF, but tray badge still updates
steps: Trigger a notification while the window is hidden
Watch for OS toasts
Open the tray icon menu / badge
items.expected: No OS toast (payload.silent=true suppresses trayManager.notify), but the tray badge count and tray menu pending items still update so the work is discoverable

items.title: Bell toggles panel closed when already open
preconditions: Popout window NOT open (bell focuses popout instead when it is)
steps: Click the toolbar bell button (.btn-notifs-toggle) again
items.expected: Panel hides ('visible' class removed); main app fully in foreground

automationHints.notes: toggle() short-circuits to focusing the popout when popoutOpen is true — keep popout closed for this case

items.title: Open notifications panel via toolbar bell
steps: Click the toolbar bell button (.btn-notifs-toggle)
Observe the right-side panel
items.expected: Panel slides in on the right with 'Notifications' header, sound/telegram/OS-toggle icons, pop-out and ✕ buttons; with no active notifications the empty state 'No terminals waiting for you right now.' is shown; footer shows ARCHIVED count and EXPAND/COLLAPSE

automationHints.notes: Panel visibility = #notifs-panel gains class 'visible'

items.title: Close panel via collapse button
steps: Click the collapse button (#notifs-collapse-btn)
items.expected: Panel hides, main app in foreground (same hide() path as ✕)

items.title: Close panel via header ✕ button
steps: Click the ✕ button (#notifs-close) in the panel header
items.expected: Panel hides; no other state changes (toggles, lists untouched)

items.title: OS popup flag persists across restart via flag file
steps: Toggle OS popups ON via #notifs-os-toggle
Verify ~/.patapim/claude-os-notif.flag contains '1'
Restart the app
Open the panel and check the bell toggle
Cleanup: restore the original OFF state
items.expected: After restart the bell toggle renders active (flag file is the single source of truth, read via NOTIF_FLAG_GET); the notify-stop.js hook sees the same value without app restart

automationHints.notes: Visual starts OFF then corrects when the async read returns — allow a short delay before asserting

items.title: Sound mute, tone, Telegram-forward and expanded-view preferences persist across restart
steps: Set: sound MUTED, tone = Chime, Telegram forward OFF, view EXPANDED
Restart the app (rebuild-dev-safe.bat or quit+relaunch)
Open the notifications panel and inspect all four states
Cleanup: restore sound ON, tone Beep, TG ON, view as found
items.expected: All four restore from localStorage: speaker shows muted icon, tone menu checkmark on Chime, paper-plane dim, footer label COLLAPSE (expanded view active)

automationHints.notes: Keys: notification-sound-enabled, notification-sound-tone, patapim-notifs-tg-forward, patapim-notifs-expanded-view. Note: active/archived notification LISTS intentionally do NOT persist (in-memory only).

items.title: Clicking a card in the popout raises the main window and focuses the terminal
preconditions: Popout window open with ≥1 active card
Main window minimized or behind other windows
steps: Click the active card's body in the popout
items.expected: Main window restores/shows/focuses BEFORE the renderer focuses the source terminal; the card archives (reason 'click'); archive/clear actions in the popout do NOT steal focus — only 'click' raises the main window

items.title: Dock button closes popout and re-shows the in-window panel
steps: Click the dock button (#notifs-dock, tooltip 'Dock back into PATAPIM window') in the popout header
items.expected: Popout window closes (destroyed, freeing its renderer process); the in-window notifications panel re-shows with identical state; main renderer receives NOTIFS_POPOUT_STATE open:false

automationHints.notes: Dock action = NOTIFS_POPOUT_ACTION type 'dock' → show() + NOTIFS_POPOUT_CLOSE

items.title: Minimized popout restores when bell or pop-out button is clicked
steps: Minimize the popout via its caption button (note: skipTaskbar means NO taskbar entry exists)
Click the toolbar bell (or #notifs-popout-btn) in the main window
items.expected: Popout explicitly restores, re-clamps on-screen, shows, moves to top and focuses — the bell must not look 'dead' (regression guard: focus() alone neither restores nor repositions)

items.title: Pin toggle sets always-on-top and the preference persists
steps: Click the pin button (#notifs-pin, tooltip 'Keep window always on top')
Click another app's window over the popout
Close the popout, restart nothing, re-open via #notifs-popout-btn
Cleanup: unpin (tooltip becomes 'Always on top is on — click to unpin')
items.expected: Pinned popout stays above other windows (setAlwaysOnTop true); preference stored in localStorage 'patapim-notifs-popout-pin' and applied on the next open (NOTIFS_POPOUT_OPEN passes alwaysOnTop); tooltip flips between alwaysOnTopOff/alwaysOnTopOn strings

items.title: Pop-out opens floating window and hides the in-window panel
steps: Click #notifs-popout-btn
Observe the new window
Click the toolbar bell in the main window
items.expected: A 380x640 'Notifications · PATAPIM' window opens (skipTaskbar, no menu bar, dark background) showing the same state; the in-window panel hides (mutually exclusive); while the popout is open, clicking the bell focuses the popout instead of toggling the panel

automationHints.notes: ui_* tools target the main renderer only — assert popout content via computer_screenshot. Cleanup: dock or close the popout.

items.title: Popout actions mirror to main renderer state and vice versa
steps: In the POPOUT: archive an active card (×), toggle Telegram forward, toggle sound, toggle expanded view
Dock back
Verify the in-window panel reflects every change
Re-popout and trigger a new notification in the main app
Verify the popout list updates live
items.expected: Every popout action round-trips through NOTIFS_POPOUT_ACTION to the main renderer (single source of truth) and the updated snapshot is pushed back via NOTIFS_POPOUT_PUSH_STATE; both views never diverge

automationHints.notes: Popout is a separate BrowserWindow — drive it with computer_* tools, verify main panel with ui_*/app_screenshot

items.title: Off-screen popout is clamped back into the visible work area
preconditions: Means to park it off-screen (second monitor that gets disconnected, or move it beyond screen edge)
steps: Move the popout fully (or mostly) outside every display's work area
Click the bell / pop-out button to re-summon it
items.expected: clampToVisibleArea recenters the window inside the nearest display's work area (width/height capped to the area) so it is fully visible and focused

items.title: Pop-out button is present in the panel header
steps: Inspect #notifs-header
items.expected: #notifs-popout-btn (NE-arrow-leaving-box SVG, tooltip 'Pop out to a separate window') sits between the OS bell toggle and the ✕ close button

automationHints.notes: Presence-only check; opening the popout is covered by notifications-032

items.title: Left-click sound speaker mutes notification sound
steps: Click the speaker icon (#notifs-sound-toggle) once
items.expected: Icon swaps to speaker-with-X (SOUND_OFF_SVG); button gains 'muted' class; tooltip becomes 'Notification Sound: OFF (click to unmute)' (tabbar.notificationOff); localStorage 'notification-sound-enabled' = '0'

automationHints.notes: ui_state has no per-selector query; verify icon swap via screenshot. Restore ON afterwards.

items.title: Left-click sound speaker unmutes notification sound
steps: Click the speaker icon (#notifs-sound-toggle) once
items.expected: Icon swaps to speaker-with-waves (SOUND_ON_SVG); 'muted' class removed; tooltip 'Notification Sound: ON (click to mute)' (tabbar.notificationOn)

automationHints.notes: Leave sound ON as the clean final state

items.title: Finished tone vs asking tone are audibly distinct (perfect fifth higher)
steps: Trigger a 'finished' transition (Claude completes a turn in an unfocused tab) and listen
Trigger an 'asking' transition (Claude awaits a reply / plan approval) and listen
items.expected: Both play the user's selected tone, but the asking tone is pitched 1.5× higher (perfect fifth) than the finished tone — clearly distinguishable by ear

items.title: planReady notification forwards to Telegram with inline option buttons
preconditions: Claude Code in plan mode working on a plan
steps: Let Claude finish a plan so the approval menu (≥2 numbered options) appears
Keep the terminal unfocused
Check the Telegram chat
items.expected: Telegram message arrives: 🚨 + bold project name + italic terminal name + plan summary text (HTML-escaped, ≤700 chars), with an inline keyboard of 'N. label' buttons (callback_data plan:<terminalId>:<N>); tapping a button writes the chosen number into the terminal and edits the message

items.title: Right-click sound speaker opens tone-selection context menu
preconditions: PATAPIM window frontmost and unoccluded (real right-click hits the topmost OS window at the target pixels)
steps: Locate #notifs-sound-toggle on screen
Perform a real right-click on it (computer_mouse_click button:'right')
items.expected: A floating context menu (.toolbar-btn-context-menu) appears at the cursor, near the speaker icon

automationHints.notes: DOM contextmenu events via ui_click are not enough — handler is on 'contextmenu'. If the window is occluded, raise it (Win32 HWND_TOPMOST workaround in raw log) and restore z-order afterwards. Escape dismisses.

items.title: Selecting a tone sets it active and plays it
steps: Click 'Chime' in the menu
Observe: menu closes and immediately re-opens at the same position
Inspect the re-opened menu
items.expected: Checkmark moved to Chime (Beep unchecked); the tone plays once on selection; localStorage 'notification-sound-tone' = 'chime'. Cleanup: re-select Beep, press Escape

automationHints.notes: showSoundContextMenuAt re-opens the menu after selection by design. Audio itself not screenshot-verifiable — pair with notifications-027 for a human ear check.

items.title: Test Sound plays the selected tone and keeps the menu open
steps: Click '🔊 Test Sound' at the bottom of the menu
items.expected: The selected tone plays (TerminalTabBar.playTone); the context menu REMAINS open (no closeMenu on this item); no console errors

automationHints.notes: Agent verifies menu stays open + no JS errors; human confirms audio actually sounded

items.title: Tone menu lists 6 tones plus Test Sound with active checkmark
steps: Screenshot/inspect the open menu
items.expected: Menu items in order: Ping, Beep, Chime, Ding, Bubble, Knock, a divider, then 'Test Sound' with a speaker icon; the currently selected tone (default: Beep) carries the ✓ checkmark and 'tone-active' class

automationHints.notes: Labels come from tabbar.tonePing/Beep/Chime/Ding/Bubble/Knock + tabbar.testSound

items.title: Tone menu dismisses on Escape/outside click and clamps to window edges
steps: Right-click #notifs-sound-toggle to open the tone menu
Press Escape → menu closes
Re-open and click anywhere outside the menu → menu closes
Observe initial placement near the right window edge
items.expected: Escape, outside mousedown, window blur and scroll all dismiss the menu (attachOutsideDismiss); when the menu would overflow the window, it is repositioned to stay ≥5px inside the right/bottom edges

automationHints.notes: The sound toggle sits near the right edge so the horizontal clamp is naturally exercised

items.title: Add then delete a throwaway API key end-to-end

CLEANUP: verify E2E_THROWAWAY_KEY row is gone from the list and from stored keys
items.expected: Key is persisted via api-keys:set, listed masked, then fully removed via api-keys:delete; list returns to its original contents

automationHints.notes: Row action buttons only appear on :hover (opacity 0) — use computer_mouse_move or click via selector anyway. Was SKIPPED in seed run as destructive; now executable with explicit cleanup.

items.title: API Keys Add button enables only when both name and value are filled

Clear both fields — verify Add disabled again

Inspect the list and the add-key form

Click #apikeys-toggle-vis

Click #apikeys-toggle-vis again
Clear the field
items.expected: First click: value shown as plaintext (input type=text), icon switches to eye-off (slashed); second click: value masked again (type=password), icon reverts to eye

items.title: Switch theme to Light and back to Dark

items.title: Appearance tab renders theme radios, interface-mode cards and four toggles

Inspect the section

automationHints.notes: Source oddity: two renderAppearance() declarations exist (lines ~2128 and ~2278); the later async one wins via hoisting — the theme-card grid variant at 2128 is dead code. Expect the radio-row variant.

items.title: Switch interface mode Beginner ↔ Advanced

items.title: Switch locale to Español live-translates nav and content, then restore English
preconditions: Preferences modal open on Language tab
steps: Click the Español option
Verify without reopening: modal header, nav labels ([data-i18n]) and the Language pane re-render in Spanish; Español row now .active
Click another section (e.g. Appearance) and verify its strings are Spanish too

items.title: Add-model modal renders; Ollama-not-running notice and Start Ollama button

Verify the .acm-overlay modal opens titled 'Add local model' with: Model select (.acm-select) + Refresh button (.acm-refresh), Name input (.acm-input), Icon swatch (.acm-icon-btn) with hint, Save/Cancel footer
Verify the notice (.acm-notice) shows 'Ollama is not running' with a 'Start Ollama' button (.acm-notice-btn)
Click 'Start Ollama' — verify the button disables and label changes to the starting state
Wait: if Ollama starts, the model select repopulates and the notice hides; if it cannot start, the notice text changes to the not-installed message and the button re-enables
items.expected: Modal fields all render; not-running notice appears when the OLLAMA_LIST_MODELS IPC reports running:false; Start Ollama drives OLLAMA_START and either loads models or surfaces the not-installed error

automationHints.notes: Stop Ollama first (e.g. taskkill ollama) to force the notice; OLLAMA IPC handlers in src/main/ollamaBackend.js (no stop IPC exists — stopping is manual)

items.title: Click a model button — launches 'ollama run <tag>' in a terminal
preconditions: Custom model exists with a tag that IS installed in Ollama
steps: Click the custom model button in .btn-row-models
Verify a terminal receives/runs 'ollama run <tag>'
Optional negative path: create a model with a fake tag (Other… → 'fake-model:1b'), click it, and observe the model-not-found handling (t('customModel.modelNotFound'))
Cleanup: exit the ollama session (/bye or Ctrl+D) and delete any fake model entry
items.expected: Launch button starts an interactive ollama run session in a terminal tab; a missing tag surfaces the model-not-found message instead of a silent failure

automationHints.notes: Launch may load a multi-GB model into RAM — prefer a tiny model (e.g. qwen2.5:0.5b) for the happy path

items.title: Delete a custom model via context menu with confirm dialog
steps: Right-click the model button and click Delete
Verify the confirm dialog 'Delete "E2E Model 2"?' appears (.acm-overlay, red Delete + Cancel)
Click Cancel — button remains
Repeat and confirm Delete
Verify the button is removed and the .btn-row-models row disappears entirely if it was the last model
items.expected: Confirm gates the deletion; on confirm the model is removed from the row and from persistence; empty row is removed from layout

items.title: Model select lists installed tags, Refresh reloads, 'Other…' frees a custom tag with name autofill, validation errors
steps: Open the add-model modal (right-click AI button → 'Add local model…')
Verify the select briefly shows 'Loading…' then lists the installed model tags plus a final 'Other…' option; first tag preselected and Name auto-filled from it
Click Refresh (.acm-refresh) — verify the list reloads without losing the modal
Select 'Other…' — verify a custom-tag text input appears below and takes focus; type 'fake-model:1b' and watch the Name autofill prettify it
Clear the custom tag and click Save — verify the tag-required error in .acm-error
Set a tag, clear the Name, Save — verify the name-required error
Set Name to an existing model's name (if one exists) — verify the duplicate-name error
Press Escape to dismiss without saving
items.expected: Loading → tags → 'Other…' flow works; refresh re-queries Ollama; custom tag input toggles with selection; name autofills until manually edited; all three validation errors render without closing the modal

automationHints.notes: If Ollama running with zero models, select shows the disabled 'No models' option — also worth observing

items.title: Right-click model button menu: Edit / Delete / Add — edit path
preconditions: Custom model 'E2E Model' button visible in .btn-row-models
steps: Right-click the 'E2E Model' button
Verify a .terminal-context-menu appears with items Edit, Delete, divider, Add (t('customModel.add'))
Click Edit — verify the modal opens titled 'Edit local model' with tag/name/icon prefilled and a red Delete button
Rename to 'E2E Model 2' and Save — verify the button label updates
Click elsewhere to confirm the context menu dismisses on outside interaction
items.expected: Context menu renders and positions at the cursor (clamped to the window); Edit prefills and saves; outside click dismisses the menu

automationHints.notes: If editing a model whose tag is no longer installed, the tag is kept selectable at the top of the select

items.title: Save a custom model — launch button appears in the secondary sidebar row
preconditions: Add-model modal open with a valid tag selected
steps: Set Name 'E2E Model', pick an icon via the swatch (icon picker .ipk-overlay), keep the selected tag
Press Enter in the Name field (or click Save)
Verify the modal closes and a new button labelled 'E2E Model' appears in the .btn-row-models row under the static AI buttons
Verify the button id is btn-custom-<id> and shows the chosen icon
Cleanup: keep it for the context-menu/delete items or remove via right-click → Delete
items.expected: Model saved and rendered as .btn.btn-custom-model in .btn-row-models; row only visible while at least one model exists and AI buttons are shown in the sidebar

items.title: 'Custom local models' experimental toggle gates the model UI
steps: Toggle #exp-customModels ON (.exp-item[data-feat="customModels"] .exp-toggle label)
Close the Preferences modal
Right-click a built-in AI button (e.g. Claude) in the project sidebar
Verify the context menu now includes an 'Add local model…' item
Re-open Preferences → Experimental and toggle #exp-customModels OFF
Right-click the AI button again — verify the item is gone and any .btn-row-models row is hidden
items.expected: Enabling fires 'recheck-custom-models'; the add-model menu entry and the secondary model button row only exist while the flag is ON

automationHints.notes: Right-click requires computer_* tools; menu built in aiButtonContextMenu.js lines ~253-270

items.title: Experimental tab renders the four feature rows and footer note
steps: Click the 'Experimental' nav item
Inspect the pane
items.expected: 'Experimental Features' heading + disclaimer; four .exp-item rows: Beta Channel (#exp-betaChannel), System-Wide Dictation (#exp-systemDictation), Custom local models (#exp-customModels), Global Dashboard (#exp-dashboard); footer note 'Changes take effect immediately. Some features may require a restart.'

items.title: Toggle 'Global Dashboard' shows/hides the compass icon live
steps: Click the toggle label inside .exp-item[data-feat="dashboard"] .exp-toggle
Verify a compass icon appears in the tab bar
Click again to revert and verify the icon is removed
items.expected: Toggle ON adds the compass icon to the tab bar immediately; toggle OFF removes it; no crash

automationHints.notes: Gate recheck fires window event 'recheck-dashboard-gate'

items.title: Toggle 'Beta Channel' and revert
steps: Note the original state of #exp-betaChannel
Click its toggle label (.exp-item[data-feat="betaChannel"] .exp-toggle label)
Verify the checkbox state flips with no error in app logs
Click again to restore the original state
items.expected: Toggle persists via EXPERIMENTAL_SET and flips back cleanly; on IPC failure the checkbox auto-reverts (error path)

automationHints.notes: Beta channel affects which update feed is used — restore original state

items.title: Toggle 'System-Wide Dictation' invokes enable/disable backend
steps: Note the original state of #exp-systemDictation
Flip the toggle
Check app logs for dictation enable/disable activity and the tray for the mic icon appearing/disappearing
Flip back to the original state
items.expected: Toggling additionally invokes SYSTEM_DICTATION_ENABLE/DISABLE IPC (not just the flag); tray mic icon presence matches the state; on failure the checkbox reverts

automationHints.notes: Tray icon verification needs an OS-level screenshot; human should confirm hotkey actually dictates

items.title: Language tab renders five locale options with exactly one active
steps: Click the 'Language' nav item
Inspect the options
items.expected: Five options: English, 日本語, Español, Français, Português; current locale highlighted (amber border); footer 'Changes apply immediately to the entire interface.'

items.title: Switch language to Spanish and revert to English
preconditions: Preferences modal open on Language, English active
steps: Click the Spanish option (label.lang-option containing input[value="es"])
Verify the whole modal re-renders in Spanish ('Preferencias', 'Claves API', 'Apariencia', 'Idioma')
Click the English option (input[value="en"]) to revert
items.expected: Full UI re-renders in the selected language immediately on each click; English fully restored

items.title: Open Preferences modal via logo menu and verify shell renders
preconditions: App ready (dev instance), no onboarding overlay
steps: Click the sidebar logo (#sidebar-logo-icon)
Click 'Preferences…' (#logo-menu-prefs) in the logo menu
Observe the modal
items.expected: Overlay .prefs-overlay gets class 'visible'; header 'Preferences'; left nav lists sections including General, API Keys, Custom Instructions, Custom CLIs, Workspace Sync, Plugins, Gmail, Telegram, WhatsApp, Notifications, Appearance, Tray, Computer Control, Experimental, Language, About

automationHints.notes: Ctrl+, accelerator is NOT registered and computer_key cannot send comma; always open via logo menu

items.title: Notifications tab renders email/WhatsApp/default-channel/activity-window form
steps: Click the 'Notifications' nav item
Inspect the form
items.expected: Email card with #notif-email input + Test button; WhatsApp card with #notif-whatsapp tel input + Test button; default-channel radios input[name="notif-default"] (Telegram/Email); #notif-active-window select; #notif-save button

items.title: Change Activity Window select and revert (auto-save)
steps: Note the current #notif-active-window value (e.g. '30')
Set #notif-active-window to '60'
Verify a green 'Saved ✓' status appears (auto-save on change)
Set it back to the original value
items.expected: Each change shows 'Saved ✓'; value persists in notify-config; original value restored

items.title: API key, custom CLI and custom model survive an app restart
preconditions: Throwaway API key E2E_TEST_KEY, custom CLI 'E2E CLI' and (optionally) a custom model exist
steps: Create the test entities if not present (API key, custom CLI, custom model)
Restart the dev instance (./rebuild-dev-safe.bat) and wait for ready
Open Preferences → API Keys — verify E2E_TEST_KEY is listed
Open Custom CLIs — verify 'E2E CLI' row and its sidebar button are back
Verify the custom model button row re-rendered (if a model was created)
Cleanup: delete all three test entities
items.expected: All three persist across restart (keychain, ~/.patapim/custom-clis.json, custom-models store) and re-render in both the preferences lists and the sidebar

automationHints.notes: Use rebuild-dev-safe.bat, never rebuild-dev.bat, from agent context

items.title: Plugins section renders header, filter pills and plugin cards
steps: Click the 'Plugins' nav item
Inspect the pane
Click the 'Installed' filter pill, then 'Enabled', then 'All'
items.expected: Title/subtitle + refresh button (#plg-refresh-btn); three filter pills (All / Installed / Enabled) with the active one highlighted; #plg-list shows .plg-item cards each with star button, icon, name, status badge (Enabled/Installed/Available), description, 'by <author>' meta, and either a toggle+uninstall (installed) or an Install button (available); filters narrow the list accordingly; an empty filter shows the .plg-empty message naming the filter

automationHints.notes: Sort order: favorites first, then installed, then alphabetical

items.title: Enable/disable an installed plugin via its toggle and revert
preconditions: At least one installed plugin present
steps: Note the original toggle state of an installed plugin (.plg-toggle-btn on its card)
Click the toggle
Verify the toggle/status badge updates (Enabled ↔ Installed) without an error toast
Cleanup: click again to restore the original state
items.expected: Toggle invokes TOGGLE_PLUGIN IPC and the card reflects the new state; failure path shows the 'failed to toggle' toast; original state restored

automationHints.notes: Modifies the user's real Claude Code plugin config — always revert. Locale strings plugins.enabledRestart/plugins.disabled exist; observe whether a restart-hint toast appears

items.title: Favorite a plugin via the star button — sorts to top and persists
preconditions: At least 2 plugins listed, target plugin not favorited
steps: Note the position of a non-favorite plugin
Click its star (.plg-fav-btn) — verify the star fills, the card gains the 'favorite' class and the item re-sorts to the top of the list
Switch to another prefs section and back to Plugins — verify the favorite persisted
Cleanup: click the star again to unfavorite and verify it returns to its alphabetical position
items.expected: Star toggles isFavorite via IPC; favorites sort before everything else; state survives re-render; failure shows the 'failed to favorite' toast

items.title: Install an available plugin — sends command to active terminal and closes modal
preconditions: An active Claude terminal exists in the current project
steps: Click the Install button (.plg-install-btn) on an available plugin
Verify toast 'Installing <name>…' and that the Preferences modal closes
Verify the active terminal received the command 'claude plugin install <name>'
Cleanup: uninstall the plugin afterwards (claude plugin uninstall <name>)
items.expected: Install path types the install command into the active terminal via window.terminalSendCommand and hides the modal; with no terminal available it instead shows the 'terminal not available' toast and stays open

automationHints.notes: Also test the negative path: with no terminal open, expect t('plugins.terminalNotAvailable') toast

items.title: Refresh plugins list shows spinner and result toast
steps: Click #plg-refresh-btn
Verify the button gets class 'spinning' and is disabled while the refresh runs
Wait for completion
items.expected: On success: toast 'refreshed' and the list re-renders; on failure: a 'failed to refresh' toast (with the error when available) and the button re-enables; spinner always removed afterwards

items.title: Uninstall an installed plugin via the trash button
preconditions: A disposable installed plugin (e.g. one installed by preferences-b-039)
steps: Click the uninstall trash button (.plg-uninstall-btn) on the installed plugin's card
Verify the 'Uninstalling <name>…' toast and that the modal closes
Verify the active terminal received 'claude plugin uninstall <name>'
Reopen Preferences → Plugins, refresh, and verify the plugin shows as Available again
items.expected: Uninstall sends the CLI command to the terminal and closes the modal; after the command completes and a refresh, plugin status returns to Available

automationHints.notes: Only uninstall a plugin the test installed — never the user's real plugins

items.title: 'Quit PATAPIM completely' button terminates the app
preconditions: Run against the DEV instance only, never the stable instance
No unsaved work in any terminal
steps: Open Preferences → Tray
Click 'Quit PATAPIM completely' (#pref-tray-quit)
Verify the dev Electron process exits fully (no tray icon, no zombie process)
Cleanup: relaunch the dev instance (./rebuild-dev-safe.bat) and confirm app_wait_ready succeeds
items.expected: App quits completely — process gone, tray icon removed; relaunch restores normal state

automationHints.notes: Was SKIPPED (destructive) in seed run; quitting kills the MCP connection mid-test — verify exit via process list from Bash

items.title: Toggle 'Launch PATAPIM at login' ON, verify OS autostart entry, then revert OFF

Cleanup: click the toggle again to turn it OFF and verify the autostart entry is removed
items.expected: Toggle reflects state; OS autostart entry created on enable and removed on disable; no stale entry left behind

automationHints.notes: Was SKIPPED (destructive) in seed run. Check HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run via Bash; dev instance may register a distinct entry

items.title: Tray tab renders three toggles and Quit button

Inspect the pane
