items.title: Toggle 'Close window to tray' and revert
steps: Click the toggle row for #pref-tray-close-to-tray
Click again to revert
items.expected: Switch flips OFF (grey) then back ON (amber); no error

items.title: Toggle 'Show OS notifications when window is hidden' and revert
steps: Click the toggle row for #pref-tray-show-notifications
Click again to revert
items.expected: Switch flips OFF then back ON; no error

items.title: Right-click the Claude sidebar button and verify all context-menu items
steps: Right-click #btn-start-claude
Read every item in the .terminal-context-menu that opens
Press Escape to close
items.expected: Menu shows exactly: 'Set as default', 'Hide this', 'Hide all from sidebar', divider, three checkbox rows 'Antigravity: Full permissions' / 'Claude: Full permissions' / 'Codex: Full permissions', divider, 'Add custom CLI…'. Escape closes the menu

automationHints.notes: Right-click needs computer_* with button:right. computer_* space:'image' maps to the LAST screenshot's pixel space — re-screenshot the button region immediately before each right-click

items.title: 'Hide all from sidebar' hides the AI button row and syncs with the Preferences toggle
steps: Right-click #btn-start-claude → click 'Hide all from sidebar'
Confirm all three AI buttons (and any custom CLI sidebar buttons) disappear
Open Preferences → Appearance and confirm #pref-ai-in-projectbar now reads OFF
CLEANUP: toggle #pref-ai-in-projectbar back ON and confirm the buttons return
items.expected: Menu item sets localStorage patapim-ai-in-projectbar='false' — same state as the Preferences toggle; custom CLI sidebar row also hides (renderSidebarButtons gates on getAIInProjectBar)

items.title: 'Show all' appears only when more than one provider is hidden, and restores everything
preconditions: All three providers visible at start
steps: Hide Codex via its menu ('Hide this')
Right-click Claude → confirm there is a 'Show Codex' item but NO 'Show all'
Hide Antigravity via its menu
Right-click Claude → confirm the menu now contains 'Show all' (plus 'Show Antigravity' and 'Show Codex')
Click 'Show all'
items.expected: 'Show all' is rendered only when hidden.length > 1; clicking it clears patapim-ai-hidden and all three buttons reappear

items.title: Hide Codex via 'Hide this', then restore it via 'Show Codex' from another provider's menu
steps: Right-click #btn-start-codex → click 'Hide this'
Confirm the Codex button disappears and the remaining buttons reflow
Right-click #btn-start-claude → confirm a new 'Show Codex' item appears between 'Hide this' and 'Hide all from sidebar'
Click 'Show Codex'
items.expected: Codex hides (added to localStorage patapim-ai-hidden), 'Show Codex' appears on other providers' menus, and clicking it restores all three buttons

automationHints.notes: i18n key ai.showProvider renders 'Show Codex'

items.title: Hiding the default provider reassigns the default; 'Hide this' is disabled on the last visible provider
preconditions: Claude is default, all three visible
steps: Right-click #btn-start-claude (the default) → click 'Hide this'
Right-click a remaining button and verify 'Set as default' shows ✓ on the first visible provider (Antigravity) — the default was reassigned
Hide Codex too, leaving only Antigravity
Right-click #btn-start-agy → verify 'Hide this' is greyed (opacity 0.5, unclickable)
CLEANUP: restore all via 'Show all' and set Claude back as default
items.expected: Hiding the default picks the first visible provider as new default; the last visible provider cannot be hidden (visibleCount <= 1 guard); the empty-hidden safety net never leaves zero visible buttons

items.title: Set Antigravity as default via context menu, verify indicator, then revert to Claude
steps: Right-click #btn-start-agy → confirm 'Set as default' is enabled (no checkmark)
Click 'Set as default'
Re-open the Antigravity menu → confirm 'Set as default' now greyed + ✓; Antigravity button shows the ai-btn-default border
Right-click #btn-start-claude → 'Set as default' is now enabled; click it to revert
Re-open the Claude menu → confirm greyed + ✓ and the amber ai-btn-default border on the Claude button
items.expected: Default switches to Antigravity (localStorage patapim-last-ai='agy', .ai-btn-default class moves) and reverts cleanly to Claude

automationHints.notes: Menu items have no unique selectors — click by position from a fresh region screenshot. Always restore Claude as default at the end

items.title: Toggle 'Claude: Full permissions' checkbox off and back on

Confirm the menu re-renders in place with '☐ Claude: Full permissions' while Antigravity/Codex stay ☑
Click it again to restore ☑
items.expected: Checkbox toggles per provider and re-renders the menu in place; localStorage patapim-ai-full-perms.claude flips false/true; other providers unaffected

automationHints.notes: Menu stays open after toggling (showMenu re-renders in place) — re-screenshot before the second click

items.title: Verify 'Set as default' is greyed with a checkmark on the current default provider

Inspect the 'Set as default' item
items.expected: 'Set as default' renders at opacity 0.5 with pointer-events none and a trailing ✓; all other items render at full opacity

items.title: Right-click the toolbar '+' AI terminal button opens the AI picker with providers, custom CLIs and 'Add custom CLI…'

items.title: Selecting a provider in the AI picker updates the last-AI and opens a new AI terminal

items.title: Custom CLI button right-click menu: Edit/Delete/Show in sidebar/Show in toolbar; toolbar toggle adds a toolbar button

items.title: Delete a custom CLI via right-click → Delete with confirm dialog

items.title: Add a custom Ollama model via 'Add custom model' and launch it
preconditions: Experimental customModels feature enabled (otherwise the menu item is absent)
Ollama installed locally with at least one pulled model
steps: Right-click an AI button → 'Add custom model'

Click Cancel — modal closes and the pending launch aborts (no broken command dumped into the terminal)
Re-trigger, restore the network, click Retry — install restarts from 'Checking…' and succeeds
items.expected: Failure classification (eacces/enetwork/eintegrity) appends the right hint; Cancel resolves the awaiting launch with ok:false; Retry reuses the same modal; Copy/Copied! round-trips

items.title: Clicking Beginner enables beginnerMode, shows chosen/faded feedback, auto-advances after ~600ms
steps: Click .dt-mode-card[data-mode='beginner']
Observe card states for ~0.6s
After the wizard, open the logo menu and check Beginner Mode state
Cleanup: toggle Beginner Mode back off
items.expected: Clicked card gets .dt-mode-chosen, the other .dt-mode-faded; EXPERIMENTAL_SET {feature:'beginnerMode', enabled:true} is invoked and 'recheck-beginner-mode' dispatched (UI simplifies immediately); flow auto-advances to the Theme screen.

automationHints.notes: Verify Beginner Mode checkbox later via #sidebar-logo-icon → logo menu.

items.title: Mode chooser screen renders Beginner and Advanced cards with taglines and 3 bullets each, Next hidden
preconditions: Tutorial overlay on screen 1 (advance once from Welcome)
steps: Advance from Welcome via Next
Inspect the two cards
items.expected: Title (tutorial.modeChoice.title) + sub body; .dt-mode-card[data-mode='beginner'] (sprout icon, beginner tagline, 3 bullets) and .dt-mode-card[data-mode='advanced'] (code icon, tagline, 3 bullets). The Next button is hidden (hideNext) — the only way forward is picking a card; the skip link is hidden too on this screen.

automationHints.notes: hideNext also hides the module-level skip (_skipEl display none).

items.title: Clicking Advanced disables beginnerMode and advances
preconditions: beginnerMode currently ON (from a prior pick) to prove the off-write
steps: Click .dt-mode-card[data-mode='advanced']
Wait for the auto-advance
Verify Beginner Mode is unchecked in the logo menu afterwards
items.expected: EXPERIMENTAL_SET beginnerMode=false; chosen/faded visual feedback; advance to Theme screen; full (non-beginner) UI active.

items.title: Click-anywhere-to-advance works on informational screens but is disabled on mode/theme/LLM/dictation screens
steps: On Theme (screen 2), click empty card padding (not a theme card) — verify no advance
On LLM (screen 3), click empty card padding — verify no advance
On a tour screen (e.g. Fundamentals or a spotlight card body area without links), click the card body — verify it advances
On a spotlight screen, click the dimmed overlay outside the cutout — verify it advances
items.expected: Screens 1/2/3/9 require an explicit choice (card click does not advance); informational screens advance on card click; spotlight screens also advance when the dimmed .dt-overlay area is clicked.

automationHints.notes: Clicking precise empty padding may need computer_mouse_click coordinates; buttons/selects/.dt-theme-card/.dt-llm-card-big/.dt-dict-skip/.dt-tryit-cta are excluded from click-to-advance.

items.title: Dot navigation jumps directly to any screen, with active/visited states
steps: Count the dots in .dt-dots
Click the 4th dot
Click the 1st dot
Observe transitions and dot classes after each click
items.expected: One dot per screen in the active range; clicking a dot transitions (slide animation) to that absolute screen; the active dot gets .active, earlier ones .visited; backward jumps work equally.

items.title: Final-screen CTA reads 'Let's Go!' and closes the flow
preconditions: Tutorial overlay on the last screen of its range (Fundamentals in full/tour flow)
steps: Navigate to the final screen
Verify the Next button caption
Click it
items.expected: Button reads 'Let's Go!' (tutorial.nav.letsGo) instead of 'Next'; clicking it hides the overlay and (in onboarding/full phase with no projects) hands off to the workspace finalize path; in tour phase it just closes.

items.title: Keyboard navigation: ArrowRight/Enter advance, ArrowLeft goes back, Back hidden on first screen
steps: On screen 0 verify the Back button is invisible (visibility:hidden)
Press ArrowRight — should advance
Press ArrowLeft — should return
Press Enter — should advance
Rapid-fire ArrowRight twice within ~200ms
items.expected: Arrow keys and Enter navigate; first screen hides Back; the _transitioning guard prevents double-advance from rapid keypresses (lands exactly one screen forward per completed transition).

automationHints.notes: Enter on the mode/theme screens still calls _nextScreen() — note that this skips the choice; record behavior.

items.title: Skip link label is phase-correct: 'I'll set up later' on setup screens vs 'Skip tutorial' on tour screens, and dismisses
preconditions: Tutorial overlay active (full flow so both ranges are reachable)
steps: On screens 0–3 read the .dt-skip text
Navigate to screen 4+ and read it again
Click the skip link
items.expected: Setup screens (index ≤ 3) show tutorial.nav.setupLater ('I'll set up later'); tour screens show tutorial.nav.skip ('Skip tutorial'); clicking hides the overlay. The link is hidden entirely on hideNext screens (mode chooser, dictation).

items.title: Completing the full onboarding persists across restart — wizard never replays
preconditions: Fresh profile; complete the wizard end-to-end (all screens + workspace pick)
steps: Finish onboarding normally
Quit and relaunch the app twice
Observe startup each time
items.expected: No onboarding overlay on subsequent launches; chosen settings (locale/theme/LLM/beginner mode/workspace) all retained; patapim-onboarded + patapim-tutorial-completed + settings.json flag all present.

items.title: Help → Interactive Tutorial launches the tour at Projects and a second invocation toggles it off
steps: Open the native Help menu (#menu-help) and click 'Interactive Tutorial'
Verify the tour starts on 'Projects = Folders' (screen 4 = TUTORIAL_FIRST) with the tour dot count, NOT on the language/setup screens
Open Help → Interactive Tutorial again
items.expected: First invocation shows the tour (phase 'tutorial'; setup screens 0–3 are excluded — those live in onboarding/Preferences); second invocation toggles the overlay OFF (isActive→hide). No workspace step at the end of the tour phase.

automationHints.notes: Help menu is a NATIVE Electron menu — not in the DOM; click items via computer_* in screen space (multi-monitor coordinate mapping caveats apply).

items.title: Legacy welcome picker (#welcome-screen-overlay) appears as fallback when onboarding ends with zero projects
preconditions: Onboarding/full flow finished WITHOUT a workspace pick (e.g. tour completed via 'Let's Go!' in full phase) and project list empty
steps: Complete/dismiss the flow with no projects configured
Inspect the screen
Click #welcome-card-home
Also verify (separately, with locale ja) the Japanese heading variant and the footer line render
items.expected: The non-dismissible #welcome-screen-overlay home-dir picker shows (welcome.heading, Open Folder/Home cards with descriptions, 'I don't know' link, welcome.footer); Enter/Space activates the focused card; picking home fires PROJECT_SELECTED → overlay hides and finalizeOnboarding marks completion.

items.title: Play Tutorial CTA appears in the tab bar after onboarding completes and replays the tour
steps: Look at the terminal tab bar for the Play Tutorial CTA
Click it
Skip the tour
items.expected: CTA is revealed by tabBar.showPlayTutorialCta() right after finalize; clicking it runs devTutorial.showTutorial() (tour screens only); dismissing returns to normal UI. CTA does not appear in /desktop remote view.

automationHints.notes: CTA selector lives in terminalTabBar.js showPlayTutorialCta() — confirm its element id/class via ui_state before clicking.

items.title: Tutorial show() defers while the force-update overlay is visible
steps: Make .force-update-overlay visible at startup
Observe that the tutorial does not appear on top of it
Clear the overlay and wait ~2-4s
items.expected: show() retries every 2s while the force-update overlay is displayed; the tutorial only appears after the overlay is gone — never stacked over the updater.

items.title: Picking Light applies the theme app-wide immediately and auto-advances after ~700ms
steps: Click .dt-theme-card[data-theme='light']
Observe the app behind the overlay and the card state
Wait for auto-advance to the LLM screen
Cleanup: navigate Back (dot or ArrowLeft) and re-pick Dark, or restore later in Preferences → Appearance
items.expected: document.documentElement data-theme='light' immediately (whole app + open terminals retheme via applyThemeToAllTerminals), patapim-theme persisted, THEME_SET sent; card gains .dt-theme-selected/.dt-theme-chosen; auto-advance to screen 3.

automationHints.notes: Screenshot before/after to verify the light/cream retheme behind the overlay.

items.title: Theme screen renders Dark/Light preview cards with the current theme pre-selected
steps: Reach screen 2 (pick a mode on screen 1)
Inspect the two theme cards and the title/body/hint copy
items.expected: Two .dt-theme-card elements (data-theme dark/light) each with a miniature sidebar+terminal preview, names (tutorial.theme.dark/light) and descriptions; the card matching localStorage patapim-theme carries .dt-theme-selected; hint line (tutorial.theme.hint) shown muted.

items.title: Projects spotlight: clip-path cutout, pulsing ring, highlighted sidebar buttons, arrowed card
preconditions: Tour active on screen 4 (Help → Interactive Tutorial lands here)
steps: Launch the tour
Inspect the overlay, ring, and the #projects-header area
Read the card copy
items.expected: Overlay gets .dt-overlay-spotlight (lighter dim) with a clip-path cutout around #projects-header; .dt-spot-ring pulses around it; #btn-add-project and #btn-quick-create-project carry .dt-highlight-pulse; the 380px .dt-card-spotlight sits near the target with a directional arrow; copy shows tutorial.projects.* (title, Open Folder/New Project strongs, hint).

items.title: Browser and Remote Access spotlight screens render, including the animated remote demo scene
steps: Advance to screen 7 (Browser) — verify spotlight on .btn-browser-toggle and tutorial.browser.* copy incl. the example line

Advance to screen 8 (Remote) — verify spotlight on .btn-remote-toggle and the animated desktop→waves→phone/laptop demo with localized labels ('Your computer', 'Phone', 'Laptop') and demo terminal lines (building/compiled/deploying/waiting)
items.expected: Both screens spotlight their toolbar buttons; the remote demo scene renders with all five tutorial.remote.demo* strings and the three device labels; body/hint copy present.

items.title: Quick Tasks and Terminals spotlight screens target the right toolbar buttons with correct copy
steps: Advance to screen 5 (Quick Tasks) — verify ring/highlight on #btn-quick-task and tutorial.quickTasks.* copy
Advance to screen 6 (Terminals) — verify ring/highlight on .btn-claude-terminal and tutorial.terminals.* copy incl. the right-click tip
items.expected: Each screen relocates the cutout + .dt-spot-ring + .dt-highlight-pulse to its own target; card repositions near the target; titles/bodies match the respective locale keys.

items.title: Spotlight screen with a missing target falls back to a centered card without crashing
preconditions: A spotlight target hidden from the DOM (e.g. hide the browser toggle via toolbar button customization, or run in a state where .btn-remote-toggle is absent)
steps: Advance to the screen whose target is missing
Observe rendering
items.expected: No exception; the card renders centered (overlay flex-centered, no cutout/ring) because the targetEl branch falls through to centered mode; navigation continues normally.

items.title: Language dropdown lists 5 locales and switching to Español retranslates live
steps: Open #dt-lang-select and verify options: English, 日本語, Español, Français, Português
Select 'Español'
Advance to the next screen and check its title
Cleanup: switch back to English via the same dropdown (Back to screen 0)
items.expected: Locale applies immediately: patapim-locale='es' persisted, LANGUAGE_SET sent, DOM behind the overlay retranslated, Next/Back button captions update to Spanish, and subsequent screens (mode chooser title etc.) render in Spanish.

automationHints.notes: ui_fill on a <select> sets value; dispatch change may be needed — verify via screenshot that captions changed.

items.title: Welcome screen renders hero logo, gradient wordmark title, language dropdown and inline Next pill
preconditions: Tutorial/onboarding overlay visible on screen 0 (dev auto-show or fresh first run)
steps: Inspect the welcome card
items.expected: Inlined base64 PATAPIM logo image renders (no broken-image icon), title 'Welcome to PATAPIM' with gradient wordmark, #dt-lang-select dropdown with chevron, and the Next button relocated inline next to the dropdown (.dt-welcome-lang row); the bottom nav row is hidden; Back is not visible on the first screen.

automationHints.notes: Logo is a data URI (welcomeLogoData.js) — regression-watch for the historical missing-asset bug on packaged builds.

items.title: Language picked in onboarding persists after finishing the wizard and across an app restart
steps: Pick 日本語 in #dt-lang-select
Finish or skip the rest of the flow
Restart the instance
Inspect main UI language
Cleanup: restore English in Preferences → Language
items.expected: After restart the whole UI (sidebar, preferences) renders in Japanese; patapim-locale survived the restart.

automationHints.notes: Restart dev instance via rebuild-dev-safe.bat; verify with app_screenshot.

items.title: Esc hint shown on Welcome screen only in onboarding phase, absent in full/dev flow
preconditions: Ability to trigger both phases: onboarding (fresh first run or DevTools window.__patapimTutorial.onboarding()) and full flow (dev auto-show)
steps: Trigger onboarding phase, inspect screen 0 for the esc hint line (tutorial.welcome.escHint)
Dismiss; trigger the full flow (dev-instance auto-show)
Inspect screen 0 again
items.expected: Onboarding phase shows .dt-welcome-eschint ('press Esc…' hint) under the language row; the full/dev phase omits it entirely.

automationHints.notes: Onboarding phase needs DevTools console hook (app_evaluate is CSP-blocked) — human assist for that half.

items.title: Next on the LLM screen (onboarding phase) shows the inline workspace picker as a 5th step
preconditions: Onboarding phase active (fresh first run, or window.__patapimTutorial.onboarding() from DevTools)
On the LLM screen with ≥1 installed LLM
steps: Click Next on the LLM screen
Inspect the rendered step, dots row, and nav
items.expected: Same card slides to a workspace step: title 'Pick your workspace' (welcome.heading), subtitle, two .dt-ws-card buttons (Open Folder with folder icon + 'Browse your system'; Home (~/) with tilde icon) and the .dt-ws-dontknow ghost link ('I don't know — just use home'). A 5th dot is active, prior 4 dots visited; Next hidden, Back visible, skip link hidden. Note: on the LLM screen the CTA read 'Next', not 'Let's Go!', because this step follows.

automationHints.notes: Onboarding phase trigger needs DevTools console (app_evaluate CSP-blocked) or a fresh stable profile.

items.title: Workspace step → Home card and 'I don't know' link both select the home directory
steps: Click the Home (~/) card (or, on a second run, the 'I don't know — just use home' link)
Observe the overlay and the project list
items.expected: Both paths send WELCOME_USE_HOME_FOLDER; PROJECT_SELECTED arrives, overlay closes, the user's home directory becomes the workspace/project, onboarding finalizes.

automationHints.notes: No OS dialog involved, so this half is agent-drivable once the step is visible.

items.title: Workspace step → Open Folder opens the OS picker; choosing a folder closes onboarding and selects the project
preconditions: A scratch folder available to pick
steps: Click the Open Folder card
In the OS dialog, choose a scratch folder
Observe the overlay and project list
Cleanup: remove the scratch project from the project list
items.expected: SELECT_PROJECT_FOLDER opens the native dialog; on pick, PROJECT_SELECTED fires → the overlay hides, the folder appears as the active project, onboarding finalizes (markers set, Play Tutorial CTA revealed), and the legacy #welcome-screen-overlay never appears.

items.title: Back from the workspace step returns to the LLM screen with correct dots
steps: Click Back
Inspect the screen and dot states
Click Next again to confirm the workspace step is re-enterable
items.expected: Returns to the LLM screen (dot 4 active, workspace dot no longer active), PROJECT_SELECTED listener unsubscribed (_leaveWorkspaceStep); Next re-enters the workspace step cleanly. Clicking the 5th dot directly also jumps to the workspace step.

items.title: Canceling the Open Folder OS dialog leaves the wizard on the workspace step
steps: Click the Open Folder card
Press Cancel/Esc in the OS folder dialog
Observe the wizard
items.expected: No PROJECT_SELECTED fires; the wizard stays on the workspace step, fully interactive (cards clickable again); no crash, no premature finalize.

items.title: Pending received message survives an app restart (bubble re-renders on launch)
preconditions: A pending clipboard-sync message exists (received but neither pasted nor dismissed)
steps: Receive a message from another device; do NOT paste or dismiss it
Quit and relaunch PATAPIM (dev instance: ./rebuild-dev-safe.bat or normal restart)
After the renderer loads, check the bottom-right of the terminal area
items.expected: ClipboardSyncUI._loadPendingMessage() invokes CLIPBOARD_SYNC_GET_PENDING on init — if the main process persists the pending message, the received bubble re-renders after restart; if it does not reappear, record whether pending messages are intended to be session-scoped

automationHints.notes: Persistence depends on the main-process clipboardSync store — outcome documents actual behavior either way

items.title: Picker dismisses via × button, overlay outside-click, and Escape
steps: Click the × button (data-action='close') — picker closes
Reopen; click the dimmed overlay outside the panel — picker closes
Reopen; with the overlay focused, press Escape — picker closes
items.expected: All three paths remove the overlay and clear pendingSelectionText; Escape is handled by the overlay's own keydown (overlay is focused on open) so it works even when document-level chords are flaky

automationHints.notes: Picker has no stable selectors — drive by coordinates from a screenshot

items.title: Ctrl+Shift+Alt+V paste shortcut: 'no pending' error and multi-line confirm guard
steps: With NO pending message, press Ctrl+Shift+Alt+V — verify error toast 'clipboardSync.noPending'
Receive a MULTI-LINE message from another device, focus a terminal, press Ctrl+Shift+Alt+V
Verify a native confirm 'Paste multi-line text into terminal?' with a ≤120-char preview
Cancel — nothing is pasted and the pending message survives
Repeat and accept — text pastes with trailing newlines stripped (no auto-execution of a final empty line)
items.expected: noPending error when empty; multi-line terminal paste is gated behind confirm(); trailing \r\n stripped so the last line does not auto-execute

automationHints.notes: Native confirm() blocks the renderer — needs computer_* interaction

items.title: Received message bubble: 'Paste' inserts into the active terminal/editor; × dismisses
preconditions: A second PATAPIM instance/device sends this machine a clipboard-sync message
steps: From the other device, send 'RECEIVE_TEST_999' to this machine
Verify the bubble appears bottom-right with title (clipboardSync.receivedTitle) and 'From <device>' (clipboardSync.receivedFrom)
With a terminal focused, click the blue 'Paste' button
Verify the text lands in the terminal input, the bubble disappears, and the 'clipboardSync.pasted' toast shows
Have another message sent; this time click the × (dismiss)
Verify the pending message is cleared (bubble removed, no paste)
items.expected: CLIPBOARD_SYNC_PENDING_CHANGED renders the bubble; Paste routes to focused field → editor → terminal and clears pending; dismiss invokes CLIPBOARD_SYNC_CLEAR_PENDING; with nowhere to paste, error toast clipboardSync.noPasteTarget

automationHints.notes: Bubble buttons are data-action='paste' / 'dismiss' inside an unselectored absolute div

items.title: Ctrl+Shift+Alt+C with a terminal selection opens the device picker (or no-selection / no-targets states)
preconditions: Computer Control OFF (its emergency-stop layer consumes the Ctrl+Alt+Shift chord)
Terminal open in the test project
No previous clipboard-sync target used this session
steps: With NOTHING selected, press Ctrl+Shift+Alt+C — verify the error toast 'clipboardSync.noSelection'
Run 'echo CLIPBOARD_SYNC_TEST_TEXT_12345' and drag-select the output line
Press Ctrl+Shift+Alt+C
Verify the picker overlay appears with title (clipboardSync.pickerTitle) and subtitle
If the account has no other devices, verify the dashed empty box with 'clipboardSync.noTargets'
items.expected: No selection → error toast; selection + no prior target → sendSelectionToLastTarget gets 'missing-target' and opens the picker overlay (z-index 75) listing targets or the noTargets empty state

automationHints.notes: Seed BLOCKED: chord never reached the renderer while Computer Control was active. Picker elements have no ids/classes — locate visually via screenshot. Cmd works as the mod key on macOS

items.title: Picker rows show device status and 'Last used' chip; clicking a target sends and toasts 'Sent to <name>'
preconditions: Signed in with at least one other online device
Picker open with a selection (see remote-cowork-026)
steps: Inspect target rows: device name (or 'clipboardSync.unnamedTarget'), platform tag, and status line ('Online' / 'Seen recently' heartbeat / 'Offline')
If a target was used before, verify the blue 'Last used' chip on that row
Click an online target row
Verify the success toast 'Sent to <deviceName>' and the picker closes
On the receiving device, verify the received bubble appears
items.expected: CLIPBOARD_SYNC_SEND_TEXT with targetDeviceToken succeeds → clipboardSync.sentTo toast, picker removed; on failure the picker stays and an error toast shows clipboardSync.sendFailed or the server error

automationHints.notes: Status mapping: online→targetOnline, status==='heartbeat'→targetHeartbeat, else targetOffline

items.title: Second Ctrl+Shift+Alt+C reuses the last target and toasts 'Sent' without showing the picker
preconditions: A successful picker send already happened this session (last target remembered)
steps: Select new text in the terminal
Press Ctrl+Shift+Alt+C
Verify the 'clipboardSync.sent' success toast appears immediately with NO picker overlay
items.expected: sendSelectionToLastTarget() succeeds via useLastTarget:true; picker only reappears if the last target is gone (missing-target)

items.title: CoworkView renders in beginner mode with empty state, chips and composer
steps: Enable Beginner Mode via the logo menu: click #sidebar-logo-icon then #logo-menu-beginner (use the DOM selector — pixel clicks on the menu are unreliable)
Verify #cowork-view covers the terminal area
items.expected: Light-theme chat: header 'Claude' + sparkle logo, empty state 'What can I help you build?' with subtitle 'Describe what you want to do — Claude takes it from here.', three suggestion chips ('Explain what this project does', 'Help me fix a problem', 'Add a new feature'), composer .cw-input with placeholder 'Message Claude…', send button, and 'Show terminal' top-right. Side effect: a Claude session auto-launches in the underlying terminal

automationHints.notes: Cleanup: disable Beginner Mode via #logo-menu-beginner again; the auto-launched Claude REPL stays idle

items.title: 'Show terminal' peek reveals the raw terminal; 'Back to chat' restores the chat
steps: Click '#cowork-view .cw-peek' ('Show terminal')
Verify #cowork-view hides, the raw terminal is revealed, and #cowork-peek-fab ('Back to chat') appears in the terminal tab bar's action row
Click #cowork-peek-fab
items.expected: Peek: chat display:none, docked controls return to the tab bar, peek FAB visible at the far right of the action row; Back to chat: #cowork-view restored (display:flex) with state intact, FAB hidden

items.title: Suggestion chip click populates the composer and enables Send
preconditions: Beginner mode active, CoworkView empty state visible
steps: Click the first .cw-suggest-chip ('Explain what this project does')
Inspect .cw-input and the send button
items.expected: .cw-input value equals the chip text, input is focused, .cw-send becomes enabled (cw-has-text class on root); empty state remains until a message is actually sent

automationHints.notes: Cleanup: clear .cw-input afterwards so nothing gets sent

items.title: Composer send mechanics: Enter sends, Shift+Enter newlines, optimistic bubble + working indicator
preconditions: Beginner mode active, Claude session bound (not in 'Starting Claude…' state)
steps: Type 'say the word PING and nothing else' in .cw-input
Press Shift+Enter — verify a newline is added, message NOT sent
Press Enter — verify the message sends
Observe the chat immediately and over the next seconds
items.expected: Send disabled when input empty; on send: input clears and shrinks, an optimistic right-aligned user bubble appears, 'Claude is working…' dots row shows while processing, then the assistant reply renders as markdown prose with the sparkle avatar; the optimistic bubble reconciles with the real transcript (no duplicates)

automationHints.notes: Sends a real prompt to Claude — keep it trivial. Delivery uses separate text + \r writes with retry-until-confirmed (max 2 retries at 3.5s)

items.title: Attachments: drag-drop a file and paste an image become removable chips; sent with the message
preconditions: Beginner mode active, CoworkView visible (not peeking)
steps: Drag a local image file from Explorer onto the chat
Verify a .cw-att chip appears in the composer strip with thumbnail + filename, and Send enables even with empty text
Copy an image to the clipboard and press Ctrl+V in .cw-input — verify a second chip appears (saved to a temp file)
Click one chip's × (.cw-att-x) — verify it is removed
Send with the remaining attachment + text 'what is this image?'
Verify the user bubble shows the attachment chip and the delivered terminal line included the quoted file path
items.expected: addAttachments() owns drops in beginner mode (no raw path typed into the hidden terminal); paste of image/* items is consumed; × removes; on send, paths (quoted if containing spaces) are delivered before the typed text while the bubble shows clean text + chips

automationHints.notes: OS-level file drag needs computer_*; image paste path uses IPC.SAVE_QUICK_TASK_IMAGE

items.title: Plan/Telegram/mic dock into the composer and toolbox icons into the header; all restore on exit
preconditions: Beginner mode toggled on from advanced mode
steps: Enter beginner mode and inspect the composer bar and chat header

Verify #plan-mode-footer and #telegram-route-footer render as chips in the composer's left dock, the mic (.btn-dictation-float) in the right dock, and the toolbox icons (.btn-browser-toggle, .btn-sessions-toggle, .btn-notifs-toggle, .btn-remote-toggle, .btn-report-bug, .btn-toolbar-menu, .btn-custom-toolbar-toggle) in .cw-dock-tools
Toggle the Plan chip — verify it works (checked state colors) and still controls plan mode
Exit beginner mode
Verify every relocated control returned to its original parent (tab bar / floating position) with classes cw-docked/cw-tool-docked removed
items.expected: dockControls() moves the real elements (same handlers/state) and undockControls() restores them via _cwReturnParent; no duplicated or orphaned buttons in either mode

automationHints.notes: Also verify dictation intercept: while chat shown, dictated text goes to .cw-input, not the PTY

items.title: Activity cards expand/collapse (state persists across re-renders), code-copy button, scroll-to-bottom FAB
preconditions: Beginner mode active with a conversation that produced tool activity and a code block (e.g. ask Claude to read a file and show a snippet)
steps: Click a .cw-act-head on a collapsed activity card — verify it expands listing .cw-step rows; chevron rotates
Click a step with data-has-detail='1' — verify its detail <pre> expands
Wait for a transcript update (or send another message) — verify expanded cards STAY expanded
Hover a code block and click its .cw-copy button — verify it flashes a check and the code is in the clipboard
Scroll the messages up — verify the .cw-scroll-bottom FAB fades in; click it — smooth-scrolls to bottom and FAB hides
items.expected: expandedActivities/expandedSteps sets keep open state across incremental re-renders; copy button writes code text to clipboard with 1.2s check feedback; FAB visible only when >120px from bottom

automationHints.notes: Multi-step runs collapse into one card showing the latest step + 'N steps' count chip; spinner while running, check when done

items.title: Changing terminal-list orientation on the host live-mirrors to connected /desktop remote clients (remote-layout-changed)
preconditions: A /desktop remote-view client connected (e.g. via the remote switcher from another device, or a browser on http://localhost:31415/desktop with auth)
steps: With the remote /desktop client showing the workspace, note the current terminal-list layout
On the host, switch the tab-bar layout via right-click → Vertical (or View menu Terminal List radios)
Verify the remote /desktop client re-renders to the vertical list layout without a manual reload
Switch the host to Horizontal, then Disabled, verifying the remote view mirrors each orientation ('v' | 'h' | 'disabled')
items.expected: Every orientation change broadcasts remote-layout-changed and the /desktop client's layout follows the host live

automationHints.notes: broadcastLayout in main/remoteServer.js fans out to /desktop websocket clients; agent can host the client in an MCP browser session

items.title: Connection-lost banner and automatic reconnect after a server restart
preconditions: Desktop reachable to restart the remote server
steps: With the phone session live, click 'RESTART SERVER' on the desktop Remote panel
On the phone, verify the connection-lost UI (#connection-lost) and/or mobile.connection.retrying toast appears
Wait for the server to come back
Verify the phone either auto-reconnects (terminal resumes) or shows mobile.connection.reconnectFailed / mobile.connection.unreachable with a manual retry path
Verify terminal interaction works again after reconnect
items.expected: Loss is detected and communicated (no silent dead terminal); retry loop reconnects once the WS endpoint is back; mobile.toast.connectionLost shown for in-flight actions during the outage

automationHints.notes: Can be driven with the embedded browser as the 'phone'; note the tunnel URL may change across restart — localhost:31415 avoids that

items.title: Mobile passkey: register (waiting-for-approval flow), login button, and delete
preconditions: Real phone with WebAuthn support connected to the tunnel URL
steps: On the phone, open Settings → PassKeys section, tap 'Add passkey' (mobile.passkey.add)

Complete the WebAuthn ceremony — verify 'Registering…' then either 'waiting for approval' screen (#pending-approval) with waiting message/hint and a 'Cancel and use token' button, or immediate 'registered' toast
Approve on the desktop (Remote panel pending section) — phone proceeds to connected state

Click X to hide to tray
Right-click tray → Show Window
items.expected: Window reappears with the exact same renderer state (terminals, tabs, scrollback) — hide does not reload the renderer; sessions were also saved to disk on hide (crash-safety save in the close handler)

automationHints.notes: Compare terminal_read output before hide and after restore.

items.title: Clicking X with close-to-tray disabled saves sessions and actually closes
preconditions: Tray menu checkbox 'Close to tray' (preferences.tray.closeToTray) unticked
Run against dev instance or accept stable restart
steps: Untick 'Close to tray' in the tray context menu
Click the window X
Observe the process
items.expected: Close intercepted ONCE to await sessionPersistence.saveState (skipped on dev), then window closes for real; window-all-closed with no trayKeepAlive=false path... tray still installed keeps the process alive tray-resident on Windows only if tray installed — with closeToTray off the window closes but process stays tray-resident (trayKeepAlive true, isQuitting false). Verify window gone, tray icon still present, 'Show Window' restores it

automationHints.notes: Subtle: with tray installed the process never dies on X regardless of closeToTray; only the hide-vs-destroy of the window differs. Restoring after destroy exercises recreateMainWindow.

items.title: Close-to-tray setting persists across restart and round-trips between tray menu and Preferences
steps: Untick 'Close to tray' in the tray context menu
Open Preferences → Tray and confirm the same setting shows unticked
Quit fully and relaunch
Right-click the tray icon and check the checkbox state
items.expected: settingsStore.patch('tray', {closeToTray:false}) persists; tray menu and Preferences pane stay in sync (settingsStore.onChange triggers a menu refresh, debounced 200ms); state survives restart

automationHints.notes: Restore closeToTray to enabled (default) after the test.

items.title: Dev and stable instances coexist with fully isolated ports and userData
steps: Run ./rebuild-dev-safe.bat to start the dev instance
Confirm both windows are open simultaneously
Check dev window title is 'PATAPIM [DEV]' and stable is 'PATAPIM'
Verify ports: stable remote server on http://127.0.0.1:31415, dev on http://127.0.0.1:31416; CDP 9222 (stable, only with PATAPIM_DEBUG) vs 9223 (dev)
Verify %APPDATA%/PATAPIM-dev/ exists and is separate from %APPDATA%/PATAPIM/, including patapim-dev.pid
items.expected: Both run concurrently — dev's userData rename happens BEFORE requestSingleInstanceLock so the locks don't collide; titles, AppUserModelIds (com.patapim.ide vs .dev), MCP token files (mcp-token vs mcp-token-dev), ports and userData are all distinct; stable sessions are never overwritten by dev (saveState skipped on dev)

automationHints.notes: app_* tools take instance:'dev'|'stable'. Check both respond with their own port/title.

items.title: Dev instance renders amber [dev] branding in the sidebar
steps: Look at the dev window's sidebar logo area
Compare with the stable window
items.expected: Renderer received 'patapim-instance-variant' IPC with 'dev': sidebar logo shows 'patapim [dev]' in amber; stable shows plain 'patapim'. Window icon uses build/icon-dev.ico

automationHints.notes: Screenshot both instances and compare the logo region.

items.title: Acknowledging all notifications clears toasts, tooltip and menu entries (badge → 0)
preconditions: 2 pending tray notifications with live OS toasts in the Action Center
steps: Open the window and view each flagged terminal (or archive the notifications in the bell panel)
Check Windows Action Center for the toasts
Hover the tray icon and right-click it
items.expected: When the renderer reports unreadCount 0, setBadge(0) closes all live OS toasts programmatically, clears pending menu entries, and the tooltip returns to bare 'PATAPIM'

items.title: 3+ notifications within 2s coalesce into a single summary toast
preconditions: 3 terminals able to fire attention notifications near-simultaneously
steps: Hide the window to tray
Trigger 3+ terminal-attention notifications within 2 seconds (e.g. three scheduled one-shot commands at the same minute)
Observe the OS notification(s)
items.expected: Exactly one native toast appears, titled with tray.notif.summaryCount (count=N) and body listing up to 3 distinct titles joined by ', ' (+'…' if more); clicking it shows the main window

items.title: Tray notifications respect the showNotifications=false setting
steps: Disable tray notifications in Preferences
Hide the window
Trigger a terminal-attention event
Watch for OS toasts, then right-click the tray icon
items.expected: No native toast fires (notify() returns early), but the ⚠ pending entry still appears in the tray menu and the tooltip suffix updates (addNotification path is independent of the toast preference)

items.title: Second launch of stable focuses the existing window instead of duplicating
preconditions: Stable instance already running with window visible
steps: Launch a second stable copy (double-click the shortcut or run electron.exe . again)
Observe both the new process and the existing window
items.expected: Second process fails requestSingleInstanceLock and exits immediately (process.exit(0), no window flash); existing instance receives second-instance: window restored from minimized if needed, shown, and focused

automationHints.notes: Count electron PIDs before/after via Get-Process. Beware: dist/main.js(c) overrides source for the main process.

items.title: Second launch while tray-only with destroyed window recreates the window
preconditions: Stable running tray-resident with the window destroyed (close X with closeToTray off, do not quit)
steps: With the process alive but window destroyed, double-click the PATAPIM shortcut
Observe
items.expected: second-instance handler hits the else-branch: createWindow() rebuilds the window WITHOUT re-registering IPC (no duplicate ipcMain handlers — terminal input must not double); window appears functional

automationHints.notes: After recreate, type one char in a terminal and confirm it is not echoed twice (duplicate-listener regression check).

items.title: --start-minimized boots tray-resident without showing the window
steps: Launch with the flag: <electron.exe> <app path> --start-minimized (mirrors the autostart command)
Watch the screen for ~15s
Confirm tray icon appeared
Left-click the tray icon to open the window
items.expected: No window is shown at any point (ready-to-show handler returns early when startMinimized && tray installed); no focus steal; first-ever minimized boot fires a one-time toast 'PATAPIM is running' with body explaining the tray icon (tray.firstAutostartShown then persisted so it never repeats)

automationHints.notes: The first-time toast only fires once per profile — reset tray.firstAutostartShown in settings to retest.

items.title: --start-minimized falls back to showing the window when the tray failed to install
preconditions: tray.enabled set to false in settings (tray init skipped)
steps: Disable the tray in Preferences
Quit fully
Launch with --start-minimized
items.expected: Because trayManager.isInstalled() is false, the ready-to-show handler shows the window anyway — the user is never left with a running app they cannot reach

items.title: 'Show Window' menu item restores a hidden window
steps: Right-click the tray icon
Click the item labelled with tray.showWindow ('Show Window')
items.expected: Main window becomes visible and focused; if it was minimized it is restored; if it had been destroyed, recreateMainWindow rebuilds it via createWindow()

automationHints.notes: Native context menu — needs computer_* right-click; cannot be driven by ui_click.

items.title: Quit from tray fully exits the app and removes the tray icon
preconditions: Run against the DEV instance to avoid killing the user's working stable instance
steps: Right-click the dev tray icon
Click the tray.quit item
Wait 5s, then check Task Manager / process list for the dev electron process
Hover where the tray icon was
items.expected: app.isQuitting set → close-to-tray is bypassed, before-quit saves sessions synchronously and drains PTYs, will-quit destroys the tray; process exits on FIRST click (no second-click-needed regression) and no ghost tray icon remains

automationHints.notes: Verify process death via PowerShell Get-Process. The PID file %APPDATA%/PATAPIM-dev/patapim-dev.pid identifies the dev process.

items.title: 'Open Scheduled Tasks panel' shows the window and opens the panel
steps: Right-click tray icon → Scheduled Tasks submenu
Click the tray.openSchedPanel item
items.expected: Window shows and focuses, then receives IPC OPEN_SCHEDULED_TASKS_PANEL — the Scheduled Tasks panel is open in the renderer

automationHints.notes: Screenshot after click to confirm the panel is visible.

items.title: Clicking a scheduled task in the tray submenu runs it immediately
preconditions: One enabled recurring scheduled task that produces an observable effect (e.g. writes to a terminal)
steps: Right-click tray icon → Scheduled Tasks submenu
Click the task entry
Check the target terminal/project for the task having executed
items.expected: scheduledTasksManager.runTaskNow(task.id) fires: the task runs now without waiting for its cron time; menu refreshes afterwards with the updated next-run time

automationHints.notes: Verify execution via terminal_read on the target terminal rather than menu state.

items.title: Pending notification entries appear at the top of the menu and focus their terminal when clicked
steps: Right-click the tray icon
Confirm a '⚠ <title>' item appears above 'Show Window'
Trigger a second ping for the SAME terminal and reopen the menu (should still be one entry)
Click the ⚠ entry
items.expected: Entries keyed by terminalId coalesce duplicates; clicking removes the entry, refreshes tooltip/menu, shows the window, and sends IPC FOCUS_TERMINAL with that terminalId+projectPath so the right terminal is focused

automationHints.notes: Generate the ping by letting a Claude terminal finish while window hidden, or via the notifications panel test hooks.

items.title: Scheduled Tasks submenu lists tasks with relative next-run labels
preconditions: At least 2 enabled recurring scheduled tasks with different next-run times (one within minutes, one days away)
steps: Right-click the tray icon
Open the tray.scheduledTasks submenu
Read each task entry's label and parenthesised time
Optionally create a task with nextRunAt in the past and re-check
items.expected: Tasks sorted by nextRunAt ascending, max 8 shown; text truncated at 40 chars with ellipsis; time suffix uses tray.inLessThanMin / tray.inMinutes / tray.inHours / tray.inDays as appropriate, and tray.overdue for past-due tasks

items.title: Tools submenu lists tray-checked toolbar buttons and runs a command headlessly
preconditions: A project with a toolbar.json button of type 'command' marked 'Show in tray' (observable side effect, e.g. touches a file)
steps: Right-click the tray icon
Confirm a tray.tools submenu exists and contains the button's label
Click the tool entry
Verify the side effect in the project directory
Add an identically-named tool in a second project and reopen the menu
items.expected: Tool runs as a detached child process with cwd = its project path, no terminal opens, output discarded; duplicate labels are deduplicated (first occurrence wins); submenu is absent entirely when no project has tray tools

automationHints.notes: Use a command like 'echo hi > tray-tool-proof.txt'; check the file afterwards. url-type tools call shell.openExternal, file-type call shell.openPath (relative paths resolve against the project).

items.title: Empty scheduler shows disabled 'no scheduled tasks' placeholder in submenu
steps: Right-click the tray icon
Open the Scheduled Tasks submenu
items.expected: Submenu contains a single greyed-out (enabled:false) item with the tray.noSchedTasks string, a separator, and the tray.openSchedPanel item

items.title: Menu header shows scheduled-task count subtitle in all three plural forms
steps: With zero enabled cron tasks, right-click the tray icon and read line 2 of the menu
Create one recurring scheduled task (Scheduled panel), reopen the tray menu
Create a second recurring task, reopen the menu
items.expected: Line 2 (disabled item) cycles through tray.scheduledTasksNone ('No scheduled tasks' style), tray.scheduledTasksCountOne, and tray.scheduledTasksCount with count=2. One-shot commands and disabled tasks are excluded from the count

items.title: Tray menu labels rebuild on app language change
steps: Open Preferences and switch the app language (e.g. English → Spanish)
Without restarting, right-click the tray icon
Read 'Show Window', 'Scheduled Tasks', 'Quit' and the checkbox labels
Hover the icon to read the tooltip
items.expected: onLocaleChange handler rebuilds menu and tooltip: all tray.* strings render in the new locale immediately; the 'PATAPIM' brand header stays untranslated

items.title: Left-click tray icon toggles window visibility (Windows)

Left-click again — window should show, restore from minimized if needed, and take focus
Minimize the window via titlebar, then left-click the tray icon
items.expected: Click 1 hides the focused visible window; click 2 shows + focuses it; clicking while minimized restores (win.restore()) rather than just showing

items.title: Tray icon appears on launch with tooltip 'PATAPIM' (stable)
steps: Launch PATAPIM stable
Locate the PATAPIM icon in the Windows notification area (expand hidden icons if needed)
Hover the icon to read the tooltip
items.expected: Tray icon is present (PATAPIM mark, 16px, color version on Windows); tooltip reads exactly 'PATAPIM' with no suffix when there are no pending notifications

items.title: Dev instance tray uses dev icon and tooltip 'PATAPIM [DEV]'
preconditions: Dev instance running (PATAPIM_INSTANCE=dev, e.g. via rebuild-dev-safe.bat)
steps: Start the dev instance alongside stable
Find the second PATAPIM tray icon
Hover it to read the tooltip
Right-click and check the first (disabled) header line
items.expected: Dev tray uses build/icon-dev.png (visually distinct from stable); tooltip and menu header read 'PATAPIM [DEV]'; the brand label is not translated in any locale

items.title: Tooltip gains attention suffix when terminals need attention

steps: Hide the window (close-to-tray)
Trigger one terminal-attention notification
Hover the tray icon and read the tooltip
Trigger a second notification from a different terminal and re-read the tooltip
Open the window and view both terminals so attention clears, then re-read
items.expected: With 1 pending: tooltip is 'PATAPIM — ' + tray.tooltipAttentionOne text; with 2+: tray.tooltipAttention with the count; after acknowledging all (badge count 0) the suffix disappears and pending menu entries are cleared

items.title: Tray falls back to a programmatic square when icon files are missing

steps: Temporarily rename build/icon.png and build/icon.ico out of the way
Launch the app
Inspect the tray area
CLEANUP: restore the icon files
items.expected: Tray still appears as a 16x16 flat square (cream for stable, orange for dev) instead of failing to init — close-to-tray and the menu remain functional

items.title: Custom titlebar minimize / maximize / close buttons work
steps: Click #btn-minimize — window minimizes to taskbar
Restore from taskbar, click #btn-maximize — window maximizes
Click #btn-maximize again — window restores to previous bounds
Click #btn-close — close-to-tray behavior applies (window hides if closeToTray on)
items.expected: Each button sends its IPC ('window-minimize'/'window-maximize'/'window-close') handled in main; maximize toggles; close routes through the same close handler as the OS X button. Button tooltips/labels use window.minimize / window.maximize / window.close locale strings

automationHints.notes: Verify minimized/maximized state via app_info or BrowserWindow state rather than screenshots alone.

items.title: Logo dropdown menu offers window controls and dismisses on Escape / outside click
steps: Click the sidebar logo (#sidebar-logo-icon) — the #logo-menu dropdown opens
Press Escape — menu closes
Reopen, click anywhere outside the menu — it closes
Reopen and click the minimize item (#logo-menu-min)
Restore, reopen the menu and click #logo-menu-max, then #logo-menu-close
items.expected: Menu toggles on logo click; Escape and outside-mousedown (capture phase) both dismiss it; logo-menu-min/max/close send the same window-* IPC as the titlebar buttons and the menu hides itself after each action

automationHints.notes: Escape needs computer_key since it's a document-level keydown.

items.title: Account icon toggles the account menu; menu closes on outside click
steps: Click #account-icon-btn
Verify the dropdown content
Click anywhere else in the app (e.g. the projects list)
Re-open and click the email label
items.expected: Menu (#account-menu) opens showing the account email (#account-email, non-empty) on top, then Account (#account-btn-account), Dashboard (#account-btn-dashboard), Redeem License Key (#account-btn-redeem), and Sign out (#account-sign-out) below a separator. Outside click closes it (document 'click' listener — synthetic clicks work). Clicking the email label does NOT close the menu (stopPropagation).

automationHints.notes: Unlike the logo menu (mousedown), this menu closes on document 'click', so ui_click outside works.

items.title: Account and Dashboard menu items open the right external URLs
steps: Open the account menu via #account-icon-btn
Click 'Account' (#account-btn-account) — observe system browser; close it
Re-open the menu and click 'Dashboard' (#account-btn-dashboard)
items.expected: 'Account' opens https://patapim.ai/account, 'Dashboard' opens https://patapim.ai/go, each in the system browser; the menu closes after each click.

items.title: Instance (device) name: inline edit commits on Enter, reverts on Escape, persists and heartbeats
steps: Click the instance-name tag (#instance-name) in the header
Verify an inline input (.instance-name-input, placeholder 'Name this device', maxlength 50) replaces it focused/selected
Type 'E2E-RIG' and press Enter
Click the tag again, type junk, press Escape
Restart the app and re-check the tag
items.expected: Enter commits: tag shows 'E2E-RIG', AUTH_SET_INSTANCE_NAME saves it to account.json and an immediate heartbeat sends deviceName='E2E-RIG'. Escape reverts to the prior value. The name survives an app restart. Clearing the name shows the placeholder (app.instance.namePlaceholder) styled as placeholder.

automationHints.notes: Escape may be intercepted by the emergency-stop layer; blur also commits, so test Escape-revert with a human keyboard if needed. Restore the original device name (e.g. 'CASA') as cleanup.

items.title: Force-update overlay full lifecycle: required screen, Update Now download, restart state, and retry on failure
preconditions: Dev instance pointed at a controlled patapim-version.json gist (or the gist temporarily sets forceUpdate:true with a version newer than the installed one)
App version older than the gist's version so FORCE_UPDATE_REQUIRED fires
steps: Launch the app and wait for the update check (~startup) so the main process sends FORCE_UPDATE_REQUIRED
Verify the blocking overlay #force-update-overlay becomes visible and cannot be dismissed by Escape or clicking outside
Verify the title text 'Update required' (forceUpdate.required) and #force-update-version shows 'Version X.Y.Z is required' (forceUpdate.versionRequired) with the gist version interpolated
Verify release notes render in #force-update-notes when the gist provides notesMd
Verify #force-update-btn reads 'Update now' (forceUpdate.updateNow); click it
Verify the button flips to the disabled 'Updating…' state (forceUpdate.updating) and #force-update-status first shows 'Starting download…' (forceUpdate.starting) while #force-update-bar progresses
When the download completes, verify the button becomes the enabled restart variant labeled per forceUpdate.restart (class 'force-update-btn restart-btn')
Failure path: repeat with the network blocked — verify #force-update-error shows forceUpdate.failed and the button becomes 'Retry' (forceUpdate.retry, class 'retry-btn')
Click Retry and verify the status shows forceUpdate.retrying and the download restarts
Cleanup: restore the gist/flag and restart the app without the forced flag
items.expected: Overlay blocks the whole UI until updated; every state string (required, versionRequired, updateNow, starting, updating, restart, retry, retrying, failed) renders localized; restart applies the update

items.title: License badge renders 'pro' state for a Pro account
preconditions: Signed in as a Pro/Lifetime account with status active or trialing
steps: Launch PATAPIM and look at the sidebar header next to the logo text
Inspect the #license-badge element
items.expected: Badge is visible, textContent is 'pro' (account.pro), className is 'license-badge pro', title tooltip is 'Pro plan active' (account.proPlan). #btn-referral-header has the 'pro' class; #menu-upgrade in the title bar is display:none.

automationHints.notes: Static index.html markup defaults to 'license-badge free'/'free'; JS updates it at runtime via LICENSE_GET_STATUS — verify the runtime state, not the static HTML.

items.title: Free-tier badge click opens the upgrade page in the system browser
preconditions: Signed in as (or switched to) a FREE-tier account, or signed out so the badge shows 'free'
steps: Verify #license-badge reads 'free' with title 'Upgrade to Pro' (account.upgradeToPro)
Click the badge
Observe the system browser
items.expected: shell.openExternal opens https://patapim.ai/upgrade (with ?email=<account email> appended when #account-email has text). Clicking a 'pro' badge does nothing (handler is gated on the 'free' class).

items.title: Redeem section toggles via 'Have a license key?' link
steps: Click 'Have a license key?' (#upgrade-redeem-link) to collapse the redeem section
Click it again to expand
items.expected: #upgrade-redeem-section toggles display none/visible; on expand the key input (#upgrade-redeem-input, placeholder 'Enter your license key') receives focus.

items.title: Redeem with empty key is a silent no-op; input accepts text
preconditions: Upgrade modal open with redeem section expanded
steps: Click 'Redeem' (#upgrade-redeem-btn) with the input empty
Fill #upgrade-redeem-input with 'INVALID-TEST-KEY-DO-NOT-REDEEM' and confirm the value
Clear the input (cleanup); do NOT click Redeem with the test key unless running item 022
items.expected: Empty click: no IPC call, button still reads 'Redeem' (never 'Verifying...'), no error/success message (guard `if (!key) return`). Input accepts and retains typed text.

items.title: Redeeming an invalid key shows the error state and restores the button (click and Enter paths)
preconditions: Upgrade modal open with redeem section expanded
Network available (hits the real license:redeem-key backend)
steps: Fill #upgrade-redeem-input with an obviously invalid key (e.g. 'INVALID-TEST-KEY-0000')
Click 'Redeem' and observe the button label while pending
After the response, clear the input, re-enter the invalid key and press Enter in the input instead
items.expected: Button disables and reads 'Verifying...' while pending; on failure #upgrade-redeem-error shows the server error or 'Invalid license key'; button re-enables and reads 'Redeem'. Enter in the input triggers the same flow (keydown handler). On exception/network failure the error reads 'Something went wrong. Try again.'.

automationHints.notes: Makes a real backend call with a bogus key — harmless but avoid hammering (rate limits). Clear the input afterward.

items.title: Redeeming a valid license key activates the plan and closes the modal
preconditions: A valid, unused license key available
Account currently on a lower tier than the key grants
steps: Open the upgrade modal redeem section
Enter the valid key and click 'Redeem'
Observe success message and badge
CLEANUP: coordinate with backend/admin to release or note the consumed key
items.expected: #upgrade-redeem-success shows 'License activated! Plan: <plan>'; modal auto-closes after ~2s; license badge flips to 'pro'; license.json/account reflect the new plan; upgrade CTAs (#menu-upgrade) hide.

items.title: Local update: blue button appears from marker file and applies the staged build
preconditions: Run scripts/local-update-pc.bat to stage a build and write ~/.patapim/local-update-ready.json
steps: After staging, watch the tab bar (fs.watch picks the marker up without restart)
Verify the button styling and label
Click the button
After relaunch, verify the new build is running and the button is gone
items.expected: Button shows class 'local-update' (blue 'update-breathe-local' breathing variant), restart icon, label 'Local Update v{version}', tooltip 'Install local build and restart'. Click sends APPLY_LOCAL_UPDATE: sessions saved, applying marker written, WMI-spawned bat waits for exit, copies (xcopy, up to 3 retries) or launches in installAlreadyUpdated mode, deletes the ready marker BEFORE launching (no phantom button on restart), and PATAPIM relaunches. A regular UPDATE_AVAILABLE arriving while local-ready is pending must NOT override the blue button.

automationHints.notes: Restarts the app — run on the dev instance and schedule a follow-up check. Apply log: ~/.patapim/local-update-apply-<ts>.log.

items.title: Interrupted local apply is detected at startup and surfaces a retry error
steps: With the app stopped, write ~/.patapim/local-update-applying.json with {"startedAt": <now minus 5 minutes in ms>, "version":"9.9.9", "mode":"fullCopy"} and a dummy local-update-ready.json
Start the dev instance
Wait ~6-10s after the window loads
items.expected: init() sees the applying marker older than 60s: clears BOTH markers (so the broken Apply button can't be re-clicked, which previously eroded sessions.json) and ~6s later sends UPDATE_PROGRESS stage:error — update button shows the error state with 'Local update was interrupted before completing. Re-run scripts/local-update-pc.bat to retry.' A marker younger than 60s is left alone (apply may be in flight).

automationHints.notes: Clean up any leftover marker files afterward. Restart via rebuild-dev-safe.bat only.

items.title: Previous apply failure marker surfaces an error on next launch and is consumed
steps: With the app stopped, write ~/.patapim/local-update-failed.json with {"failedAt":"xcopy"}
Start the dev instance
Wait ~6-10s
Check the marker file afterward
items.expected: Update button enters the error state with 'Local update failed at: xcopy. See /tmp/patapim-local-update.log' (message references the unix log path even on Windows — minor copy bug worth flagging); local-update-failed.json is deleted after surfacing so the error shows only once.

automationHints.notes: On Windows the real apply log is ~/.patapim/local-update-apply-<ts>.log; the error string hardcodes the macOS path.

items.title: Stale local-update marker (missing stagedPath) is cleaned up without showing the button
steps: Write ~/.patapim/local-update-ready.json with {"version":"9.9.9","stagedPath":"C:/nonexistent/staged"}
Wait >3s for the watcher/poll
Verify the tab bar and the marker file
items.expected: checkForLocalUpdate detects the missing stagedPath, deletes the marker, and never sends LOCAL_UPDATE_READY — no blue button appears. (Startup path additionally validates installAlreadyUpdated mode against <targetPath>/PATAPIM.exe.)

automationHints.notes: Use Bash to write/inspect the marker; confirm the file was unlinked afterward. ~/.patapim is shared between stable and dev instances — both watch it, so run when only one instance is up.

items.title: Account persists across restart and a stale license cache is reconciled at boot
preconditions: Dev instance signed in as Pro
steps: Quit the dev instance
Edit ~/.patapim/license.json to set plan to 'free' (simulating a desynced cache); keep account.json intact
Start the dev instance (./rebuild-dev-safe.bat)
Inspect the header and badge after load
items.expected: App restores the signed-in state from ~/.patapim/account.json without re-auth (email shown, heartbeat starts). reconcileLicenseWithAccount() repairs license.json at boot before the UI reads the plan: badge shows 'pro' again, not the stale 'free'.

automationHints.notes: ~/.patapim is shared between instances — back up license.json first and restore it after. Use rebuild-dev-safe.bat, never rebuild-dev.bat from the Bash tool.

items.title: Referral link copy button copies to clipboard; invalid invite email shows error toast

Verify the toast
Fill #referral-invite-input with 'notanemail' and click 'Invite'
Clear the input (cleanup)

items.title: Referral panel toggles via the gift-icon header button and closes via X
steps: Click #btn-referral-header (gift icon, tooltip 'Refer friends & get Pro free')
Screenshot the right-side panel
Click #btn-referral-header again to toggle closed
Re-open and close via #referral-close ('✕')
items.expected: Panel (#referral-panel) gains/loses the 'visible' class. For an account with referral data it shows: 'Refer & Unlock Pro' header, progress '{n} of 10 activated' / '{m} invited' + bar, 'Your referral link' (https://patapim.ai/r/<code>) with copy button, Friend's email input + Invite button, and the Referrals list with Activated/Pending statuses. Both the icon toggle and the X close work.

items.title: Referral progress state renders link, invite form, and referral list
preconditions: Signed in with referral data (email set in license manager)
steps: Open the referral panel
Verify the progress-state elements
Fill #referral-invite-input with text and clear it (do NOT click Invite)
items.expected: Progress label '{activated} of 10 activated' and '{count} invited'; .referral-link-copy button next to the URL (disabled with 'Loading...' until the code loads); #referral-invite-input (placeholder "Friend's email") accepts text; #referral-invite-btn ('Invite') present; list shows entries with 'Activated'/'Pending' status pills, or 'No referrals yet. Invite friends above!' when empty.

items.title: Inviting a valid friend email sends a real invitation

items.title: Referral intro state collects email and validates it

steps: Open the referral panel

Verify the intro renders: trophy icon, 'Get Pro Forever — Free', 'Refer 10 friends who install & register PATAPIM.', 4 benefit checks (Unlimited terminals & projects / Unlimited dictation / Remote access via URL / Tasks, GitHub & Plugins)

items.title: Referral reward-unlocked state shows license key with copy

steps: Open the referral panel

items.title: Release-notes 'details' popover opens, renders parsed notes, and dismisses

Inspect the popover

Clicking 'details' does NOT trigger the update (stopPropagation). All three dismiss paths work (attachOutsideDismiss handles outside mousedown, Escape, and window blur); re-clicking 'details' toggles.

items.title: Logo icon click opens the logo menu with all expected items

Re-clicking the logo icon toggles the menu closed.

items.title: Logo menu dismisses on Escape key

items.title: Sign-in flow: link opens browser pairing page, polling completes, header flips to signed-in

Click sends APPLY_UPDATE again with 'Retrying...'. A subsequent 'up-to-date' progress resets to idle and hides the button.
