automationHints.notes: Do not click the toggle in this item — covered by integrations-010

items.title: Toggle Workspace Sync off and back on
steps: In Preferences → Workspace Sync, click the toggle (#workspace-sync-btn) to disable
Verify the toggle and subtext flip to the disabled state
CLEANUP: click the toggle again to re-enable and verify 'Synced with <email>' returns
items.expected: IPC workspaces-sync:set-enabled round-trips: state flips off then on without errors; no project-list corruption; re-enable re-uploads the workspace list

automationHints.notes: Re-emit of seed skip-execute case (external cloud upload). Local paths/pinned browsers are never uploaded per UI copy

items.title: Routing-moved toast appears when another PATAPIM instance takes Telegram routing
steps: With the stable instance actively routing Telegram, start/claim routing from the second (dev) instance
Observe the first instance
items.expected: The instance that lost routing shows a toast: 'Telegram routing moved to another PATAPIM instance.' (#tg-active-lost-toast-text); messages from the phone now land in the claiming instance

items.title: Verify Telegram → terminal message routing precedence (reply, route toggle, active)
preconditions: Phone with the linked Telegram chat
steps: From the phone, REPLY to a previous bot notification that came from terminal A — verify the text is injected into terminal A prefixed with '[tg] '
Enable the Telegram route toggle for terminal B (avioncito), send a free-form (non-reply) message — verify it lands in terminal B
Disable the route toggle, focus terminal A in the UI, send another free-form message — verify it lands in the active terminal A
Close ALL terminals and send a message — verify the bot replies 'No active terminal…' guidance instead of injecting
items.expected: Routing precedence: explicit reply > route-toggle > active terminal; injected text carries the '[tg] ' prefix so Claude routes its answer back via notify-self; with no terminal, a helpful bounce message is sent

items.title: Toggle the Telegram route footer and verify the tab badge
preconditions: Telegram route footer not hidden via View → Toolbar Buttons
steps: Locate the telegram route footer (#telegram-route-footer with #telegram-route-checkbox) on the terminal area
Check the checkbox for the active terminal
Verify the terminal's tab shows the paper-plane badge (.tab-tg-icon with title 'Route Telegram messages here')
Uncheck it and verify the badge disappears
Right-click the footer and verify the hide/show context menu for footers works
items.expected: Checkbox state syncs with terminal.telegramRouted; tab badge renders only while routed; hidden-footer preference persists

automationHints.notes: Right-click on the footer needs computer_*; routing behavior itself is integrations-042

items.title: Send a Telegram voice note — echo is exactly '🎤 <transcript>' with 👀 reaction
preconditions: Telegram paired in OFFICIAL mode (custom mode has no inline audio bytes)
steps: From the phone, send a short voice note to the bot
Wait for transcription (local Parakeet first, Whisper relay fallback)
Inspect the bot's echo message in the chat
Verify the transcript text is injected into the active terminal
items.expected: Echo is '🎤 ' + transcript ONLY — no engine tag, no duration/metadata (per voice-echo style rule); a 👀 reaction is attached to the echo message (fallback: to the voice message if the echo failed); transcription failure replies '🎤 Could not transcribe — try again or type it.'; in custom mode the bot bounces with a hint to switch modes

items.title: Run the full Telegram pairing wizard via 'Connect to Telegram' and reach success step
preconditions: Telegram NOT paired (unlink first if needed)
steps: In Preferences → Telegram, click 'Connect to Telegram' (#tg-connect)
Wizard welcome step appears — click Continue (#tgw-welcome-continue)
Wait through connecting step; embedded Telegram Web step appears (modal grows to .tgw-modal--wide)
If logged out of Telegram Web, scan its QR with the phone; PATAPIM then auto-routes to the bot chat with the pairing code
Tap START in the bot chat on the phone
Verify wizard advances to success step; click Done (#tgw-success-done)
CLEANUP: if this run was only for testing, click Unlink (#tg-unlink) to restore the prior unpaired state, or leave paired if that was the original state
items.expected: Pairing completes: success step shows 'Connected!' title, 'Linked to <chat hint>' line and 3 usage tips; prefs view flips to paired (#tg-chat-hint populated, Unlink visible); #tg-status-text becomes 'Connected · paired'

items.title: Advance to the embedded Telegram Web step — modal grows, status + countdown render
steps: Open the wizard and click Continue
Observe the connecting step (spinner + label)
Wait for the embedded step: modal grows to the wide variant (.tgw-modal--wide), #tgw-tgweb-host shows Telegram Web (or its QR login) overlaid by the native view
Verify the status line (#tgw-embedded-status) shows one of: 'Checking…' / 'Scan the QR' / 'Opening bot' / 'At bot' as state advances
Verify the countdown (#tgw-countdown) ticks down in m:ss form ('expires in …')
Click Cancel (#tgw-embedded-cancel) to abort without pairing
items.expected: TELEGRAM_WEB_OPEN and TELEGRAM_PAIR_START fire in parallel; native view bounds track #tgw-tgweb-host (including during the 200ms grow transition); cancel hides the native view, shrinks the modal and cancels the pairing

automationHints.notes: Stop before tapping START on the phone — pairing completion is integrations-003. The embedded view is native; use computer_screenshot to see its content

items.title: Verify wizard welcome step content and all cancel paths
preconditions: Telegram NOT paired (the Connect button only shows in the unpaired view)
steps: Click 'Connect to Telegram' (#tg-connect) to open the wizard
Verify the welcome step: Telegram brand header, hero icon, title, subtitle, numbered 3-step list, trust line, Continue and Cancel buttons
Press Escape — wizard closes, show() resolves false
Reopen; click the × (.tgw-close) — closes
Reopen; click the dimmed backdrop outside the modal — closes
Reopen; click Cancel (#tgw-welcome-cancel) — closes
items.expected: All four dismissal paths remove #tg-wizard-overlay; closing mid-flow invokes TELEGRAM_PAIR_CANCEL so no orphan pairing remains; #tg-connect re-enables after the promise resolves

automationHints.notes: Cancelling at the welcome step never contacts the relay — safe. Locale keys: tgWizard.welcome.*

items.title: Let the pairing code expire — expired box appears and 'Generate new code' restarts
preconditions: Wizard at the embedded step with an active countdown
steps: Reach the embedded step and do NOT complete pairing
Wait for the countdown to reach 0 (~5 minutes; it turns amber/urgent under 30s)
Verify: countdown shows 'expired', the Telegram Web host hides, the expired box (#tgw-expired-box) appears with a 'Generate new code' button, the modal shrinks back from wide
Click 'Generate new code' (#tgw-regenerate)
Verify a fresh pairing starts (connecting → embedded with a new countdown)
items.expected: Expiry tears down the embedded view cleanly and offers regeneration; the retry path resets loginRouted/code state and produces a working new code

automationHints.notes: ~5 min wall-clock wait — schedule a Monitor/poll rather than blocking. Urgent styling kicks in at ≤30s remaining

items.title: Wizard error step renders mapped message when the relay is unreachable
preconditions: Network to the relay blocked (offline or firewall rule)
steps: Open the wizard and click Continue
Wait for the pairing attempt to fail

Verify the error step: red icon, friendly mapped message ('relay unreachable' family for relay timeout/disconnect errors), 'Show details' expander with the raw technical error, 'Try again' and 'Cancel' buttons
Restore network; click 'Try again' and verify pairing restarts cleanly
items.expected: mapErrorMessage routes relay-connection failures to tgWizard.error.relayUnreachable (generic otherwise); details collapse holds the raw error; Try again re-invokes startPair

items.title: Right-click the WhatsApp toolbar button → 'WhatsApp Bot' focuses the bot terminal
preconditions: WhatsApp feature enabled, toolbar button visible
steps: Right-click .btn-whatsapp-toggle
Verify a context menu appears with 'WhatsApp Bot' and 'Export chat' items separated by a divider (NOT the generic Hide/Show All button menu)
Click 'WhatsApp Bot'
Verify a bot terminal is focused/created (WHATSAPP_BOT_FOCUS returns a terminalId and that tab becomes active)
items.expected: Custom context menu replaces the standard customize menu for this button; selecting WhatsApp Bot activates the dedicated bot terminal tab

automationHints.notes: Right-click needs computer_* (CSP blocks app_evaluate); button is intentionally skipped from the generic Hide/Show context menu wiring

items.title: Open the WhatsApp Export chat dialog and cancel via all three paths
steps: Right-click .btn-whatsapp-toggle → click 'Export chat'
Verify the export dialog appears: title, prompt, contact-name input (#wa-export-input, focused), Cancel and Export buttons
Press Escape — dialog closes
Reopen; click outside the dialog (on the dim overlay) — dialog closes
Reopen; click Cancel (#wa-export-cancel) — dialog closes
Reopen; click Export with an EMPTY input — nothing happens (guard on empty name)
items.expected: All dismissal paths remove the overlay without starting an export; empty-name Export is a no-op

items.title: Run a real WhatsApp chat export for a named contact
preconditions: A test contact/chat with a few messages exists
steps: Open the Export chat dialog (right-click .btn-whatsapp-toggle → Export chat)
Type the test contact's name into #wa-export-input and press Enter (or click Export)
Verify a terminal is focused running the export (WHATSAPP_CHAT_EXPORT returns terminalId)
Wait for completion and locate the exported file
CLEANUP: delete the exported file
items.expected: Export starts in a dedicated terminal, scrapes the chat and writes an export artifact; the named contact's messages appear in the output; errors surface in the terminal rather than failing silently

automationHints.notes: Enter key in the input triggers export; Escape cancels

items.title: Send a message to the self-chat ('Message yourself') and verify no echo loop
preconditions: Self-chat ('Message yourself' / own number) exists in the chat list
steps: Call whatsapp_send_to targeting the user's own number / 'Message yourself' chat with text 'patapim e2e self-chat test'
Verify the tool reports success
Call whatsapp_get_messages on the self-chat — the sent message appears with fromMe
Wait one bot poll cycle and verify the bot does NOT re-ingest its own sent message (sent-message fingerprinting must suppress it — critical for self-chats where ALL messages are fromMe)
CLEANUP: optionally delete the test message from the phone
items.expected: Message sends via compose box + send button; trackSent/addFingerprint prevents the scraper from treating the sent text as an incoming command; no duplicate injection or notification loop

automationHints.notes: whatsappBot.js fingerprints sent messages specifically for the self-chat case (line ~716) — this is the regression to watch

items.title: Exercise WhatsApp MCP read tools: status, chat list, open chat, read messages
steps: Call whatsapp_status — expect connected/ready
Call whatsapp_get_chats — expect a non-empty chat list with names and unread info
Call whatsapp_open_chat for a known chat name
Call whatsapp_get_messages — expect recent messages of that chat with sender/fromMe flags
items.expected: All four tools succeed against the live WebContentsView (DOM scraping); chat names match what the panel shows; no message sent

automationHints.notes: whatsapp_search_messages can be added as a bonus check

items.title: Connect WhatsApp Web from scratch — QR scan to ready state
preconditions: WhatsApp disconnected and persist partition logged out (worst-case fresh pairing)
steps: Open the WhatsApp panel; in the disconnected view verify the green WhatsApp icon, 'WhatsApp Web' label and 'Open WhatsApp Web' button (#wa-connect-btn)
Click 'Open WhatsApp Web'
WhatsApp Web loads in the native view and shows its QR code
Scan the QR with the phone (Linked devices)
Wait for chats to load
items.expected: State transitions disconnected → loading → ready; status dot (#whatsapp-status-dot) goes green with title 'Connected'; chat list renders inside the panel area; prefs card status shows 'Connected'

items.title: Toggle the WhatsApp side panel via the toolbar button
steps: Left-click the WhatsApp toolbar button (.btn-whatsapp-toggle)
Verify #whatsapp-panel gains class 'visible' and content renders
Left-click the button again
Verify the panel hides (class 'visible' removed) and the native WebContentsView is hidden (no stray overlay left on screen)
items.expected: Panel opens (auto-connecting if disconnected) and closes cleanly; whatsapp:hide IPC fires on close so the native overlay disappears with the panel

automationHints.notes: WebContentsView renders above all DOM — verify via screenshot that nothing remains after hide

items.title: Detach WhatsApp to a floating window and reattach to the panel
steps: Click the detach button (#wa-tb-detach, title 'Open in separate window')
Verify the in-app panel closes and a separate floating WhatsApp window appears
Click the toolbar WhatsApp button again (show() while floating triggers reattach)
Verify the floating window closes and WhatsApp snaps back into the side panel; detach button title flips to 'Attach back to panel' while floating
items.expected: WHATSAPP_DETACH toggles detached state; WHATSAPP_DETACHED IPC keeps the renderer in sync (panel hidden while floating, bounds re-sent on reattach); icon and tooltip swap between detach/attach variants

automationHints.notes: The floating window is a separate native window — use computer_screenshot to verify it exists

items.title: Drag the panel resize handle — native view hides during drag and re-bounds after
steps: Press and drag the side panel's left resize handle to make the panel wider
While dragging, observe the WhatsApp WebContentsView disappears (panel-resize-start → WHATSAPP_HIDE)
Release the drag
Verify the WebContentsView reappears exactly fitted to the new panel size (6px left margin for the handle)
items.expected: No mouse-event blocking during drag; after release the native overlay realigns with #wa-browserview-area at the new size; resizing the app window also re-syncs bounds (window resize listener)

automationHints.notes: Misaligned overlay after resize = sendBounds() regression; also exercise a layout-preset switch (patapim:layout-changed) if available

items.title: Verify keychain explain modal appears before first WhatsApp connect
preconditions: Keychain explanation not yet acknowledged (fresh profile or reset flag)
steps: Open the WhatsApp panel while disconnected (toolbar button or 'Open WhatsApp Web' #wa-connect-btn)
Verify the keychain explain modal shows BEFORE the WebContentsView attaches (it must not be covered by the native view)
Acknowledge with 'Got it'
Verify the connect flow then proceeds and the panel renders the connected/loading view
items.expected: Modal explains keychain usage (item text mentions WhatsApp session); the native WebContentsView is NOT attached while the modal is pending (no softlock); after acknowledgement the connect continues and renderView() catches up

automationHints.notes: Code explicitly defers renderConnected() while isKeychainExplainPending() — regression here softlocks the modal under the native overlay

items.title: Use the WhatsApp panel toolbar Refresh button to reload the session
steps: Click the refresh button (#wa-tb-refresh) in the panel toolbar
Observe state transitions and final render
items.expected: Refresh performs disconnect + reconnect (that is its implementation); WhatsApp Web reloads and returns to ready without requiring QR re-scan; no duplicate event listeners or stuck overlays after the cycle

automationHints.notes: Also note: the back button (#wa-tb-back) is a wired no-op placeholder — verify clicking it does nothing harmful

items.title: Verify WhatsApp status dot reflects connection state
steps: With panel connected, hover/inspect #whatsapp-status-dot — class 'connected', title 'Connected'
Disconnect via panel toolbar (#wa-tb-disconnect) — dot class 'disconnected', title 'Disconnected'
Reconnect — during load the dot shows class 'connecting' with title 'Loading…', then back to connected
items.expected: Dot class and localized title track the three states (ready/disconnected/loading) pushed via WHATSAPP_STATE IPC

automationHints.notes: Involves a real disconnect/reconnect cycle — session cookies persist so no QR re-scan expected

items.title: Verify WhatsApp session persists across app restart (no QR re-scan)
steps: Quit and relaunch PATAPIM
Open the WhatsApp panel
Wait for load
items.expected: Panel reconnects to the existing WhatsApp Web session (persist partition keeps cookies) and reaches ready with chats visible — no QR login screen

automationHints.notes: Restart the dev instance via rebuild-dev-safe.bat, then app_wait_ready before checking
